  Positive statement → negative tag (auxiliary + n't + pronoun?)
  Negative statement → positive tag (auxiliary + pronoun?)
• Special 'I am' tags:
  - 'I am right' (positive) → 'aren't I?' ← EXCEPTION! (not 'amn't I')
  - 'I am not right' (negative) → 'am I?' ← Regular positive tag
• Why 'am I?' not 'are I?' — 'am' is the auxiliary in the statement, use same.
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: 'I am' question tags — PSC-ൽ special exception ആയി frequently tested.
• Full tag pattern:
  - I am → aren't I? (positive statement exception)
  - I am not → am I? (negative statement, regular)
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. I am not late, am I?
  2. I am responsible, aren't I?
  3. She isn't coming, is she?
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

(A) are I?
• 'Are I' = grammatically impossible — 'are' never goes with 'I' as subject.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Negative statement → positive tag, students think 'are' (common auxiliary) — but 'I' takes 'am'.

(C) aren't I?
• 'Aren't I?' = tag for POSITIVE 'I am' statements — 'I am right, aren't I?'
• Here statement is NEGATIVE ('am not') → positive tag needed → 'am I?' not 'aren't I?'
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Aren't I?' is the special exception tag students remember — but it's for positive 'I am', not negative.

(D) is it?
• 'Is it?' = used when subject is a thing/situation, not 'I'.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Generic tag — some students use 'is it?' for anything.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'AM NOT → AM I? / AM → AREN'T I?' — flip the not, keep the am!
  Negative (am not) → positive (am I?)
  Positive (am) → negative exception (aren't I?)
• PSC exam: 'I am not...' question → find 'am I?' in options → guaranteed correct.
• The only TWO 'I am' tags to remember: 'I am → aren't I?' and 'I am not → am I?' — that's it!

questionText:
Pick the correct question tag.

Please pass me the sugar, ____________ ?

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (D) will you?

• 'Please pass me the sugar' — ഇത് ഒരു polite request ആണ്, imperative sentence! ആരോടെങ്കിലും ഒരു കാര്യം ചെയ്യണം എന്ന് അഭ്യർഥിക്കുമ്പോൾ 'Please' + verb ഉപയോഗിക്കും.
• 'Please' ഉള്ള ഈ imperative sentence-ന്റെ question tag = 'will you?' — ഈ ഒരൊറ്റ rule ഓർത്തിരുന്നാൽ ഈ ചോദ്യം guaranteed mark!
• 'Please help me, will you?' / 'Please sit down, will you?' — polite request-കൾക്ക് ശേഷം tag = will you? എന്നു മാത്രം.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula:
  Please/Do + V (positive imperative) → will you?
  Don't + V (negative imperative) → will you?
  Let's / Let us → shall we?
• Imperative sentence-ൽ subject 'you' implied ആണ് — അതുകൊണ്ടാണ് tag-ൽ 'you' വരുന്നത്.
• 'Won't you?' = invitation-ന്റെ tag ('Have some tea, won't you?') — request-ന് 'will you?' ആണ് standard.
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Imperative tags PSC-ൽ ഇടയ്ക്ക് വരും. 'Let's → shall we' ആയും 'Please → will you' ആയും ഒരുമിച്ച് ഓർക്കുക — ഇവ രണ്ടും PSC favourites.
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. Please help me, will you?
  2. Open the window, will you?
  3. Let's start studying, shall we?
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

(A) won't you?
• 'Won't you?' = invitation-ന്റെ tag — 'Please have a seat, won't you?' style.
• ഇവിടെ ഒരു sugar pass ചെയ്യാൻ request ചെയ്യുകയാണ്, invite ചെയ്യുകയല്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Won't you?' imperative-ന് ശേഷം technically possible — ഈ knowledge ഉള്ള students-ഐ confuse ചെയ്യാൻ PSC ഇത് ഇട്ടു. Standard PSC answer = will you?

(B) can you?
• 'Can you?' = ability ചോദിക്കുന്ന modal — 'Can you swim?' style.
• Imperative tag ആയി 'can' use ചെയ്യില്ല — ability ഉണ്ടോ എന്ന് ചോദിക്കുകയല്ല, ഒരു കാര്യം ചെയ്യണം എന്ന് request ചെയ്യുകയാണ്.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Can you pass the sugar?' = natural way to request — അതിനാൽ 'can you?' feel ആകും. പക്ഷേ tag rule = will you?

(C) can't you?
• 'Can't you?' = frustrated/irritated tone — 'Can't you just be quiet?' style.
• 'Please' ഉപയോഗിച്ച് polite ആയി request ചെയ്തിട്ട് irritated tag ഇടില്ല — tone match ആകില്ല!
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Negative tag + imperative = feel ആകും. പക്ഷേ 'can't' = ability-related, imperative tag = will you?
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'PLEASE + VERB = WILL YOU? — Please-ഉം Will-ഉം രണ്ടും polite! ഒരുമിച്ചേ വരൂ!'
• Imperative tag family ഒരുമിച്ച് ഓർക്കൂ:
  Please/Do + V → will you? | Don't + V → will you? | Let's → shall we?
• PSC exam strategy: Question-ൽ 'Please' കണ്ടാൽ ഒരു second ആലോചിക്കേണ്ട — answer = will you? ഒരിക്കലും miss ആകില്ല!

questionText: Choose the correct sentence from the following:
optionA: We need the water and the air to live
optionB: We need the water and air to live
optionC: We need water and air to live
optionD: We need water and the air to live

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (C) We need water and air to live

• 'Water', 'air' — ഇവ uncountable nouns ആണ്, general sense-ൽ use ചെയ്തിരിക്കുന്നു. ജീവിക്കാൻ വെള്ളവും വായുവും വേണം — ഇത് ഒരു universal truth!
• Universal truth / general sense-ൽ uncountable noun ഉപയോഗിക്കുമ്പോൾ article ഒന്നും വേണ്ട — zero article rule.
• 'The water' = specific water (ഈ കുപ്പിയിലെ വെള്ളം). 'Water' = water in general (എല്ലാ വെള്ളവും). ഇവിടെ general ആണ് — so no article!
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: General/universal uncountable noun → NO article (zero article)
  Specific uncountable noun → THE
• Article usage:
  - 'Water is essential' (zero article = general truth) ✓
  - 'The water in this bottle is cold' (the = specific) ✓
  - 'A water' = WRONG (uncountable noun-ന് a/an ഇടില്ല) ✗
• Life essentials (water, air, food, love, peace, music) = general sense → article ഇല്ല.
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Zero article with uncountable nouns = PSC-ൽ regular ആയി വരുന്ന pattern. 'the' ഇടണോ വേണ്ടയോ എന്ന question frequently test ചെയ്യും.
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. We need water and air to live.
  2. Love is more powerful than hatred.
  3. The water in this glass is clean. (specific = the)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

(A) We need the water and the air to live
• 'The water', 'the air' = specific water/air — 'ഏതോ ഒരു specific water' ആണ് ജീവിക്കാൻ വേണ്ടത് എന്ന് ആകും! General truth-ൽ 'the' ഇടില്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'The' ഇടുന്നത് sentence-നെ formal/complete ആക്കും എന്ന് students തോന്നും. പക്ഷേ general sense = zero article.

(B) We need the water and air to live
• 'The water' (specific) + 'air' (general) = inconsistent! ഒരേ sentence-ൽ ഒന്നിന് 'the' ഇടുകയും മറ്റേതിന് ഇടാതിരിക്കുകയും ചെയ്യില്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Partially correct — ഒന്നിൽ article തെറ്റ്, ഒന്നിൽ ശരി. Students ഒന്ന് ശരി ആണ് എന്ന് കണ്ട് select ചെയ്തേക്കാം.

(D) We need water and the air to live
• 'Air' (general) + 'the air' (specific) — same problem as B, ഇപ്പോൾ 'air'-ൽ ആണ് 'the' വന്നിരിക്കുന്നത്.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: B-യും D-യും ഒരേ error-ന്റെ mirror versions — partially wrong, confusion ഉണ്ടാക്കാൻ.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'General = Zero! Universal truth-ൽ article zero — ആകാശം, വെള്ളം, സ്നേഹം — ഇവ specific ആർക്കും കൊടുത്തിട്ടില്ല, general ആണ്!'
• Quick rule: 'Water is life' (zero) / 'The water is cold' (specific water = the). Context നോക്കൂ.
• PSC strategy: 'Water', 'air', 'love', 'peace', 'knowledge' general sense-ൽ ആണോ? → article ഇല്ല. ഏതെങ്കിലും specific instance-ആണോ? → 'the'. ഈ ഒരൊറ്റ test!

questionText: Which of the following sentences is grammatically correct?
optionA: We kept away from the class
optionB: We kept ourselves away from the class
optionC: We kept ourself away from the class
optionD: We kept to ourself away from the class

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (B) We kept ourselves away from the class

• 'We kept _____ away from the class' — subject-ഉം object-ഉം ഒരേ person ('we') — ഇങ്ങനെ ആകുമ്പോൾ reflexive pronoun വേണം.
• 'We' (plural) → reflexive = 'ourselves'. 'Ourself' = wrong — standard English-ൽ ഈ word ഇല്ല!
• 'We kept ourselves away' = ഞങ്ങൾ ബോധപൂർവ്വം class-ൽ നിന്ന് മാറി നിന്നു — reflexive pronoun-ന്റെ perfect use.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula — Reflexive pronouns:
  I → myself | You (sg) → yourself | He → himself | She → herself | It → itself
  We → ourselves | You (pl) → yourselves | They → themselves
• 'Ourself' = PSC-ൽ ഏറ്റവും common trap — ഈ word standard English-ൽ EXIST ഇല്ല!
• Reflexive pronoun use ചെയ്യുന്നത് എപ്പോൾ:
  - Subject-ഉം Object-ഉം same person ആകുമ്പോൾ: 'She hurt herself.'
  - Emphasis-നായി: 'I did it myself.'
  - Fixed phrases: 'Help yourself', 'Enjoy yourself', 'Keep yourself'
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Reflexive pronouns — correction of sentences-ൽ PSC ഇടയ്ക്ക് test ചെയ്യും. 'Ourself' trap ഒരിക്കലും miss ആകരുത്.
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. We prepared ourselves for the PSC exam.
  2. She kept herself busy with studies.
  3. They blamed themselves for the mistake.
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

(A) We kept away from the class
• Reflexive pronoun ഇല്ല — subject (we) action ചെയ്തത് themselves-നോടാണ് (kept themselves away). Object missing ആകുന്നു.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Kept away' = short ആണ്, correct ആണ് എന്ന് feel ആകും. പക്ഷേ reflexive incomplete.

(C) We kept ourself away from the class
• 'Ourself' = standard English-ൽ ഇല്ലാത്ത ഒരു word! 'We' = plural → 'ourselves' (not 'ourself').
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Myself/yourself/himself/herself pattern നോക്കിയാൽ 'ourself' logical ആണ് എന്ന് തോന്നും. പക്ഷേ plural-ൽ -selves ആണ്.

(D) We kept to ourself away from the class
• 'To ourself' + 'away' = രണ്ട് conflicting phrases, grammatically confused. 'Keep to oneself' = private ആയിരിക്കുക (idiom), 'keep away' = ദൂരെ നിൽക്കുക — ഒരുമിച്ച് use ചെയ്യില്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Keep to oneself' idiom ഓർക്കുന്ന students ഇത് pick ചെയ്തേക്കാം.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'WE = OURSELVES, THEY = THEMSELVES — plural pronouns always -SELVES! ഒന്നിൽ കൂടുതൽ people = SELVES!'
• ഒരിക്കലും 'ourself', 'theirself' എഴുതരുത് — ഇവ real English words അല്ല!
• PSC strategy: 'Ourself' option-ൽ കണ്ടാൽ ഉടനേ eliminate ചെയ്യൂ — automatic wrong answer. 'We' → 'ourselves' only!

topic: Types of Sentences and Interchange of Sentences
questionText: Identify the simple sentence from the following:
optionA: The doctor admitted that he was helpless
optionC: The doctor admitted that he is helpless

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (B) The doctor admitted his helplessness

• Simple sentence = ഒരൊറ്റ independent clause — ഒരു subject, ഒരു verb, ഒരു complete thought.
• 'The doctor admitted his helplessness' — subject: doctor, verb: admitted, object: his helplessness. That's it! No subordinate clause, no 'that/which/who'.
• 'His helplessness' = noun phrase (object). Noun phrase ≠ clause. Clause-ൽ verb ഉണ്ടാകും — ഇവിടെ verb ഇല്ല, simple noun phrase മാത്രം!
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula:
  Simple sentence = 1 independent clause (Subject + Verb + Object/Complement)
  Complex sentence = 1 independent + 1 dependent clause (that/which/who/because/if/when)
  Compound sentence = 2 independent clauses (and/but/or)
• Simple sentence identify ചെയ്യാൻ easy test: 'that/which/who/because/if/when' ഉണ്ടോ? → ഉണ്ടെങ്കിൽ Simple അല്ല!
• 'Admitted his helplessness' = noun phrase as object (simple ✓)
• 'Admitted that he was helpless' = noun clause as object (complex ✗)
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Simple vs Complex sentence identify = PSC-ൽ regular. Subordinating conjunction/relative pronoun ഇല്ലാത്ത sentence = Simple.
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. Simple: She completed the work. (ഒരൊറ്റ clause)
  2. Complex: She completed the work that was assigned. (relative clause)
  3. Compound: She worked and she passed. (two independent clauses)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

(A) The doctor admitted that he was helpless
• 'That he was helpless' = subordinate noun clause — ഇത് Complex sentence ആണ്, Simple അല്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: A-യും B-യും same meaning — meaning ഒന്നായതിനാൽ students A-ക്കും B-ക്കും ഇടയ്ൽ confuse ആകും. Clause structure ആണ് difference.

(C) The doctor admitted that he is helpless
• A-പോലെ same — 'that he is helpless' = noun clause → Complex sentence.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Past tense 'was' vs Present tense 'is' — tense-ൽ focus ചെയ്ത് clause-type miss ചെയ്തേക്കാം.

(D) None of the above
• (B) clearly simple sentence ആണ് — 'none of the above' wrong.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Unsure ആകുന്ന students 'safe option' ആയി 'None of the above' pick ചെയ്യും.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'SIMPLE = SIMPLE! that/which/who/because ഇല്ലേ? → Simple sentence confirmed!'
• Quick shortcut: Sentence-ൽ 'that' കണ്ടാൽ — almost always Complex. 'That' = red flag for Simple sentence identification!
• PSC strategy: Options-ൽ 'that he was/is...' pattern കണ്ടാൽ eliminate ചെയ്യൂ — subordinate clause ഉള്ള sentence simple ആകില്ല!

questionText:
Which is the correct preposition to be used in the following sentence?

It is very rude to point __________ the people.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (B) at

• 'Point at someone' = ആരെയെങ്കിലും വിരൽ ചൂണ്ടി കാണിക്കുക — 'at' ഇവിടെ direct direction/target indicate ചെയ്യുന്നു.
• 'It is very rude to point at the people' — ആളുകളെ ചൂണ്ടി കാണിക്കുന്നത് ഒരു rude gesture ആണ് — ഇതറിയാം, grammatically-ഉം 'at' ആണ്!
• 'Point at' = fixed collocation (fixed preposition pair). PSC-ൽ ഇങ്ങനെ fixed pairs ഓർക്കണം.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Point AT (person/object directly towards) vs Point TO (direction/location)
• 'AT' = target/direction toward a specific person or thing:
  point at | look at | stare at | laugh at | shout at | aim at | glare at
• 'TO' = general direction or location:
  point to the map | point to the exit | walk to the station
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Fixed preposition collocations = PSC-ൽ ഏറ്റവും test ചെയ്യുന്ന area. 'At' + directed action verbs ഒരുമിച്ച് ഓർക്കുക.
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. It is rude to point at people.
  2. Don't laugh at others' mistakes.
  3. She pointed to the direction we should take.
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

(A) to
• 'Point to' = direction indicate ചെയ്യുക — 'Point to the north'. ആളുകളെ ചൂണ്ടി കാണിക്കുമ്പോൾ 'at' ആണ്, 'to' അല്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'To' = very common preposition, feel ആകും. 'Point to' exist ചെയ്യുന്നതുകൊണ്ട് confuse ആകും — meaning difference ശ്രദ്ധിക്കൂ.

(C) by
• 'Point by' = no such collocation in English.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'By' = common preposition — ചിലർ randomly pick ചെയ്തേക്കാം.

(D) for
• 'Point for' = no such collocation.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'For' = frequently tested preposition — options-ൽ ഉള്ളതുകൊണ്ട് guess ആയി pick ചെയ്തേക്കാം.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'AT = Arrow pointing AT the target! Point AT, Aim AT, Shoot AT, Laugh AT — എല്ലാം target-ഉള്ള actions!'
• Directed action + person/object = AT. General direction = TO.
• PSC strategy: Verb + person (ആരോടെങ്കിലും directed action) → almost always 'at'. Point at, laugh at, stare at, shout at — ഈ list ഓർക്കൂ!

questionText:
She is making tea.
(Change into Passive Voice)
optionC: Tea is being made by her.
optionD: Tea was being made by her.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (C) Tea is being made by her.

• Active: 'She IS MAKING tea' — Present Continuous tense (is + V-ing).
• Present Continuous Passive = is/am/are + being + V3 — 'Tea IS BEING MADE by her.'
• Object (tea) → subject ആകുന്നു. 'She' → 'by her' ആകുന്നു. Tense: present continuous passive!
• 'Being made' — 'being' = the key word! Present Continuous Passive-ൽ 'being' ഉണ്ടാകണം.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula — Tense Passive table:
  Simple Present: makes → is made
  Present Continuous: is making → is being made ← ഈ ചോദ്യം!
  Simple Past: made → was made
  Past Continuous: was making → was being made
  Present Perfect: has made → has been made
  Future: will make → will be made
• Key: Active tense = Passive tense (ഒരിക്കലും tense change ചെയ്യരുത്!)
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Active to Passive conversion = PSC-ൽ ഏറ്റവും test ചെയ്യുന്ന grammar topic. Tense table ഓർത്ത് exam hall-ൽ കൊണ്ടുപോകൂ.
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. She is writing a letter. → A letter is being written by her.
  2. They are building a house. → A house is being built by them.
  3. He is cooking dinner. → Dinner is being cooked by him.
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

(A) Tea made by her.
• Auxiliary verb ഇല്ല — 'is/was/will be' ഒന്നും ഇല്ലാതെ passive ആകില്ല. Grammatically incomplete.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Short ആയ sentence = simple feel. പക്ഷേ auxiliary missing = wrong.

(B) Tea is making by her.
• 'Is making' = active form! Passive-ൽ 'is being made' ആണ്. Active verb-ഐ directly passive position-ൽ ഇടില്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Subject + object swap ചെയ്ത് active verb-ഐ keep ചെയ്യുന്ന common mistake — students ഇങ്ങനെ ചെയ്തേക്കാം.

(D) Tea was being made by her.
• 'Was being made' = Past Continuous passive — tense wrong! Active 'is making' = Present Continuous → Passive = 'is being made' (present), 'was being made' (past) അല്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Was being made' valid passive form ആണ് — past continuous-ൻ്റെ passive. പക്ഷേ active sentence present continuous ആണ്, tense lock!
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'Present Continuous Passive = IS BEING DONE — IS + BEING = two helpers together! is making → is BEING made!'
• Tense lock rule ഓർക്കൂ: Active tense ഏതോ, Passive tense-ഉം അതേ — ഒരിക്കലും tense change ആകില്ല!
• PSC shortcut: 'is/are + V-ing' active ആണ് → passive = 'is/are + being + V3'. 'Being' insert ചെയ്ത് V3 ഇടൂ. Done!

questionText: Here is the book that you asked __________.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (D) for

• 'Here is the book that you asked FOR' — 'ask for' = ഒരു കാര്യം request ചെയ്യുക, ആവശ്യപ്പെടുക.
• 'You asked for this book' = നിങ്ങൾ ഈ book ആവശ്യപ്പെട്ടു. 'Ask for' = fixed phrasal collocation.
• 'Here is the book you asked for' = ഇതാ, നിങ്ങൾ ചോദിച്ച book! Natural English sentence.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Ask FOR = request/seek something — 'for' always with 'ask'
• 'Ask' collocations:
  - Ask FOR (something) = request ചെയ്യുക: 'Ask for help', 'Ask for the book'
  - Ask (someone) = question ചോദിക്കുക: 'Ask the teacher'
  - Ask someone TO do = ഒരു കാര്യം ചെയ്യാൻ request: 'Ask him to come'
• 'For' = seeking/wanting verbs-ൽ ഉപയോഗിക്കും:
  ask FOR | look FOR | search FOR | wait FOR | hope FOR | apply FOR | long FOR
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Fixed preposition collocations — 'ask for' PSC-ൽ test ചെയ്തിട്ടുണ്ട്. 'For' = wanting/seeking category ഓർക്കൂ.
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. She asked for permission to leave early.
  2. Here is the report you asked for.
  3. He applied for the government job.
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

(A) of
• 'Ask of' = archaic/formal ('much is asked of you') — everyday usage-ൽ 'ask of' ഇങ്ങനെ ഉപയോഗിക്കില്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Of' = common preposition, random choice ആയി pick ചെയ്തേക്കാം.

(B) off
• 'Ask off' = no standard collocation here — 'ask off' = take time off from work (informal) — book request-മായി ബന്ധമില്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Of' vs 'off' = spelling confusion — same sound, different word.

(C) on
• 'Ask on' = no such collocation.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'on' = frequently used preposition, random guess ആകാം.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'Ask FOR, Look FOR, Wait FOR, Hope FOR — want ചെയ്യുന്ന verbs-ഒക്കെ FOR! Something വേണ്ട = FOR!'
• FOR = wanting/seeking family: ask for · look for · wait for · search for · hope for · apply for
• PSC strategy: blank-ൽ 'ask' ഉണ്ടെങ്കിൽ, meaning 'request' ആണോ? → FOR. ഈ ഒരൊറ്റ test!

questionText: That is the ___________ bracelet I have ever seen.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (C) prettiest

• 'The _____ bracelet I have EVER seen' — 'ever' + 'the' ഉള്ളപ്പോൾ superlative degree ആണ്!
• 'Pretty' → 'prettiest' (superlative). '-y' ending adjective: y → i, add -est. pretty → prettIEST.
• 'The prettiest bracelet I have ever seen' = ഞാൻ ഇതുവരെ കണ്ടിട്ടുള്ളതിൽ ഏറ്റവും beautiful bracelet — superlative perfect!
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula:
  1 syllable: add -er/-est (tall→taller→tallest)
  2 syllables ending in -y: change y→i, add -er/-est (pretty→prettier→prettiest)
  2+ syllables (others): more/most (beautiful→more beautiful→most beautiful)
• 'Most pretty' = WRONG! '-y' ending words-ൽ 'most' use ചെയ്യില്ല — prettiest ആണ് (not most pretty).
• Superlative signal words: 'ever', 'of all', 'in the world', 'in the class', 'the + ___est'
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Degrees of comparison — adjective formation PSC-ൽ regular. -y ending words (pretty, happy, heavy, easy) → -iest superlative = common trap.
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. She is the prettiest girl in the class.
  2. This is the happiest day of my life.
  3. That is the most beautiful painting I have ever seen. (beautiful = 3 syllables → most)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

(A) pretty
• Positive degree — 'the pretty bracelet' = comparison ഇല്ല. 'I have ever seen' + 'the' = superlative signal.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Sentence-ൽ 'pretty bracelet' naturally feel ആകും. 'Ever seen' signal miss ചെയ്തേക്കാം.

(B) more pretty
• Comparative degree + wrong formation. 'More pretty' = grammatically wrong ('prettier' ആണ് comparative). Comparative ഇവിടെ need-ഉം ഇല്ല — superlative signal 'ever/the' ഉണ്ട്.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'More' = comparison feel — students 'the most ___' sense-ൽ 'more' pick ചെയ്തേക്കാം.

(D) most pretty
• 'Most pretty' = wrong formation. '-y' ending adjectives-ൽ 'most' + adjective ഉപയോഗിക്കില്ല — 'prettiest' ആണ്.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Most' = superlative feel, 'most pretty' = superlative sound. പക്ഷേ '-y' ending = -iest rule!
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'prettY ends in Y → prettIEST ends in IEST! Y → I, add EST. happy→happIEST, easy→easIEST, pretty→prettIEST!'
• Never 'most pretty' — '-y' ending = change y to i, add est. ഈ ഒരൊറ്റ rule!
• PSC strategy: 'ever/of all/in the world' signal words കണ്ടാൽ → superlative. '-y' ending adjective ആണോ? → -iest (not most ___). Guaranteed correct!

questionText: Prasad doesn't look well today, ___________ ? (Use proper question tag)

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (A) does he?

• 'Prasad doesn't look well today' — NEGATIVE statement (doesn't = does + not).
• Negative statement → Positive tag rule. 'Doesn't' → auxiliary 'does' → positive tag = 'does he?'
• 'Prasad' = singular male → pronoun = 'he'.
• 'Prasad doesn't look well today, does he?' — ഇന്ന് Prasad-ന് സുഖമില്ലേ? — natural conversation!
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula:
  Negative statement (doesn't/isn't/won't/haven't) → Positive tag (does/is/will/have)
  Positive statement → Negative tag
• Negative auxiliary → positive tag (strip the 'n't'):
  doesn't → does | isn't → is | aren't → are | won't → will | can't → can | hasn't → has
• Subject: Prasad = he (singular male)
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Simple Present negative tags (doesn't/don't → does/do) — PSC-ൽ frequent.
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. She doesn't like coffee, does she?
  2. He doesn't work here, does he?
  3. They don't know the answer, do they?
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

(B) doesn't he?
• 'Doesn't he?' = negative tag — statement negative ആണ്, tag-ഉം negative ആകില്ല! Polarity flip = mandatory.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Statement-ൽ 'doesn't' കണ്ട് tag-ലും 'doesn't' ഇട്ടേക്കാം — ഏറ്റവും common mistake!

(C) did he?
• 'Did' = past tense auxiliary. Statement present tense ('doesn't look') — past tense tag use ചെയ്യില്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Today' = recent, past feel ആകും. 'Did he' guess ചെയ്തേക്കാം.

(D) didn't he?
• Past negative — tense wrong, polarity-ഉം wrong (negative statement → positive tag വേണം).
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Double error — tense-ഉം polarity-ഉം wrong. Students random guess ആയി pick ചെയ്തേക്കാം.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'DOESN'T → strip the NOT → DOES! Negative flip to positive — always!'
  doesn't → does | isn't → is | won't → will | can't → can
• Two steps: ① Statement negative ആണോ? → tag positive ആകണം. ② Auxiliary strip ചെയ്ത് positive form ഇടൂ.
• PSC tip: 'doesn't look' → strip 'n't' → 'does' → positive → 'does he?' ഈ 3-second formula!

questionText: Ammu ________ her exam last year.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (D) passed

• 'Ammu _____ her exam LAST YEAR' — 'last year' = specific past time. Simple Past tense guaranteed!
• 'Pass' = regular verb → past tense = 'passed' (add -ed).
• 'Ammu passed her exam last year' = Ammu കഴിഞ്ഞ വർഷം exam pass ചെയ്തു — simple, clean, correct!
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: 'Last year/week/month', 'yesterday', 'ago' → Simple Past (V2) always!
• Tense signal words:
  yesterday / last night / last year / in 2020 / ago → Simple Past
  just / already / yet / recently → Present Perfect
  every day / usually / always → Simple Present
  now / at this moment → Present Continuous
  tomorrow / next week → Future
• 'Pass' = regular verb: pass → passed → passed
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Signal word → tense identification = PSC-ൽ every paper! 'Last year' = Simple Past — ഇത് memorize ചെയ്തിരുന്നാൽ guaranteed mark.
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. She passed her PSC exam last year.
  2. He finished the project two days ago.
  3. They won the championship in 2023.
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

(A) passes
• Simple Present — 'last year' past signal-മായി match ആകില്ല. 'Ammu passes' = habitual action present.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Third person singular + present = 'passes' natural feel. 'Last year' signal ignore ചെയ്തേക്കാം.

(B) had passed
• Past Perfect — ഒരു past action മറ്റൊരു past action-ന് മുമ്പ് happen ചെയ്തിരുന്നു എന്ന് show ചെയ്യാൻ. ഇവിടെ second past action ഇല്ല — only 'last year'.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Had passed' = formal, complete feel. Exam context-ൽ formal sound ചെയ്തേക്കാം. പക്ഷേ 'last year' alone = Simple Past!

(C) has passed
• Present Perfect — 'just/already/yet' ഉള്ള sentences-ൽ use ചെയ്യും. 'Last year' specific time ഉള്ളപ്പോൾ Present Perfect use ചെയ്യില്ല!
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Achievement feel — 'has passed' = success announcement feel. പക്ഷേ 'last year' specific past time = Simple Past.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'LAST = PAST — last year, last week, last night = always Simple Past! Last-ൽ past hide ആയിരിക്കുന്നു!'
• 'Has passed' + 'last year' = NEVER together! Present Perfect + specific past time = impossible combo.
• PSC guaranteed: 'last year' → circle it → Simple Past → check regular/irregular verb → write V2 → done in 5 seconds!

questionText: Identify the correct sentence from the following options.
optionA: Flyers were all around the venue
optionB: Flairs were all around the venue
optionC: Fleyers were all around the venue
optionD: Flayers were all around the venue

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (A) Flyers were all around the venue

• 'Flyers' = event venue-ൽ distribute ചെയ്ത promotional leaflets/pamphlets — F-L-Y-E-R-S.
• 'Fly' → 'Flyer' → 'Flyers' (regular word formation).
• Context: venue-ൽ flyers = event advertisements, promotional materials. ഇത് daily life-ൽ കാണുന്ന word — PSC exam hall-ൽ ഒരിക്കലും confuse ആകരുത്!
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: FLY → FLYER → FLYERS (promotional leaflet/one who flies)
• Similar-sounding words, different meanings — ഇതാണ് PSC trap:
  Flyers = leaflets/pamphlets (venue-ൽ ഉള്ളത്) ✓
  Flairs = plural of flair (talent/ability — 'She has a flair for music')
  Flayers = plural of flayer (one who skins/flays animals)
  Fleyers = NOT a real English word!
• Context clue: 'around the venue' = event = promotional leaflets = Flyers.
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Spelling + context-based word choice — similar-sounding words PSC test ചെയ്യും.
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. Flyers were distributed at the entrance of the event.
  2. She has a flair for languages. (flair = talent — different word!)
  3. High flyers in PSC study consistently.
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

(B) Flairs were all around the venue
• 'Flairs' = plural of flair (talent). 'Talents were all around the venue' = meaningless sentence!
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Flairs'/'Flyers' = similar sound. Phonetic confusion create ചെയ്ത് context ignore ചെയ്തേക്കാം.

(C) Fleyers were all around the venue
• 'Fleyers' = real English word അല്ല — 'Flyers'-ന്റെ wrong spelling.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Fly' + 'ers' = 'fleyers' variant spelling ആണ് എന്ന് students think ചെയ്തേക്കാം. ഇല്ല!

(D) Flayers were all around the venue
• 'Flayers' = animals skin ചെയ്യുന്നവർ — venue-ൽ ഇവർ ആണോ? Context completely wrong!
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Flayers'/'Flyers' = ഒരൊറ്റ letter difference (a vs y). Casual reading-ൽ miss ആകും.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'FLY + ER = FLYER — ഒരു event-ൽ FLY ചെയ്ത് distribute ആകുന്ന pamphlet = FLYER!'
• Context first: venue + distribute = promotional = Flyers. Flair = talent (music/art context). ഇവ confuse ആകരുത്!
• PSC strategy: Similar-sounding words ആകുമ്പോൾ context ആദ്യം നോക്കൂ — meaning match ചെയ്യുന്ന word select ചെയ്യൂ.

questionText:
Fill in the blank by adding the correct phrasal verbs given as options.

We had to _______ the party to another day owing to the sudden turn of events.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (D) Put off

• 'We had to _____ the party to another day' — party ഒരു different day-ലേക്ക് മാറ്റി — postpone ചെയ്തു!
• 'Put off' = postpone/delay — മറ്റൊരു ദിവസത്തേക്ക് മാറ്റിവയ്ക്കുക.
• 'Owing to a sudden turn of events' = unexpected reason-ൽ party postpone ചെയ്തു — life-ൽ ഇങ്ങനെ ഉണ്ടായിട്ടുണ്ടേ?
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Put OFF = postpone (calendar-ൽ നിന്ന് OFF ആക്കി later-ലേക്ക് push!)
• 'PUT' phrasal verbs family:
  Put off = postpone ('The match was put off due to rain.')
  Put on = wear / organize ('Put on a show', 'Put on your shoes')
  Put up = accommodate / tolerate ('Put up with rudeness', 'Put up a guest')
  Put out = extinguish ('Put out the fire') / publish
  Put in = insert / submit ('Put in an application')
  Put across = communicate ('Put across your ideas')
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: 'Put off' = PSC-ൽ ഏറ്റവും test ചെയ്ത phrasal verb. Postpone context കണ്ടാൽ = put off!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. The cricket match was put off due to rain.
  2. Don't put off what you can do today.
  3. The meeting was put off to next Monday.
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

(A) Put on
• 'Put on' = wear / organize an event. 'Put on the party' = party organize ചെയ്യുക — postpone ചെയ്യുക അല്ല!
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Put on a party' = organize ആണ്, 'to another day'-ക്ക് 'put on' fit ആകില്ല.

(B) Put in
• 'Put in' = insert / submit. Party postpone-മായി ഒരു ബന്ധവും ഇല്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'In' particle = common, random guess ആകാം.

(C) Put out
• 'Put out' = extinguish (fire) / publish. Party-ക്ക് apply ആകില്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Put out' = end/cancel feel — party cancel = put out? No! Cancel = call off. Postpone = put off.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'PUT OFF = calendar-ൽ നിന്ന് OFF ആക്കി push! Party OFF-ആക്കി later ലേക്ക് = Put off = Postpone!'
• Call off (cancel forever) vs Put off (postpone = later date) — ഈ difference ഓർക്കൂ!
• PSC strategy: 'to another day / due to rain / because of illness' context കണ്ടാൽ → postpone → 'put off'. ഒരിക്കലും miss ആകില്ല!

questionText:
Identify the error in the following sentence.

The fire fighters were well equipped with latest methods.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (D) Latest

• 'well equipped with latest methods' — error: 'latest' superlative adjective-ന് മുമ്പ് 'the' ഇല്ല!
• 'The latest methods' ആണ് correct — superlative adjective-ന് മുമ്പ് 'the' mandatory.
• 'Latest' = superlative of 'late'. Superlatives: the best, the worst, the latest, the first, the last — 'the' ഇല്ലാതെ superlative incomplete!
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: THE + superlative adjective — ALWAYS! 'the' ഇല്ലാതെ superlative use ചെയ്യില്ല.
  the latest | the best | the worst | the most modern | the first | the last
• ഇവിടെ error: 'equipped with latest methods' → 'equipped with THE latest methods'
• Superlative-ൽ 'the' mandatory ആകുന്നത് because: superlative = ഏറ്റവും = specific/unique = definite article 'the' വേണം.
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Article before superlative = PSC-ൽ error identification-ൽ test ചെയ്യും. 'The' missing before superlative = common error type.
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. They used the latest technology.
  2. She is the best student in the class.
  3. This is the most efficient method.
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

(A) Fire fighters
• 'Fire fighters' — correctly used compound noun (also written 'firefighters'). No error.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Compound word spelling doubt create ചെയ്ത് ഇവിടെ focus ആക്കും — real error 'latest'-ൽ ആണ്.

(B) Were
• 'Fire fighters were' = subject-verb agreement correct (plural subject + plural verb). No error.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Verb agreement check ചെയ്ത് error ഇല്ല എന്ന് confirm ആകും, real error miss ആകും.

(C) Equipped
• 'Well equipped with' — grammatically correct. 'Equipped with' = fixed collocation.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Preposition 'with' doubt create ചെയ്ത് ഇവിടെ focus ആക്കും.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'SUPERLATIVE = SUPREME = needs THE crown! THE latest, THE best, THE greatest — royalty always needs THE!'
• Error spotting order: ① Subject-verb ② Tense ③ Article ④ Preposition — 'latest' without 'the' = Article error.
• PSC tip: 'latest/best/worst/first/last' ഉള്ള sentence-ൽ 'the' ഇല്ലേ? → guaranteed error → 'the' add ചെയ്യൂ!

questionText: Identify the correctly spelt word from the options.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (B) Conscientious

• Correct spelling: C-O-N-S-C-I-E-N-T-I-O-U-S — 'conscientious' = ഉത്തരവാദിത്വബോധത്തോടെ, carefully and thoroughly ജോലി ചെയ്യുന്ന.
• Key tricky part: 'SCI' — ഇത് silent 'c'! 'Science'-ൽ 'sci' ഉള്ളതുപോലെ. CON-SCI-EN-TIOUS — science ഉണ്ടോ ഈ word-ൽ?
• 'A conscientious officer' = ഒരു dedicated, thorough officer — PSC-ൽ ഇങ്ങനെ ആകണം!
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: CON + SCI + EN + TI + OUS — 5 parts ആയി break ചെയ്ത് ഓർക്കൂ!
• 'SCI' part-ൽ silent 'c' — 'SCIENCE'-ൽ 'sci' = /ʃ/ sound (sh sound). Conscientious-ഉം same: 'con-SHI-en-tious'.
• Word family:
  Conscientious (adj) → Conscientiously (adv) → Conscientiousness (n)
  Related: Conscience (n) = moral sense (the inner voice!)
• Etymology: Latin 'conscientia' = con (with) + scire (to know) = knowing what is right.
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: 'Conscientious' = PSC-ൽ ഏറ്റവും test ചെയ്ത spelling word-കളിൽ ഒന്ന്.
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. A conscientious PSC candidate studies every day.
  2. She is conscientious about her work.
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

(A) Consientious
• 'sci' → 'si' — silent 'c' missing! 'Consientious' vs 'Conscientious' — 'c' drop ചെയ്തിരിക്കുന്നു.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Pronunciation-ൽ 'sci' = 'shi' sound — 'c' hear ആകില്ല, so students writing by sound 'c' drop ചെയ്തേക്കാം.

(C) Consceintious
• 'ie' → 'ei' swap — 'consc-EI-ntious'. 'SCIENCE'-ൽ 'ei' ഇല്ല, 'ie' ആണ്: sci-ENCE.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: ie/ei confusion — 'i before e except after c' rule confuse create ചെയ്ത് wrong order.

(D) Consecintious
• 'Consec-' = consecutive-ൻ്റെ beginning! Letter order completely wrong.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Consecutive' familiar word — students conflate the spelling.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'CONSCIENTIOUS contains SCIENCE — a conscientious student loves SCIENCE! CONSC-I-ENT-IOUS = find SCIENCE inside!'
• Breakdown: CON + SCIENCE + TIOUS = conscientious. Science ഓർത്തിരുന്നാൽ spelling ഓർക്കാം!
• PSC tip: 'sci' part-ൽ 'c' ഉണ്ട് — silent ആണ്, പക്ഷേ spelling-ൽ ഉണ്ടാകണം. Conscientious — science ഓർക്കൂ, ജയിക്കൂ!

questionText:
Which word given as options means exactly the same as the one in bold in the following sentence.

To go abroad was the most frivolous decision taken by her.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (C) Silly

• 'Frivolous' = നിസ്സാരമായ, ഗൗരവമില്ലാത്ത — not serious, trivial, without good reason.
• Sentence: 'To go abroad was the most frivolous decision' = ഒരു കാര്യവും ഇല്ലാതെ abroad പോകാൻ decide ചെയ്തത് = silly decision!
• 'Silly' = foolish, not serious — 'frivolous'-ന്റെ exact synonym. ഒരേ meaning, different word.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Frivolous = not serious = silly = trivial = petty
• Synonym group:
  frivolous = silly, trivial, petty, flippant, superficial, trifling, childish
• Antonym group:
  frivolous ↔ serious, grave, earnest, responsible, sensible, thoughtful
• Word family:
  Frivolous (adj) → Frivolously (adv) → Frivolity (n)
• Etymology: Latin 'frivolus' = crumbled, of little worth.
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: 'Frivolous' synonym — PSC-ൽ vocabulary test-ൽ ഇടയ്ക്ക് വരുന്ന word.
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. She was criticised for her frivolous/silly spending habits.
  2. Don't waste time on frivolous matters.
  3. His frivolous attitude affected his PSC preparation.
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

(A) Timely
• 'Timely' = ശരിയായ സമയത്ത് ചെയ്തത് (positive word) — 'frivolous' = negative word. Opposite directions!
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Decision' context-ൽ 'timely decision' = natural phrase. Frivolous-ൻ്റെ opposite feel-ൽ 'timely' pick ചെയ്തേക്കാം.

(B) Tactful
• 'Tactful' = tactfully handle ചെയ്യുന്ന (positive) — 'frivolous' = negative/silly. Again opposite!
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Professional context → 'tactful' = good quality feel. Frivolous ≠ tactful.

(D) Thoughtful
• 'Thoughtful' = carefully considered (positive) — frivolous = WITHOUT thought (negative). Direct antonym!
• 🪤 PSC Trap: A, B, D — all three are POSITIVE words! Frivolous = NEGATIVE. Positive words = antonyms, not synonyms. Eliminate all three instantly!
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'FRIVOLOUS = TRIVIAL — same feel, both mean NOT SERIOUS! Friv-OLOUS = friv-OLITY = trivial!'
• Quick elimination: Frivolous = negative word. Timely/Tactful/Thoughtful = positive words → all three wrong → Silly = correct!
• PSC strategy: Synonym ചോദ്യത്തിൽ word positive ആണോ negative ആണോ identify ചെയ്ത് same polarity option select ചെയ്യൂ.

questionText: De facto means

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (B) In reality or in fact

• 'De facto' = Latin — 'de' (from/of) + 'facto' (fact/deed). Literally: 'from fact'.
• Meaning: actually ഉള്ളത്, legally ഇല്ലെങ്കിലും practically ഉള്ളത് — in reality or in fact.
• 'De facto leader' = officially appointed അല്ലെങ്കിലും actually leader ആയി function ചെയ്യുന്ന ആൾ.
• ഓർക്കൂ: De facto ↔ De jure (by law) — ഈ pair ഒരുമിച്ച് ഓർക്കൂ!
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: De facto = in reality/practice ↔ De jure = by law (legal)
• Latin phrases PSC must-know list:
  De facto = in reality/practice
  De jure = by law/right
  Sine die = without fixed date (indefinitely postponed)
  Bona fide = in good faith/genuine
  Status quo = existing state of affairs
  Prima facie = at first sight/appearance
  Per capita = per person
  Vox populi = voice of the people
  Alma mater = school/university attended
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Latin phrases = PSC-ൽ every paper! De facto, Bona fide, Sine die — ഈ മൂന്നും ഏറ്റവും frequent.
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. He became the de facto ruler after the president fled.
  2. De jure, she is the owner, but de facto, he controls everything.
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

(A) By law
• 'By law' = De JURE — De facto-ൻ്റെ exact opposite! ഈ pair confuse ചെയ്ത് PSC test ചെയ്യും.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: De facto / De jure = classic PSC confusable pair. 'De' = Latin, students confuse the two.

(C) By force
• 'By force' = no Latin phrase means exactly this in context. 'De facto' government-ൽ power seizure feel ആകും — students 'by force' pick ചെയ്തേക്കാം.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Military governments 'de facto' control ചെയ്യും — 'by force' association create ചെയ്ത് confuse ആക്കും.

(D) By accident
• Completely unrelated to 'de facto' meaning.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Latin phrase = formal/technical — 'accident' = unexpected event — random distractor.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'De FACTO = De FACT = based on FACT = REALITY! De JURE = De JURY = court = LAW!'
  Factory = fact-based. De facto = fact-based reality. De jure = jury/judge = law.
• De facto (reality) ↔ De jure (law) — ഈ pair ഒരുമിച്ച് ഓർക്കൂ, ഒരിക്കലും confuse ആകരുത്!
• PSC Latin hot list: De facto · De jure · Sine die · Bona fide · Status quo · Prima facie — ഇത് 6 ഓർത്തിരുന്നാൽ Latin questions guaranteed!

questionText: A life story written by a person about himself is called

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (B) Autobiography

• 'A life story written BY A PERSON ABOUT HIMSELF' = Autobiography.
• Greek: 'auto' (self) + 'bio' (life) + 'graphy' (writing) = self-life-writing = autobiography!
• 'Auto' = self! Automatic car = drives itself. Autobiography = writes itself about itself — ഒരാൾ സ്വന്തം ജീവിതം സ്വയം എഴുതുന്നത്.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: AUTO + BIO + GRAPHY = self + life + writing = autobiography (self writes own life)
• Greek roots:
  auto = self (automatic, autonomy, autopilot, autograph)
  bio = life (biology, biography, biodiversity)
  graphy = writing (biography, photography, geography, calligraphy)
• Biography vs Autobiography:
  Biography = another person writes about someone (bio + graphy)
  Autobiography = person writes about themselves (AUTO + bio + graphy)
• Memoir = personal memories, partial life story (not complete)
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Biography vs Autobiography = PSC-ൽ classic one-word substitute question. ഓരോ year-ഉം ഈ pair test ചെയ്യും.
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. 'My Experiments with Truth' = Gandhi's autobiography.
  2. A biography of APJ Abdul Kalam was written by his friend.
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

(A) Biography
• Biography = ANOTHER PERSON writes about someone. 'Written BY A PERSON ABOUT HIMSELF' = self-written → autobiography, not biography.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Biography vs Autobiography — most confusable pair. 'Life story' context-ൽ 'biography' naturally feel ആകും. 'By himself' detail miss ആകരുത്!

(C) Memoir
• Memoir = personal memories/experiences, usually partial/thematic — complete life story അല്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Memoir = personal writing → 'autobiography' feel. പക്ഷേ memoir ≠ complete life story, autobiography = complete life story.

(D) Chronicle
• Chronicle = historical events time order-ൽ record ചെയ്ത document — personal life story അല്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Record of events' = chronicle sound professional — students biography/chronicle confuse ചെയ്തേക്കാം.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'AUTO = AUTOMATIC = BY YOURSELF! AUTObiography = write BY YOURSELF about YOURSELF!'
  Auto ഇല്ലേ? → biography (someone else). Auto ഉണ്ടോ? → autobiography (yourself)!
• Greek root shortcut: auto (self) ഓർത്തിരുന്നാൽ one-word substitutes-ൽ 'auto' ഉള്ള words = always self-related.
• PSC: 'written by a person about himself' → AUTO is the key. Answer = Autobiography. 3 seconds!

questionText: The train will __________ at 6 a.m.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (A) leave

• 'The train will _____ at 6 a.m.' — 'will' = future auxiliary already sentence-ൽ ഉണ്ട്!
• 'Will' + V1 (base form/bare infinitive) = Simple Future. 'Will leave' — 'leave' = base form.
• 'Will + V1' = simple rule. Will-ന് ശേഷം verb-ഉടെ base form മാത്രം — no -s, no -ed, no -ing!
• 'The train will leave at 6 a.m.' = തീവണ്ടി രാവിലെ 6 മണിക്ക് പോകും — timetable future!
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: will/shall + V1 (base form) — NEVER will + V2 or will + V-ing!
  will leave ✓ | will go ✓ | will arrive ✓
  will left ✗ | will went ✗ | will leaving ✗
• 'Leave' vs similar-looking options:
  leave (V1) = to depart ← correct!
  live (V1) = to reside (different word, different meaning)
  left (V2) = past tense of leave ← wrong after 'will'
  life (noun) = ജീവൻ ← not a verb at all!
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: will + V1 = basic future tense + confusable words (leave/live/left/life) = double trap!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. The train will leave at 6 a.m.
  2. She will complete the work by tomorrow.
  3. We will arrive next week.
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

(B) live
• 'Live' = ജീവിക്കുക/താമസിക്കുക — 'The train will live'?! Train ജീവിക്കുകയോ? Meaning completely wrong!
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'leave' vs 'live' = ഒരൊറ്റ letter difference (ea vs i). Fast reading-ൽ confuse ആകും.

(C) left
• 'Left' = V2 (past tense of leave). 'Will left' = grammatically wrong — 'will' + V2 ഒരിക്കലും ഇടില്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Left' = departure meaning (train left) ആണ് — 'left' feel correct ആകും, പക്ഷേ 'will' + V2 = wrong.

(D) life
• 'Life' = noun (ജീവൻ). 'Will life' = noun-ഐ verb position-ൽ ഇടൽ — impossible!
• 🪤 PSC Trap: leave/live/left/life = 4 words, same sound family — phonetic trap create ചെയ്ത് confuse ആക്കും.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'WILL + BARE VERB only! Will leaves ✗, will left ✗, will LEAVE ✓ — will-ന് ശേഷം bare/naked verb!'
• Word alert: leave (depart) ≠ live (reside) ≠ left (past) ≠ life (noun) — 4 traps ഒരൊറ്റ question-ൽ!
• PSC strategy: 'will ___' blank ആണോ? → V1 (base form) only. Past tense (left), noun (life), different word (live) — eliminate ചെയ്ത് leave select ചെയ്യൂ!

questionText: Choose the correctly spelt word.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (C) Definitely

• Correct spelling: D-E-F-I-N-I-T-E-L-Y — 'definitely' = തീർച്ചയായും, without any doubt.
• Key: base word = 'DEFINITE' + 'LY' = definitely. 'Definite' ഓർത്തിരുന്നാൽ 'definitely' ഒരിക്കലും miss ആകില്ല!
• Common mistake: 'definately' — 'i' → 'a' ആക്കൽ. Pronunciation-ൽ 'et' sound ആണ്, പക്ഷേ spelling-ൽ 'ite' — 'definITE'!
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: DEFINITE + LY = DEFINITELY (base word ഇളക്കില്ല!)
  definite (adjective) → definitely (adverb) — simple -ly addition!
• Why students write 'definately': Pronunciation = 'DEF-in-et-lee' → 'a' sound ആണ്. പക്ഷേ spelling = 'i' (definITE).
• Similar pattern:
  complete + ly = completely (keep 'e')
  absolute + ly = absolutely (keep 'e')
  definite + ly = definitely (keep all of 'definite')
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: 'Definitely' = ഇംഗ്ലീഷിലെ most misspelled words list-ൽ ഉണ്ട്. PSC-ൽ ഇടയ്ക്ക് test ചെയ്യും.
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. She will definitely pass the PSC exam.
  2. This is definitely the correct answer.
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

(A) Definately
• 'i' → 'a' — 'defin-A-tely'. 'Definite'-ൽ 'i' ആണ്, 'a' അല്ല. Pronunciation mislead ചെയ്ത് 'a' write ചെയ്തേക്കാം.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: ഏറ്റവും common misspelling — pronunciation follow ചെയ്ത് 'a' ഇടൽ. PSC ഇത് option-ൽ ഇടുന്നത് ഇതുകൊണ്ടാണ്.

(B) Definetely
• 'definEtely' — 'i' → 'e' ആക്കിയിരിക്കുന്നു. 'Definite'-ൽ 'i' ആണ്.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Sound-based spelling — 'e' sound ആണ് (correctly) pronounce ചെയ്യുക = 'e' write ചെയ്തേക്കാം.

(D) Definatly
• 'a' + '-tly' (ite drop ചെയ്ത്) — double error.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: A-ൻ്റെ shortened version — two mistakes ഒരുമിച്ച്.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'DEFINITELY = DEFINITE + LY — I am DEFINITE about the I in definitely!'
  definITE → definITEly. 'i' is definitely there!
• Short rule: DEFINITE base word ഓർക്കൂ. '-ly' add ചെയ്ത് definitely ആകും. Base word ഒരിക്കലും change ആകില്ല.
• PSC tip: 'definately', 'definetely' = options-ൽ കണ്ടാൽ ഉടൻ eliminate ചെയ്യൂ. Only 'definitely' (-itely ending) = correct!

questionText: 'To throw in the towel' means

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (B) To give up

• 'Throw in the towel' = give up, admit defeat — ഉപേക്ഷിക്കുക, തോൽവി സമ്മതിക്കുക.
• Origin: Boxing! Trainer, fighter-ന് fight continue ചെയ്യാൻ കഴിയില്ല എന്ന് കണ്ടാൽ ring-ലേക്ക് towel throw ചെയ്യും — surrender signal!
• 'After years of trying, he finally threw in the towel' = വർഷങ്ങൾ ശ്രമിച്ചിട്ടും, ഒടുവിൽ ഉപേക്ഷിച്ചു. PSC-ൽ ഒരിക്കലും throw in the towel ചെയ്യരുത്!
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Throw in the towel = give up = surrender (boxing origin)
• Sports/boxing idioms group:
  Throw in the towel = give up
  Below the belt = unfair action
  On the ropes = in serious trouble
  Saved by the bell = rescued at the last moment
  Throw your hat in the ring = enter a competition
• Synonyms of 'throw in the towel':
  give up | surrender | quit | admit defeat | wave the white flag | call it quits
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Idiom meanings = PSC-ൽ Vocabulary section-ൽ frequent. Boxing idioms test ചെയ്തിട്ടുണ്ട്.
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. Despite failures, a PSC aspirant should never throw in the towel.
  2. He threw in the towel after failing three times.
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

(A) To wash clothes
• Towel = bathroom item → washing/drying association. പക്ഷേ idiom meaning = literal meaning അല്ല!
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Towel' word literal ആയി interpret ചെയ്ത് washing/drying connect ചെയ്തേക്കാം.

(C) To clean the room
• Towel = cleaning ആകാം — but again literal interpretation, idiom ≠ literal!
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Throw in' = room-ലേക്ക് towel throw ചെയ്ത് clean ചെയ്യൽ feel. Literal trap!

(D) To become angry
• 'Throw' = aggressive action → anger feel. പക്ഷേ 'throw in the towel' = surrender/quit, anger അല്ല!
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Throw' = forceful, angry action association. Idiom = complete opposite — it's about giving up, not getting angry.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'THROW IN THE TOWEL = Boxing trainer-ൻ്റെ WHITE FLAG! Towel throw = ഞങ്ങൾ quit ചെയ്തു = GIVE UP!'
• Idiom key: Boxing ring-ൽ towel throw = surrender. Visualize the boxing scene — trainer throwing white towel = fight over = give up!
• PSC strategy: Idioms-ൽ literal meaning NEVER correct ആകില്ല. Towel = washing/cleaning feel ആകും — eliminate ചെയ്ത് figurative meaning pick ചെയ്യൂ. Give up = throw in the towel!

topic: Types of Sentences and Interchange of Sentences
questionText: The weather was extremely bad. ________ the match was not cancelled.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (C) Nevertheless

• 'Nevertheless' means 'എന്നിരുന്നാലും' — opposite result ഉണ്ടെങ്കിലും മുന്നോട്ട് പോകുന്നു.
• Weather bad ആണ്, BUT match cancelled ആയില്ല — ഇവിടെ contrast/concession വേണം.
• 'Nevertheless' = 'in spite of that' — ഇത് two contrasting ideas-നെ connect ചെയ്യുന്നു.
• PSC context: 'The exam was tough. Nevertheless, she passed.' — ഇതേ pattern.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Contrast connectors — nevertheless / however / yet / still = 'എന്നിരുന്നാലും'
• Nevertheless = adverb of concession; sentence-ന്റെ തുടക്കത്തിൽ വരും, comma follow ചെയ്യും.
• Connectors group:
  - Addition: Moreover, Furthermore, Besides
  - Contrast: Nevertheless, However, Yet, Still
  - Cause: Therefore, Hence, Thus
  - Concession: Although, Even though, Despite
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Connectors/Linkers questions PSC-ൽ ഇടയ്ക്കിടെ വരുന്നു.
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. It was raining. Nevertheless, they continued the match.
  2. She was tired. Nevertheless, she finished the work.
  3. The price was high. Nevertheless, he bought it.
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

(A) Therefore
• 'Therefore' = 'അതിനാൽ' — cause-effect relationship.
• Bad weather → match cancelled ആകണം (effect). But match was NOT cancelled, so 'therefore' logic തെറ്റ്.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Therefore' ഒരു common linking word ആണ്, students automatically choose ചെയ്യും.

(B) Although
• 'Although' conjunction ആണ്; two clauses-നെ connect ചെയ്യും — 'Although it rained, the match continued.'
• ഇവിടെ blank-ന് ശേഷം full clause ഇല്ല, sentence തുടരുന്നു — conjunction ആകില്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Although'-ഉം contrast meaning ഉണ്ട്, so students confuse ചെയ്യും.

(D) Moreover
• 'Moreover' = 'കൂടാതെ' — addition.
• ഒരു extra point add ചെയ്യാനാണ് 'moreover'; contrast ആകില്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'moreover' common word ആണ്, meaning അറിയാതെ choose ചെയ്യും.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'Never-the-LESS' — bad ആണെങ്കിലും less important ആക്കി, move on! Contrast feel ഓർക്കാൻ easy.
• Family: Nevertheless = However = Yet = Still = Nonetheless — all mean 'എന്നിരുന്നാലും'
• PSC strategy: Blank-ൽ വരുന്ന connector choose ചെയ്യുമ്പോൾ ആദ്യം ആ two sentences-ന്റെ relationship നോക്കുക — Addition? Contrast? Cause? അത് identify ചെയ്താൽ answer easy!

questionText: Which of the following statements is not correct?
optionA: Hundred rupees is not a big amount
optionB: The furniture was bought from Paris
optionC: Every School and College have a play ground
optionD: The children, as well as the teacher have left

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (C) Every School and College have a play ground

• 'Every' + noun + and + noun = SINGULAR verb — 'has' വേണം, 'have' wrong.
• 'Every school and college HAS a playground' — ഇതാണ് correct.
• 'Every' ഉള്ളപ്പോൾ subject plural ആയാലും verb singular ആകും.
• ഈ statement grammatically incorrect ആണ്, so this is the answer to 'which is NOT correct'.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Every/Each + noun(s) → always SINGULAR verb
• 'Every boy and girl IS present.' (not are)
• 'Each student HAS a book.' (not have)
• Other options explanation:
  - 'Hundred rupees IS' ✅ — amount/sum = singular
  - 'Furniture WAS' ✅ — uncountable noun = singular
  - 'Children... as well as teacher HAVE' — main subject = children (plural) → have ✅
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: 'Every/Each' rule PSC-ൽ ഏറ്റവും കൂടുതൽ test ചെയ്യുന്ന S-V agreement rule ആണ്.
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. Every man and woman has equal rights.
  2. Each teacher and student was present.
  3. Every pen and pencil has been kept.
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

(A) Hundred rupees is not a big amount
• Amount/sum of money = singular verb. ✅ Correct statement.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'rupees' plural ആണ്, so 'are' വേണം എന്ന് തോന്നും — but amount as a whole = singular.

(B) The furniture was bought from Paris
• 'Furniture' uncountable noun = singular verb. ✅ Correct statement.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: ഒന്നിലധികം pieces of furniture ഉണ്ടെങ്കിലും 'furniture' singular ആണ്.

(D) The children, as well as the teacher have left
• 'As well as' = the main subject (children) governs the verb. Children = plural → 'have' ✅ Correct.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'as well as teacher' കൂടി ഉള്ളതിനാൽ singular ആകും എന്ന് തോന്നും — wrong!
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'EVERY time = ONE time' — Every/Each = singular, always!
• PSC-ൽ 'every/each' ഉള്ള ഏത് sentence-ഉം singular verb expect ചെയ്യുക.
• Quick check: 'as well as / together with / along with' — main subject-നെ മാത്രം നോക്കുക, bracket ചെയ്ത് ignore ചെയ്യുക.

questionText: Radhika will attain good marks, _________?

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (A) Won't she?

• 'Radhika will attain good marks' — positive statement, auxiliary = 'will'.
• Positive statement → Negative tag: won't she?
• Subject = Radhika → pronoun = she.
• 🔑 Rule applied: will → won't (negative tag).
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula:
  Positive statement + negative tag (verb + n't + pronoun?)
  Negative statement + positive tag (verb + pronoun?)
• Same auxiliary verb in statement = same in tag.
• Subject → correct pronoun:
  Radhika = she, children = they, I = am I? / aren't I?
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Question tags PSC-ൽ almost every paper-ൽ വരുന്നു.
• Special tags:
  - 'Let's go...' → shall we?
  - 'I am...' → aren't I?
  - Imperative → will you? / won't you?
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. She can swim, can't she?
  2. They haven't left, have they?
  3. Let's go, shall we?
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

(B) Can't she?
• Statement-ൽ auxiliary 'will' ആണ്, 'can' അല്ല — wrong auxiliary.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'can' common auxiliary ആണ്, confuse ചെയ്യാൻ PSC ഇടും.

(C) Will she?
• Positive statement → positive tag wrong! Negative tag വേണം.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Same verb use ചെയ്തു positive tag — rule മറന്നാൽ ഇത് choose ചെയ്യും.

(D) Can she?
• Wrong auxiliary (can instead of will) + wrong polarity (positive instead of negative).
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Double error — both auxiliary and polarity wrong.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'Positive in, Negative out — Negative in, Positive out' — like a seesaw!
• Tag-ൽ statement-ന്റെ auxiliary-നെ മാത്രം use ചെയ്യുക — will→won't, can→can't, is→isn't.
• PSC strategy: First identify positive/negative → flip it → find same auxiliary → done!

questionText: Vishal's father is going abroad ___________ a month.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (D) For

• 'For a month' = ഒരു മാസത്തേക്ക് (duration).
• 'Going abroad for a month' — specific duration of time indicate ചെയ്യുന്നു.
• 'For' = period of time (a month, two years, a week) — duration.
• 'Since' = starting point of time (Monday, 2020, childhood) — not duration.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula:
  FOR + duration (how long) → for a month, for two days
  SINCE + point of time (when it started) → since Monday, since 2020
• Time preposition guide:
  - AT: specific time (at 5 pm, at noon)
  - ON: day/date (on Monday, on 15th July)
  - IN: month/year/season (in July, in 2026, in summer)
  - FOR: duration (for a month)
  - SINCE: starting point (since morning)
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: For vs Since confusion PSC-ൽ ഏറ്റവും common preposition question.
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. I have been waiting for two hours.
  2. She has lived here since 2018.
  3. He went abroad for a year.
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

(A) Since
• 'Since' starting point indicate ചെയ്യുന്നു — 'since Monday' ✓ but 'since a month' ✗.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: For/Since confusion PSC-ൽ classic trap — both relate to time.

(B) In
• 'In' = month/year/season/room — 'in a month' means 'ഒരു മാസം കഴിഞ്ഞ്' (after a month), not 'for a month'.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'in a month' grammatically sounds okay but meaning wrong here.

(C) At
• 'At' = specific point of time (at 5 pm). Duration ആകില്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'At' common preposition, students random ആയി choose ചെയ്യും.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: FOR = HOW LONG (duration), SINCE = WHEN (starting point)
  'I studied FOR three hours SINCE morning' — both in one sentence!
• PSC strategy: Blank-ൽ time expression നോക്കുക — 'a month/year/day' → FOR; 'Monday/2020/childhood' → SINCE.

questionText: Rakesh __________ an appointment to see the doctor. It is at 11 a.m. tomorrow.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (A) Has made

• 'It is at 11 a.m. tomorrow' — appointment already arranged for the future.
• Present Perfect ('has made') = recent past action with present relevance.
• Appointment was made RECENTLY and it is RELEVANT NOW (tomorrow at 11).
• Present Perfect = past action + present connection.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Present Perfect = have/has + V3 (past participle)
• When to use Present Perfect:
  - Recent action with present result: 'I have lost my key' (ഇപ്പോഴും കൈയിൽ ഇല്ല)
  - Scheduled/arranged future: 'I have booked a ticket'
  - Just/already/yet/recently signal words
• Signal words for tenses:
  - Yesterday/last week/ago → Simple Past
  - Just/already/yet/recently → Present Perfect
  - Tomorrow/next week (arranged) → Present Perfect
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Present Perfect vs Simple Past — PSC-ൽ ഏറ്റവും frequent tense question.
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. She has booked the tickets. (already done, future trip)
  2. He has made an appointment for tomorrow.
  3. I have just finished the work.
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

(B) Make
• Base form — no auxiliary, grammatically incomplete.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Simple present feel ആകും but 'Rakesh make' subject-verb agreement-ഉം wrong.

(C) Makes
• Simple Present — habitual action ആണ്. Appointment scheduled ചെയ്ത past action അല്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Rakesh makes' grammatically correct ആണ്, but tense wrong.

(D) Made
• Simple Past — specific past time indicate ചെയ്യും. ഇവിടെ appointment still relevant (tomorrow).
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Past action ആണ് (appointment was made earlier) so 'made' logical feel ആകും — but present relevance ഉള്ളതിനാൽ Present Perfect വേണം.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: Present Perfect = Past action, Present proof!
  'She HAS MADE the appointment' — Made in past, appointment exists NOW.
• PSC strategy: Future reference (tomorrow/next week) + past action = Present Perfect. 'I have arranged everything for tomorrow' — this pattern often appears.

questionText: Which of the following words is the antonym of 'Optimism'?

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (B) Pessimism

• 'Optimism' = ജീവിതത്തിൽ നല്ലത് മാത്രം കാണുന്ന മനോഭാവം — positive outlook.
• 'Pessimism' = ജീവിതത്തിൽ ചീത്ത/നെഗറ്റീവ് മാത്രം കാണുന്ന മനോഭാവം — exact opposite.
• Latin: 'optimus' (best) vs 'pessimus' (worst) — opposite roots!
• PSC context: Optimist vs Pessimist — exam-ൽ ഈ pair ഒന്നിച്ച് ഓർക്കുക.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Antonym = exact opposite in meaning.
• Word family:
  - Optimism (n) → Optimist (person) → Optimistic (adj) → Optimistically (adv)
  - Pessimism (n) → Pessimist (person) → Pessimistic (adj) → Pessimistically (adv)
• Etymology:
  - Optimism: Latin 'optimus' = best
  - Pessimism: Latin 'pessimus' = worst
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Optimism/Pessimism antonym pair PSC-ൽ multiple times tested.
• Related antonym pairs:
  - Hope ↔ Despair
  - Confidence ↔ Diffidence
  - Faith ↔ Doubt
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. His optimism kept him going despite failures.
  2. Pessimism never helps in difficult times.
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

(A) Scepticism
• 'Scepticism' = doubt/questioning — ഒന്നിലും വിശ്വസിക്കാതിരിക്കൽ. Antonym അല്ല, related concept.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: '-ism' ending ഉള്ള word ആണ്, similar feel — confuse ചെയ്യും.

(C) Fanaticism
• 'Fanaticism' = extreme devotion/obsession — തീർത്തും different meaning.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: '-ism' ending ആണ്, but meaning ബന്ധമില്ല.

(D) Philanthropism
• 'Philanthropy' = love of humanity, charitable — positive meaning but not antonym of optimism.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Long difficult word — students unfamiliar ആണ്, so confused ആകും.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: OPTImism = OPTImal/best; PESSImism = PESSIMISTIC/worst — 'PESSI' sounds like 'PASS' cheyyilla (fail)!
• Word family learn ചെയ്യുക: Optimist → sees glass half FULL; Pessimist → sees glass half EMPTY.
• PSC strategy: '-ism' words antonym questions-ൽ paired opposites ഓർക്കുക — optimism/pessimism, realism/idealism.

questionText: Which of the following idiomatic expressions has the meaning 'to reveal the secret'?
optionB: At the drop of a hat

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (A) To spill the beans

• 'Spill the beans' = രഹസ്യം വെളിപ്പെടുത്തുക (to reveal a secret accidentally or intentionally).
• Origin: Ancient Greek voting — beans were used, spilling them revealed the vote!
• PSC context: 'Don't spill the beans about the surprise party!' — secret reveal ആകല്ലേ.
• ⭐ PSC-ൽ ഈ idiom multiple times tested ആണ്.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Idiom meaning = literal meaning അല്ല — always figurative!
• Food/Kitchen idioms group:
  - Spill the beans = reveal a secret
  - In a pickle = in trouble
  - Bite the bullet = endure pain bravely
  - Piece of cake = very easy
  - Bite off more than you can chew = take on too much
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: 'Spill the beans' PSC-ൽ repeatedly tested idiom.
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. She accidentally spilled the beans about the plan.
  2. Don't spill the beans — it's a surprise!
  3. He spilled the beans to the press.
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

(B) At the drop of a hat
• Meaning: instantly, without hesitation — ഒട്ടും ആലോചിക്കാതെ ഉടനെ ചെയ്യുക.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Common idiom ആണ്, students meaning confuse ചെയ്യും.

(C) A bolt from the blue
• Meaning: unexpected shock — ആകാശത്ത് നിന്ന് ഇടിവെട്ടുന്ന പോലെ unexpected surprise.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Dramatic sounding idiom, reveal/secret-മായി connect ആകും തോന്നും.

(D) Call a spade a spade
• Meaning: speak plainly/bluntly — truth directly പറയുക.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Speaking truth' feels similar to 'revealing secret' — but different nuance entirely.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: Beans are SECRET ingredients — spill ചെയ്താൽ recipe (secret) reveal ആകും!
• PSC Top Idioms: Spill the beans | Hit the nail on the head | Bite the bullet | Burn the midnight oil | Once in a blue moon | Break the ice | Let the cat out of the bag | A blessing in disguise | Beat around the bush | Cost an arm and a leg
• 'Spill the beans' = 'Let the cat out of the bag' — both mean reveal secret!

questionText: Find out the correctly spelt word:

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (D) Accommodation

• Correct spelling: A-C-C-O-M-M-O-D-A-T-I-O-N
• Double 'C' + Double 'M' — രണ്ടും double ആണ്!
• Latin origin: 'ac' (to) + 'commodus' (suitable) — 'accommodate' = suitable place provide ചെയ്യുക.
• PSC-ൽ ഏറ്റവും popular spelling question: 'accommodation'.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Accommodation = AC + COMM + ODATION
  Double C (cc) + Double M (mm) — both double!
• Etymology: Latin 'accommodare' = to make fit/suitable
  ac- (to) + com- (together) + modus (measure/way)
• Similar double-letter words (PSC favourite):
  - Recommend (1C + 2M) — note: only ONE 'c'!
  - Necessary (1C + 2S)
  - Occurrence (2C + 2R)
  - Committee (2M + 2T)
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Accommodation spelling PSC-ൽ multiple times appeared.
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. The hotel provides excellent accommodation.
  2. Accommodation in the city is expensive.
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

(A) Accomodation
• Only ONE 'm' — wrong! Double 'm' (mm) missing.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Double 'c' correct ആണ്, but 'm' single — half correct feel ആകും.

(B) Acommodation
• Only ONE 'c' — wrong! Double 'c' (cc) missing.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Double 'm' correct ആണ്, but 'c' single — again half correct.

(C) Accommadation
• 'a' instead of 'o' in middle — 'accommAdation' wrong, 'accommOdation' correct.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: All letters seem right but vowel 'a/o' confusion — subtle error.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'Accommodation has CC and MM — like a hotel room has 2 Chairs and 2 Mattresses!'
• Rule: ac-COMMO-dation — say it as 3 parts: ac + COMMO + dation
• PSC spelling trick: When in doubt between single/double — 'accommodation' ALWAYS double both (cc + mm).

questionText: The train __________ the station.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (A) Call at

• 'Call at' = (a place) visit ചെയ്യുക / stop at a place briefly.
• Train 'calls at' a station = station-ൽ നിർത്തുന്നു (stops briefly).
• Ships 'call at' ports, trains 'call at' stations — transport context-ൽ ഇത് standard usage.
• 'The train calls at Thrissur and Palakkad.' — official announcement style!
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: 'Call at' = visit/stop at a PLACE
• All 'CALL' phrasal verbs:
  - Call at = stop at a place (train/ship)
  - Call on = visit a person ('I called on my friend')
  - Call for = demand / collect ('Call for stricter rules')
  - Call off = cancel ('The match was called off')
  - Call out = shout / summon ('Call out the names')
  - Call up = phone / remember ('Call up your parents')
• Key distinction: Call AT (place) vs Call ON (person)
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Phrasal verbs with 'call' PSC favourite — especially call at/on/for/off.
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. The train calls at five stations.
  2. She called on her teacher yesterday.
  3. The event was called off due to rain.
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

(B) Call on
• 'Call on' = visit a person, not a place.
• 'I called on the doctor' ✓ (person), but 'the train called on the station' ✗.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Call on' vs 'Call at' — most common confusion PSC creates.

(C) Call for
• 'Call for' = demand or require ('This calls for celebration!').
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'call for' is a very common phrasal verb, random choose ചെയ്തേക്കും.

(D) Call out
• 'Call out' = shout loudly or expose someone.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Train-station context-ൽ 'call out' (announce) feel ആകും — but standard usage 'call at'.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'AT a place, ON a person' — Train stops AT station; You visit ON your friend!
• PSC pattern: Transport (train/ship) + station/port = always 'call at'.
• Learn ALL 'call' phrasal verbs together: at/on/for/off/out/up — one set, one session!

questionText: One who is devoted to the pleasure of eating and drinking:

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (D) Epicure

• 'Epicure' = ഭക്ഷണത്തിലും പാനീയത്തിലും ആനന്ദം കണ്ടെത്തുന്ന ആൾ — food & drink connoisseur.
• Named after Greek philosopher Epicurus who believed pleasure (especially simple pleasures like food) is the highest good.
• 'An epicure never settles for bad food!' — quality food lover.
• PSC context: Definition-based word — 'devoted to pleasure of eating and drinking = epicure'.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Epicure = Epic + ure → Epic food experience seeker!
• Etymology: Greek philosopher 'Epicurus' (341–270 BC) — pleasure = good life
• Related food/person words:
  - Epicure = lover of fine food & drink
  - Gourmet = expert judge of fine food
  - Gourmand = person who loves eating (slightly greedy connotation)
  - Vegetarian = one who does not eat meat
  - Cannibal = one who eats human flesh
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: One word substitutes PSC-ൽ ഏറ്റവും frequent section — Epicure often tested.
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. As an epicure, he only visited fine dining restaurants.
  2. An epicure knows the difference between good and great food.
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

(A) Fugitive
• 'Fugitive' = നിയമത്തിൽ നിന്ന് ഓടി രക്ഷപ്പെടുന്ന ആൾ — escaped criminal.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Difficult word — unfamiliar students confuse ചെയ്യും.

(B) Cynic
• 'Cynic' = ആരിലും വിശ്വാസമില്ലാത്ത ആൾ — one who believes people are motivated only by selfishness.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Greek philosopher connection (Cynics were a Greek school like Epicurus) — PSC confuses!

(C) Vagabond
• 'Vagabond' = ഒരു ഇടത്ത് നിൽക്കാതെ അലഞ്ഞുതിരിയുന്ന ആൾ — wanderer with no fixed home.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Sounds like a person-type word, but meaning ഭക്ഷണവുമായി ബന്ധമില്ല.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'EPIC food = EPICURE' — epic-level food experience enjoy ചെയ്യുന്ന ആൾ!
• Must-learn One Word Substitutes for PSC:
  Epicure (food lover) | Bibliophile (book lover) | Philatelist (stamp collector) | Ornithologist (bird scientist) | Insomniac (cannot sleep) | Teetotaller (doesn't drink alcohol)
• PSC strategy: Definition ശ്രദ്ധിക്കുക — 'devoted to PLEASURE of eating and drinking' → pleasure seeker of food = epicure.

questionText: What is the right ending of the sentence? If I had known you were here,
optionD: I would have been met you.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (C) I would have met you.

• 'If I had known you were here' — ഇത് Third Conditional ആണ്. ഭൂതകാലത്തിൽ സംഭവിക്കാതെ പോയ ഒരു കൂടിക്കാഴ്ചയെ കുറിച്ചുള്ള regret — time machine-ൽ തിരികെ പോകാൻ കഴിഞ്ഞിരുന്നെങ്കിൽ!
• If + Past Perfect (had known) → would have + Past Participle (met) — ഈ formula match ചെയ്യുന്നത് option C മാത്രം!
• 'met' = 'meet'-ന്റെ Past Participle (V3). 'would have met' = കണ്ടേനേ (but didn't happen — missed chance).
• PSC exam-ൽ ഇതേ question structure ആവർത്തിക്കും — formula ഒരിക്കൽ ഓർത്താൽ guaranteed mark!
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: If + Past Perfect (had + V3) → would have + Past Participle (V3)
• Third Conditional = ഭൂതകാലത്തിലെ unreal/impossible situation + imagined result
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Third Conditional PSC-ൽ 6+ papers-ൽ ആവർത്തിച്ചു ചോദിച്ചിട്ടുണ്ട്!

Conditional types ഒന്നിച്ച് ഓർക്കൂ:
• First: If + Present Simple → will + V1 (real future)
• Second: If + Past Simple → would + V1 (unreal present)
• Third: If + Past Perfect → would have + V3 (unreal past — regret)

ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
1. If I had studied hard, I would have passed the PSC exam. (regret!)
2. If she had known the answer, she would have written it.
3. If they had left early, they would have caught the train.
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) I would have meet you.
• 'meet' = base form (V1). would have-ന് ശേഷം Past Participle (V3 = met) ആണ് വേണ്ടത്.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'would have meet' PSC-ൽ ഇടാറുണ്ട് — V1 vs V3 confuse ആകുന്ന students-ക്ക് ഇത് classic trap!

❌ (B) I would meet you.
• 'would meet' = Second Conditional main clause ആണ്, Third Conditional-ൽ wrong.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'would + V1' correct pattern — പക്ഷേ Second Conditional-ൽ. 'had known' Past Perfect ആണ്, അതിന് 'would have + V3' pair ആകണം!

❌ (D) I would have been met you.
• 'would have been met' = Passive construction. Active sentence-ൽ 'been' ഒരിക്കലും ഇല്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'been' add ചെയ്ത് passive feel കൊടുക്കുന്ന option — 'I' active subject ആണ്, passive form ഒരിക്കലും correct ഇല്ല!
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'Time machine regret' — 'If I had a time machine, I would have met you!' — Third Conditional = past regret = time machine feeling! ഈ image ഓർത്തിരുന്നാൽ formula ഒരിക്കലും miss ആകില്ല!
• 🔑 Quick Formula: If + had + V3 → would have + V3 — ഈ ഒരൊറ്റ formula ഓർത്താൽ Third Conditional guaranteed!
• PSC strategy: Options-ൽ 'would have meet' (V1) കണ്ടാൽ ഉടനെ eliminate — would have-ന് ശേഷം V1 ഒരിക്കലും ഇല്ല!

questionText: 'My uncle offered me a gift' can be rewritten without a change in meaning as
optionA: A gift was offered to me by my uncle
optionB: I am offered a gift by my uncle
optionC: A gift is offered to me by my uncle
optionD: A gift offered to me by my uncle

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (A) A gift was offered to me by my uncle.

• Original: 'My uncle offered me a gift' — Simple Past, Active Voice.
• Passive Voice-ലേക്ക് മാറ്റുമ്പോൾ tense maintain ആകണം — Simple Past Active → Simple Past Passive.
• 🔑 Simple Past Passive formula: was/were + Past Participle — 'offered' → 'was offered' ✅
• 'a gift' (object) → Subject ആകുന്നു. 'me' → 'to me' ആകുന്നു. Tense change ഇല്ല, meaning change ഇല്ല — exactly right!
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Active tense = Passive tense — tense ഒരിക്കലും change ആകരുത്!
  Simple Present: offers → is/are offered
  Simple Past: offered → was/were offered
  Present Perfect: has offered → has been offered
  Future: will offer → will be offered

• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Double object passive (offer, give, send, show) PSC-ൽ ആവർത്തിക്കുന്ന topic!
  Active: My uncle offered me a gift.
  Passive 1: A gift was offered to me by my uncle. ✅
  Passive 2: I was offered a gift by my uncle. ✅
  (രണ്ടും correct — options-ൽ ഉള്ളത് choose ചെയ്യുക)

ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
1. The teacher gave us homework. → We were given homework by the teacher.
2. She sent him a letter. → A letter was sent to him by her.
3. They offered her the job. → She was offered the job by them.
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (B) I am offered a gift by my uncle.
• 'am offered' = Present Passive. Original sentence Simple Past (offered) ആണ്.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Passive form correct (am offered) — പക്ഷേ tense wrong! Past → Past ആകണം, Present ആകരുത്!

❌ (C) A gift is offered to me by my uncle.
• 'is offered' = Present Passive. Question 'without change in meaning' ആണ് — meaning includes tense!
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Sentence structure perfect, 'to me' correct — പക്ഷേ tense change ആയി, meaning change ആയി!

❌ (D) A gift offered to me by my uncle.
• 'was' missing — incomplete sentence. Passive-ൽ 'be' verb mandatory ആണ്.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'offered' alone verb-പോലെ feel ആകും — പക്ഷേ passive-ൽ auxiliary (was) ഇല്ലാതെ sentence complete ആകില്ല!
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'Passive = be + V3' — 'be' ഇല്ലാതെ passive ഇല്ല! 'A gift offered' കണ്ടാൽ 'be' missing — wrong! Be = was/is/are — ഇത് ഓർത്തിരുന്നാൽ option D ഉടനെ eliminate!
• ⭐ Golden rule: Active tense = Passive tense — Past active → Past passive, ALWAYS!
• PSC strategy: Options-ൽ tense ആദ്യം check ചെയ്യൂ — 'am offered / is offered' കണ്ടാൽ original 'offered' = Past, eliminate!

questionText: Which one of the following is incorrect?
optionC: I have staying Singapore for ten years
optionD: I have been staying in Singapore since 2010

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (C) I have staying Singapore for ten years.

• ഈ sentence-ൽ 2 errors ഉണ്ട് — ഇതാണ് ഏറ്റവും incorrect option!
• Error 1: 'have staying' — WRONG. Present Perfect Continuous-ൽ 'have been staying' ആണ് correct. 'been' = bridge, ഇത് missing!
• Error 2: 'in Singapore' missing — preposition 'in' ഇല്ലാതെ ഒരിക്കലും ഉപയോഗിക്കരുത്.
• Correct sentence: 'I have been staying IN Singapore for ten years.' — 2 errors ഒറ്റ option-ൽ = most incorrect!
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula — Tense selection guide:
  Present Perfect: have/has + V3 → experience, completed action
  Past Simple: V2 + specific time → yesterday, in 1996, last year
  Present Perfect Continuous: have/has + BEEN + V-ing → ongoing action

• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: 'for vs since' + tense PSC-ൽ ആവർത്തിക്കുന്ന topic!
  for = duration (ten years, two months) → how long?
  since = starting point (2010, Monday) → when did it start?
  Both → Present Perfect Continuous: have/has + been + V-ing

• ⭐ Time expression rule: Specific past time (in 1996, last week) → Past Simple, NEVER Present Perfect!

ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
1. I have visited Singapore. (experience — no specific time ✅)
2. I visited Singapore in 1996. (specific time → Past Simple ✅)
3. I have been staying in Singapore since 2010. (ongoing action ✅)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ — ഇവ correct sentences ആണ് (incorrect-നെ identify ചെയ്യണം)

❌ (A) I have visited Singapore — CORRECT sentence.
• Present Perfect (have visited) = ജീവിതത്തിൽ ഒരിക്കലെങ്കിലും visit ചെയ്ത experience — no time mention.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'have visited' — students-ന് doubt ആകും. പക്ഷേ experience + no specific time = Present Perfect correct!

❌ (B) I visited Singapore in 1996 — CORRECT sentence.
• 'in 1996' = specific past time → Past Simple (visited) — perfect rule follow!
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'visited' past tense correct — specific time + past tense = perfect match.

❌ (D) I have been staying in Singapore since 2010 — CORRECT sentence.
• 'since 2010' = starting point → Present Perfect Continuous (have been staying) — 'been' ഉണ്ട், correct!
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Option C-പോലെ ആണ് — പക്ഷേ 'been' ഉള്ളത് correct, ഇല്ലാത്ത C wrong!
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'BEEN = the bridge' — have + BEEN + V-ing. 'have staying' = bridge (been) ഇല്ലാതെ gap! Bridge ഓർത്തിരുന്നാൽ 'been' miss ആകില്ല!
• for = duration (how long = 10 years), since = starting point (when = 2010) — ഈ pair ഒരൊറ്റ rule, ഒരിക്കലും confuse ആകരുത്!
• PSC strategy: 'have + V-ing' ('been' ഇല്ലാതെ) കണ്ടാൽ ഉടനെ wrong — 'have BEEN + V-ing' ആണ് correct formula!

questionText: Which one of the following definitions matches with the word 'Fuddy-Duddy'?
optionA: A person who has old-fashioned ideas and habits
optionD: Modern and up to the minute

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (A) A person who has old-fashioned ideas and habits

• 'Fuddy-duddy' = ഴപ്പ്/പഴഞ്ചൻ ആളുകൾ — a person who is old-fashioned and resistant to change.
• A fuddy-duddy clings to old customs, ideas, and habits.
• Informal/colloquial British English: 'Don't be such a fuddy-duddy!'
• Similar terms: old fogey, stick-in-the-mud, traditionalist (informal).
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Fuddy-duddy = old-fashioned person (informal)
  Think: 'old-fashioned + set in their ways + disapproves of modern things'
• Word type: Informal/colloquial noun (reduplication pattern: fuddy-DUDDY)
• Related informal terms for old-fashioned people:
  fuddy-duddy = stick-in-the-mud = old fogey = square (slang) = dinosaur (informal)
• Antonym: trendy, modern, progressive, forward-thinking
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Unusual/informal vocabulary definition matching — PSC-ൽ occasionally tested.
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. My uncle is such a fuddy-duddy — he refuses to use a smartphone.
  2. Don't be a fuddy-duddy; try something new!
  3. She dismissed the proposal as coming from old fuddy-duddies.
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

(B) Cleverly amusing in tone
• 'Cleverly amusing' = 'witty' or 'facetious' — completely different from fuddy-duddy.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Fuddy-duddy sounds like a funny word → students connect it with 'amusing'.

(C) Someone who speaks frequently
• 'Speaks frequently' = 'loquacious' or 'talkative' — not related to fuddy-duddy.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Duddy' sounds like 'dud' = someone who talks a lot? Random phonetic association.

(D) Modern and up to the minute
• 'Modern' = ANTONYM of fuddy-duddy. A fuddy-duddy is the opposite of modern.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Students who don't know the word might guess a positive/modern meaning from the playful sound.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'FUDDY-DUDDY = OLD BUDDY who is MUDDY (stuck) in old ways!'
  fuddy + duddy rhyme = informal emphasis on being stubbornly old-fashioned.
• Association: Grandpa refusing to use WhatsApp = fuddy-duddy!
• PSC vocabulary: Definition-matching questions — read each definition carefully and match meaning, not sound.

questionText: The idiom 'Hit the nail on the head' means
optionA: To bring some variety in it
optionB: To do or say exactly the right thing

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (B) To do or say exactly the right thing.

• 'Hit the nail on the head' = കൃത്യം ശരി പറഞ്ഞു/ചെയ്തു — exactly right, perfect precision!
• Visual: Carpenter-നെ imagine ചെയ്യൂ — nail-ന്റെ head-ൽ precise ആയി hammer hit ചെയ്താൽ nail perfectly ഇറങ്ങും. Off-center ആയാൽ bent ആകും — that's the difference!
• Hit the nail ON THE HEAD — head-ൽ കൃത്യം hit = exactly right!
• PSC answer sheet-ൽ correct bubble mark ചെയ്യുമ്പോൾ: 'You hit the nail on the head!' 😊
explanation.rule:
