• Subject pronoun: 'your brother' = 'he'. So tag = 'doesn't he?' — perfect!
• 'Your brother plays well, doesn't he?' = 'നിന്റെ സഹോദരൻ നന്നായി കളിക്കുന്നു, അല്ലേ?'
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Simple Present singular (he/she/it + V+s) → tag: doesn't he/she/it?
  Simple Present plural (they/we/you + V) → tag: don't they/we/you?
  Simple Past → tag: didn't + subject?
• Positive main clause → Negative tag
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Question tags = PSC-ൽ ഒരു regular topic. Auxiliary matching ആണ് key.
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. She sings well, doesn't she? (singular — doesn't)
  2. They play cricket, don't they? (plural — don't)
  3. He went home, didn't he? (past — didn't)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

(A) Did he?
• 'Did he?' = Simple Past-ന്റെ tag — 'plays' Simple Present ആണ്, Simple Past അല്ല.
• Tense mismatch: 'plays' = present, 'did' = past.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Did' familiar tag — tense check ചെയ്യൂ. 'Plays' = present = 'does/doesn't'.

(B) Aren't he?
• 'Aren't he?' = grammatically wrong — 'he' singular-ന് 'aren't' ഉപയോഗിക്കില്ല.
• Plus 'plays' = action verb, 'aren't' = be verb — wrong type.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Aren't' familiar — 'he' singular-ന് 'aren't' grammatically impossible.

(C) Isn't he?
• 'Isn't he?' = be verb (is) negative — 'plays' = action verb, be verb tag wrong.
• Action verbs-ന് 'do/does/did' ആണ് tag auxiliary, 'is/isn't' അല്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: ഇതാണ് trickiest — 'he' = singular, 'isn't' = singular feel. 'Plays' action verb — 'doesn't' ആണ് correct!
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: PLAYS → DOES/DOESN'T! Action verb = DO/DOES/DID tag. BE verb = IS/AM/ARE tag.
• Two-step check:
  Step 1: Positive → Negative | Negative → Positive
  Step 2: Action verb → do/does/did | Be verb → is/am/are/was/were
• Exam strategy: 'plays/goes/runs' = action verbs = does/doesn't tag. Never 'isn't/aren't' for action verbs!

questionText: What do you mean by the idiom, 'a piece of cake'?

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (B) something that is very easy

• 'A piece of cake' = വളരെ എളുപ്പമായ കാര്യം — 'it's a piece of cake' means 'it's very easy'.
• Idiom-ൽ literal meaning (cake-ന്റെ ഒരു കഷ്ണം) ആകില്ല — figurative meaning ആണ് ഉദ്ദേശം.
• 'That exam was a piece of cake!' = 'ആ exam വളരെ easy ആയിരുന്നു!' — idiomatic usage.
• Cake easily cut ചെയ്ത് enjoy ചെയ്യാം — easy task = 'piece of cake' comparison ആണ് idiom-ന്റെ origin.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Food idioms = always figurative meaning — literal food meaning ഒരിക്കലും ആകില്ല!
• PSC-ൽ ഇടയ്ക്ക് ചോദിക്കുന്ന Food idioms:
  a piece of cake = very easy (വളരെ എളുപ്പം)
  a tough nut to crack = very difficult (വളരെ കഠിനം)
  spill the beans = reveal a secret (രഹസ്യം പറഞ്ഞുപോകുക)
  in a pickle = in trouble (കുഴപ്പത്തിൽ)
  bite the bullet = endure something painful
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Food idioms PSC-ൽ ഒരു regular topic ആണ്.
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. The test was a piece of cake for him. (easy ആയിരുന്നു)
  2. Learning to ride a bike is a piece of cake. (easy ആണ്)
  3. For her, cooking is a piece of cake. (easy ആണ്)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

(A) a tasty snack
• Literal food meaning — idiom-ൽ literal meaning ഒരിക്കലും ആകില്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Cake' = food word, students literal meaning pick ചെയ്തേക്കും.

(C) which is soft
• Cake-ന്റെ texture — literal interpretation ആണ്.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Cake is soft — physical description pick ചെയ്തേക്കും. Figurative = easy task.

(D) a small piece of cake
• Literal size description — idiom = figurative meaning.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Direct translation trap — 'piece of cake' literal ആകില്ല, 'easy' ആണ് meaning.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: PIECE OF CAKE = EASY to eat = EASY to do!
• Difficulty idioms pair:
  🎂 piece of cake = very EASY
  🌰 tough nut to crack = very DIFFICULT
• Exam strategy: Food idiom = literal meaning automatic wrong. Figurative meaning ആണ് always answer!

questionText: Pick the word that is opposite in meaning to the word 'profound'

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (B) Superficial

• 'Profound' = ആഴമേറിയ, deep, thorough — ഇതിന്റെ antonym (opposite) ആണ് ചോദിക്കുന്നത്.
• 'Superficial' = ഉപരിതലം, shallow, surface-level — 'profound'-ന്റെ exact opposite ആണ്.
• Profound (deep) ↔ Superficial (surface/shallow) — perfect antonym pair.
• 'A profound understanding' = 'ആഴത്തിലുള്ള ഗ്രാഹ്യം' — 'superficial understanding' = 'ഉപരിതലത്തിലുള്ള ഗ്രാഹ്യം'.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Antonym question-ൽ synonyms trap ആയി വരും!
• 'Profound' synonym family:
  profound = deep = intense = thorough = far-reaching
• 'Profound'-ന്റെ antonyms:
  superficial = shallow = surface-level = trivial
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Profound/Superficial antonym pair PSC vocabulary-ൽ ഒരു classic ആണ്.
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. She has a profound knowledge of history. (ആഴത്തിലുള്ള knowledge)
  2. His understanding was superficial, not deep. (ഉപരിതലം)
  3. The impact was profound, not superficial.
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

(A) Intense
• 'Intense' = ശക്തമായ — 'profound'-ന്റെ synonym ആണ്, antonym അല്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Intense' powerful word ആണ്, opposite ആണ് എന്ന് feel ആകും — synonym ആണ്!

(C) Deep
• 'Deep' = 'profound'-ന്റെ near-synonym ആണ്. 'Profound' itself means 'deep'.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: ഇതാണ് ഏറ്റവും common trap — 'deep' = 'profound' same meaning.

(D) Complete
• 'Complete' = പൂർണ്ണം — profound-ന്റെ antonym അല്ല, unrelated meaning.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Complete' formal word — meaning relation ഇല്ല.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: PROFOUND = deep in the ground. SUPERFICIAL = SUPER (on top) = SURFACE LEVEL!
• Classic PSC antonym pairs:
  profound ↔ superficial | deep ↔ shallow | complex ↔ simple
• Exam strategy: 'profound' = 'deep/intense' synonyms eliminate ചെയ്യൂ, opposite meaning ആണ് answer!

questionText: Find the correct tense form: By the end of this week I ________ here for ten years.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (A) will have worked

• 'By the end of this week' = ഒരു future deadline ആണ്. ഈ week-ന്റെ അവസാനത്തോടെ action complete ആകും.
• Future Perfect tense = 'will have + V3' — future-ൽ ഒരു specific time-ന് മുൻപ് complete ആകുന്ന action.
• 'By the end of this week I will have worked here for ten years' = 'ഈ ആഴ്ചയുടെ അവസാനത്തോടെ ഞാൻ ഇവിടെ 10 വർഷം work ചെയ്തിരിക്കും'.
• Future Perfect time markers: by, by the time, by then, by tomorrow — ഇവ 'will have + V3' indicate ചെയ്യുന്നു.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: BY + future time → WILL HAVE + V3 (Future Perfect)
  by tomorrow = will have done
  by next year = will have completed
  by the end of this week = will have worked
• Future Perfect = future-ൽ ഒരു point-ൽ action already complete ആകും.
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Future Perfect tense 'by' time marker question PSC-ൽ ഇടയ്ക്ക് ചോദിക്കുന്നു.
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. By tomorrow, she will have finished the project.
  2. By 2030, they will have built the bridge.
  3. By next month, I will have saved enough money.
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

(B) had have worked
• 'Had have worked' = grammatically impossible — 'had' + 'have' ഒരുമിച്ച് use ആകില്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Had' = past perfect feel — grammatically invalid structure.

(C) may have worked
• 'May have worked' = past possibility — context = certain future, 'may' = possibility only.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'May have' modal perfect ആണ് — certainty vs possibility difference ഓർക്കൂ.

(D) could have worked
• 'Could have worked' = past ability — future context-ൽ wrong.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Modal + have + V3 correct ആണ് — 'by' = future = 'will have' ആണ്.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: BY = Before Year/time → WILL HAVE done!
• Time marker key:
  BY (future deadline) → will have + V3
  SINCE (starting point) → have been + V-ing
  FOR (duration) → have been + V-ing
• Exam strategy: 'By the end of / by tomorrow / by next week' = Future Perfect = 'will have + V3'. Automatic answer!

questionText: What do you mean by the term 'magnum opus'?

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (C) a great work

• 'Magnum opus' = ഒരു കലാകാരൻ, എഴുത്തുകാരൻ, composer-ന്റെ greatest work/masterpiece ആണ്.
• Latin: 'magnum' = great/large + 'opus' = work. Magnum opus = the great work.
• 'Beethoven's magnum opus is his Ninth Symphony' — greatest creative work.
• Artists, writers ഇവരുടെ lifetime-ലെ greatest creation-ന് 'magnum opus' ഉപയോഗിക്കുന്നു.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: magnum = great/large | opus = work → magnum opus = great work/masterpiece
• PSC-ൽ ഇടയ്ക്ക് ചോദിക്കുന്ന Latin phrases:
  magnum opus = greatest work
  alma mater = school/college one attended
  status quo = existing state of affairs
  bona fide = genuine
  prima facie = at first sight
  carte blanche = full freedom
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Latin phrases PSC-ൽ ഒരു regular topic.
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. 'War and Peace' is Tolstoy's magnum opus.
  2. The painting is the artist's magnum opus.
  3. This novel is her magnum opus.
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

(A) very wealthy
• 'Magnum' = great/large, wealth-ൽ ഒരു ബന്ധവും ഇല്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Magnum' = large → wealthy feel — work/creation ആണ്, wealth അല്ല.

(B) something very expensive
• 'Opus' = work, price-ൽ ഒരു ബന്ധവും ഇല്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Magnum' brand (expensive) → expensive feel — Latin meaning ആണ് important.

(D) very little
• 'Magnum' = great/large-ന്റെ opposite meaning ആണ്.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Easy eliminate — 'magnum' = large, 'very little' = opposite.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: MAGNUM = MAGNIFICENT! OPUS = OPERATION (work)! MAGNUM OPUS = MAGNIFICENT WORK!
• Latin: magnum = big/great (Magna Carta = Great Charter!)
• PSC Latin phrases — 5 must-know:
  magnum opus = great work | alma mater = one's school | status quo = current state | bona fide = genuine | prima facie = at first sight

questionText: What do you call someone who is a beginner in any profession?

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (B) novice

• 'A beginner in any profession' = പുതുതായി ഒരു profession-ൽ ആരംഭിക്കുന്ന ആൾ — experience ഇല്ലാത്ത ആൾ.
• 'Novice' = beginner, a person new to a skill or activity.
• 'He is a novice in photography' = 'photography-യിൽ ഒരു beginner ആണ്'.
• Other options = experienced people: veteran/adept/ace = experienced, skilled.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Experience level words:
  novice = beginner (experience ഇല്ല)
  apprentice = learning under a master
  veteran = long experience
  adept = highly skilled
  ace = expert, the best
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: One word substitutes — experience words PSC favourite.
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. She is a novice at chess but learning fast.
  2. The veteran doctor has 30 years of experience.
  3. He is an adept programmer.
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

(A) veteran
• 'Veteran' = ദീർഘ experience ഉള്ള ആൾ — 'beginner'-ന്റെ exact opposite.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Veteran' impressive word — 'beginner' ആണ് ചോദിച്ചത്, opposite pick ചെയ്യരുത്.

(C) adept
• 'Adept' = highly skilled — 'beginner' opposite.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Adept' uncommon word — 'skilled' ആണ് meaning, 'beginner' അല്ല.

(D) ace
• 'Ace' = the best, expert — 'beginner'-ന്റെ opposite.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Ace' = expert, 'Beginner' = novice — opposite concepts.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: NOVICE = NO VICE (no experience!) — just a beginner!
• Experience scale (lowest → highest):
  🟢 novice → apprentice → adept → veteran → ace (the best)
• Exam strategy: 'Beginner/new to profession' = novice. Veterans/adept/ace = experienced. Question asks beginner = novice!

questionText:
In the following group of words, all but one are synonyms. Find the odd one out:
probing, groping, driving, fumbling

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (D) driving

• ഈ question-ൽ 4 words കൊടുത്തിട്ടുണ്ട് — ഒന്ന് ഒഴികെ ബാക്കി 3 synonyms ആണ്. Odd one out ആണ് ചോദിക്കുന്നത്.
• probing = ആഴത്തിൽ തിരയുക, investigate carefully; groping = ഇരുട്ടിൽ തപ്പിത്തടയുക, search blindly; fumbling = awkwardly search/handle — ഈ മൂന്നും 'searching uncertainly' = ഒരേ idea.
• driving = ഒരു vehicle ഓടിക്കുക, forcefully pushing — ഇത് 'searching' concept-ന്റെ ഒരു ബന്ധവും ഇല്ല.
• probing, groping, fumbling = uncertain searching-ന്റെ synonyms. 'Driving' = completely different meaning — ഇത് odd one out.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Odd one out = theme/category identify ചെയ്യൂ → ആ theme-ൽ fit ആകാത്ത word = answer.
• ഈ question-ൽ theme = 'searching/feeling around uncertainly':
  probing = investigating, searching deeply (ആഴത്തിൽ explore ചെയ്യുക)
  groping = feeling around in the dark (ഇരുട്ടിൽ തപ്പുക)
  fumbling = handling or searching clumsily (awkwardly search)
  driving = operating a vehicle / pushing forward (completely different!)
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Odd one out / synonym group questions PSC-ൽ ഒരു popular vocabulary format ആണ്.
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. He was groping in the dark for the light switch. (ഇരുട്ടിൽ switch തപ്പി)
  2. She was fumbling with the keys. (keys awkwardly handle ചെയ്തു)
  3. The detective was probing into the mystery. (mystery investigate ചെയ്തു)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

(A) groping
• 'Groping' = ഇരുട്ടിൽ feel ചെയ്ത് തിരയുക — probing, fumbling-ന്റെ synonym ആണ്. Odd one out അല്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Groping' = uncommon word, students unfamiliar — meaning ഓർക്കൂ: 'searching blindly'.

(B) fumbling
• 'Fumbling' = awkwardly handling or searching — probing, groping-ന്റെ synonym. Group-ൽ ഉള്ള member.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Fumbling' less common — meaning = 'clumsy searching', group-ൽ ഉള്ളതാണ്.

(C) probing
• 'Probing' = deeply investigating/searching — synonyms group-ൽ ഉള്ള member ആണ്.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Probing' = technical word feel — 'searching/investigating' meaning, group-ൽ ഉള്ളതാണ്.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: GROPE + FUMBLE + PROBE = all mean 'search around' (G-F-P = Group Feeling & Probing!). DRIVE = go forward = completely different!
• Odd one out strategy:
  Step 1: Common theme identify ചെയ്യൂ (searching)
  Step 2: ആ theme-ൽ fit ആകാത്ത word = odd one out (driving)
• Exam strategy: 3 words-ന് common meaning ഉണ്ടെങ്കിൽ 4th word = odd one out. 'driving' = only word unrelated to searching!

questionText:
Choose the right correlative from the options given below and complete the following sentence:
Gardening is a good hobby, _________ it helps to relieve stress.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (B) moreover

• 'Gardening is a good hobby, _________ it helps to relieve stress.' — രണ്ട് clauses-ഉം POSITIVE ideas ആണ്: hobby good + stress relieve ചെയ്യും.
• 'Moreover' = കൂടാതെ, in addition, furthermore — ഒരു positive idea-ന് ശേഷം additional positive reason add ചെയ്യുമ്പോൾ ഉപയോഗിക്കുന്നു.
• 'Gardening is a good hobby, moreover it helps to relieve stress' = 'Gardening ഒരു നല്ല hobby ആണ്, കൂടാതെ stress-ഉം കുറയ്ക്കും' — perfect logical flow.
• ബാക്കി options (however, on the contrary, whereas) = contrast connectors — positive + positive combination-ൽ ഉപയോഗിക്കില്ല.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula:
  MOREOVER / FURTHERMORE / BESIDES = additional point add ചെയ്യുമ്പോൾ (same direction)
  HOWEVER / ON THE CONTRARY / WHEREAS = contrast/opposite idea (different direction)
• ഈ sentence-ൽ: hobby good → stress relieve = same direction (both positive) → 'moreover'
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Connectors/conjunctions questions PSC-ൽ ഇടയ്ക്ക് ചോദിക്കുന്ന topic.
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. She is intelligent, moreover she works hard. (both positive)
  2. He is rich, however he is not happy. (contrast)
  3. I like tea, whereas she prefers coffee. (contrast in preference)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

(A) however
• 'However' = contrast connector — both ideas positive ആണ്, contrast connector wrong.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'However' familiar — sentence direction (same/contrast) check ചെയ്യൂ.

(C) on the contrary
• 'On the contrary' = opposite idea — both clauses positive, contrast connector wrong.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Formal sounding — contrast meaning ഓർക്കൂ, ഇവിടെ fit ആകില്ല.

(D) whereas
• 'Whereas' = contrast between two different things — same direction ideas-ൽ use ആകില്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Whereas' sophisticated — contrast word, ഈ sentence-ൽ contrast ഇല്ല.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: MOREOVER = MORE + OVER = MORE reasons on top! Adding more good reasons = moreover!
• Direction test:
  Same direction (both good) → moreover, furthermore, besides, also
  Different direction (contrast) → however, but, whereas, on the contrary
• Exam strategy: Two clauses read ചെയ്ത് — same direction ആണോ opposite ആണോ check. Both positive = 'moreover'!

questionText:
Replace the underlined words with one word in the following sentence:
When I met my friend after several years, he/she spoke to me __with an unsatisfied desire__ about our good old school days.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (A) wistfully

• Underlined phrase: 'with an unsatisfied desire' — ഒരു ആഗ്രഹം നിറവേറ്റപ്പെടാതെ പോയ feeling, past-നെക്കുറിച്ചുള്ള nostalgic longing ആണ്.
• 'Wistfully' = with a feeling of longing or regret for something in the past — 'unsatisfied desire' = wistful feeling ആണ്. ഈ ഒരൊറ്റ word ആ phrase-നെ replace ചെയ്യും.
• Context clues: 'met after several years' + 'good old school days' — ഇത് past-നെക്കുറിച്ചുള്ള nostalgia ആണ്, wistful feeling-ന്റെ perfect context.
• 'He spoke wistfully about their school days' = 'school days-നെക്കുറിച്ച് nostalgic longing-ഓടെ സംസാരിച്ചു' — ഒരൊറ്റ adverb ആ whole phrase replace ചെയ്യുന്നത് കാണൂ!
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: 'with an unsatisfied desire / longing for the past' → WISTFULLY
• Wistful = past-ൽ ഉണ്ടായിരുന്ന ഒരു ആഗ്രഹം നിറവേറ്റപ്പെടാതെ പോകുമ്പോൾ ഉണ്ടാകുന്ന feeling — nostalgic + a little sad
• Etymology hint: 'Wistful' = Old English 'wistly' (intently) + influenced by 'wishful' — literally 'full of unfulfilled wishes'!
• Word family: wistful (adjective) → wistfully (adverb) → wistfulness (noun)
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: One word substitutes for emotions/adverbs PSC-ൽ popular topic — wistfully, nonchalantly, surreptitiously ഒക്കെ ഇടയ്ക്ക് ചോദിക്കുന്നു.
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. She looked wistfully at her old photographs. (old photos കണ്ട് nostalgic ആയി)
  2. He spoke wistfully of his village days. (village life ഓർത്ത് longing-ഓടെ)
  3. The old man smiled wistfully, remembering his youth. (youth ഓർത്ത് sad-but-sweet smile)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

(B) deceitfully
• 'Deceitfully' = ചതിയോടെ, dishonestly — deceive ചെയ്യുന്ന manner.
• Context-ൽ deceit ഒന്നുമില്ല — old friend-നോട് honest nostalgia ആണ്. 'Unsatisfied desire' = deceit അല്ല, longing ആണ്.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: '-fully' ending ഉള്ള adverb — students unfamiliar with 'wistfully' ആണെങ്കിൽ 'deceitfully' pick ചെയ്തേക്കും. Meaning check ചെയ്യൂ!

(C) carefully
• 'Carefully' = ശ്രദ്ധയോടെ, with attention and caution.
• 'Unsatisfied desire' = carefulness അല്ല. Attention/caution-ന്റെ idea ഇവിടെ ഇല്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Most familiar '-fully' adverb — students 'carefully' automatically pick ചെയ്യും. Context: nostalgia, not carefulness!

(D) faithfully
• 'Faithfully' = വിശ്വസ്തതയോടെ, with loyalty and dedication.
• 'Unsatisfied desire' = faithfulness അല്ല. Loyalty/dedication-ന്റെ idea ഇവിടെ ഇല്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Positive emotion word — students 'faithfully' positive ആണ്, 'wistfully' unfamiliar ആണ് എന്ന് കരുതി pick ചെയ്യും.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: WISTFUL = WISH + FULL = ഉള്ളം wishes-കൊണ്ട് നിറഞ്ഞത്, പക്ഷേ ആ wishes നിറവേറ്റാൻ കഴിയില്ല! Past-നെക്കുറിച്ചുള്ള unfulfilled wishes = WISTFUL!
• Word family to learn: wistful → wistfully → wistfulness (PSC-ൽ ഏത് form-ലും ചോദിക്കാം)
• PSC exam strategy: 'Replace the underlined phrase with ONE WORD' question-ൽ — underlined phrase-ന്റെ exact meaning ആണ് answer. 'With an unsatisfied desire' = longing + unfulfilled = wistfully. Other options ആ meaning convey ചെയ്യുന്നില്ല!

questionText:
Add the right question tag to the following statement to make it a question:
Some of us were not ready to go to the concert, __________?

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (C) were we?

• 'Some of us were not ready to go to the concert' — 'were not' = negative past (be verb).
• Negative statement → Positive tag. 'Were not' negative → 'were' positive tag ആകണം.
• Subject: 'some of us' → tag-ൽ 'we' ആണ് pronoun. 'Us' ഒരിക്കലും tag subject ആകില്ല — 'we' ആകും.
• Auxiliary: 'were' = past be verb → tag auxiliary = 'were'. 'Were we?' = correct positive tag!
• 'Some of us were not ready, were we?' = 'നമ്മളിൽ ചിലർ concert-ന് ready ആയിരുന്നില്ല, അല്ലേ?'
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula:
  'Some of us' → tag subject = 'we' (object form 'us' tag-ൽ ഉപയോഗിക്കില്ല!)
  Negative statement (were not) → Positive tag (were)
  Be verb (were) → tag-ൽ same be verb (were)
• Pronoun rule: 'some of us / all of us / most of us' → tag = 'we'
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Question tags with 'some of us/all of us' PSC-ൽ tricky pattern.
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. Some of us were tired, were we?
  2. All of us are ready, aren't we?
  3. None of us could attend, could we?
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

(A) weren't us?
• Double mistakes: 'weren't' = negative (negative statement needs positive tag) + 'us' = wrong pronoun.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Weren't' sounds natural with 'were not' — negative + negative = wrong! Plus 'us' = object, not subject.

(B) wasn't we?
• 'Wasn't' = singular (was not). 'Were' = plural — tag must match 'were', not 'wasn't'.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Wasn't' familiar negative — tense/number match check. 'Were' ആണ് match.

(D) isn't us?
• 'Isn't' = present tense (is not). 'Were' = past — tense mismatch + 'us' = wrong pronoun.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Isn't us' familiar — past sentence-ൽ present tag ആകില്ല.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'Some of US' → tag = 'WE' (US = object, WE = subject in tags!)
• Three-step tag formula:
  Step 1: Negative → Positive | Positive → Negative
  Step 2: Same auxiliary (were → were)
  Step 3: Subject pronoun (some of us → we, not us!)
• Exam strategy: 'Some/all/most of us' → tag = 'we'. 'Us' option = automatic wrong!

questionText:
Complete the following sentence with the right phrasal verb. Choose your answer from the options given below:
It is difficult to ____________ your good name once you have lost it.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (C) get back

• 'It is difficult to ____________ your good name once you have lost it.' — 'lost it' = നഷ്ടപ്പെട്ടത്. Lost ആയ reputation recover/reclaim ചെയ്യുക = context.
• 'Get back' = recover, reclaim, regain — 'get back your good name' = 'നിന്റെ നല്ല പേര് തിരിച്ചെടുക്കുക'.
• 'It is difficult to get back your good name' = 'reputation നഷ്ടപ്പെട്ടാൽ തിരിച്ചെടുക്കാൻ പ്രയാസമാണ്' — perfect meaning.
• 'Get back' = retrieve something that was lost — exactly what the sentence requires.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: GET + BACK = recover/reclaim something lost
• GET phrasal verbs:
  get back = recover, regain (നഷ്ടപ്പെട്ടത് തിരിച്ചെടുക്കുക)
  get down = depress/write down (depressed ആകുക)
  get about = move around / news spreads
  get on = progress / board a vehicle
  get over = recover from illness/shock
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Phrasal verb questions PSC-ൽ ഒരു regular topic.
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. I want to get back my stolen watch.
  2. She got back her confidence after the setback.
  3. Can you get back the money you lent him?
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

(A) get down
• 'Get down' = depressed ആകുക / write down — 'get down your good name' = meaning ഇല്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Get' prefix familiar — 'down' meaning (depress/descend) reputation recover-ൽ wrong.

(B) go off
• 'Go off' = explode / alarm ring / food spoil — reputation recover context-ൽ ഒരു ബന്ധവും ഇല്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Go' common verb — 'go off' meanings (explode/spoil) reputation-ൽ apply ആകില്ല.

(D) get about
• 'Get about' = move around / news spread — reputation reclaim = different concept.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Get about' = movement/spreading meaning, reputation recovery അല്ല.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: GET BACK = go BACK to get what you lost! LOST reputation → go BACK to get it = GET BACK!
• Context clue: 'once you have LOST it' → need to RECOVER it = GET BACK!
• Exam strategy: 'Lost something → recover' = GET BACK. Get down (depress), get about (move), get over (recover from illness) — different meanings!

questionText:
Change the following sentence from Active to Passive. Choose your answer from the given options:
Rash driving causes many accidents.
optionA: Many accidents are caused by rash driving
optionB: Many accidents will be caused by rash driving
optionC: Many accidents would be caused by rash driving
optionD: Many accidents would have been caused by rash driving

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (A) Many accidents are caused by rash driving

• Active: 'Rash driving causes many accidents.' — 'causes' = Simple Present tense, singular.
• Active → Passive formula (Simple Present):
  Subject + V(s/es) + Object → Object + is/are + V3 + by + Subject
• 'Many accidents' = plural object → 'are' (plural be verb). 'Causes' → V3 = 'caused'.
• 'Many accidents are caused by rash driving' — Simple Present Passive. Tense, subject, object, V3 ഒക്കെ correct.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Active → Passive Tense Changes:
  Simple Present (V/Vs) → is/am/are + V3
  Simple Past (V2) → was/were + V3
  Simple Future (will + V) → will be + V3
  Present Perfect (has/have + V3) → has/have been + V3
• 'Causes' (Simple Present) → 'are caused' (plural = many accidents)
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Active-Passive transformation PSC-ൽ ഒരു regular question type.
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. She writes letters. → Letters are written by her.
  2. He caused the accident. → The accident was caused by him.
  3. They will complete the project. → The project will be completed by them.
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

(B) Many accidents will be caused by rash driving
• 'Will be caused' = Simple Future Passive — 'causes' = Simple Present, not future.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Will be' modal passive — active tense check ചെയ്യൂ. 'Causes' = present = 'are caused'.

(C) Many accidents would be caused by rash driving
• 'Would be caused' = conditional passive — 'causes' Simple Present ആണ്, conditional അല്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Would be' sophisticated — active 'causes' = simple present only.

(D) Many accidents would have been caused by rash driving
• 'Would have been caused' = conditional perfect passive — 'causes' Simple Present-ന് ഒരു ബന്ധവും ഇല്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Complex structure impressive — simple 'causes' → simple 'are caused' ആണ്.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: CAUSES (present) → ARE CAUSED (present passive)! Same tense, just flip!
• Active tense = Passive tense match:
  causes (S. Present) → are caused ✓
  caused (S. Past) → was/were caused
  will cause → will be caused
• Exam strategy: Active verb tense identify → same tense passive pick. 'Causes' = present = 'are caused' only!

questionText:
Fill in the blanks with the correct tense form of the verb given in brackets:
If you had gone to the fest, you __________ (able) to see the magic show.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (D) would have been able

• 'If you had gone to the fest' — if-clause-ൽ 'had gone' = Past Perfect. ഇത് Third Conditional ആണ്.
• Third Conditional = ഭൂതകാലത്ത് ഉണ്ടാകാതിരുന്ന situation. 'Fest-ൽ പോയിരുന്നെങ്കിൽ' — പക്ഷേ പോയില്ല.
• Main clause formula: would have + V3. Verb: 'able' → 'been able' (be able → been able).
• 'Would have been able' = 'കാണാൻ കഴിഞ്ഞേനേ' — Third Conditional main clause perfect form.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: If + had + V3 → would have + V3 (Third Conditional)
  'Be able to' → past participle = 'been able to'
  Main clause: would have been able to
• Third Conditional table:
  If-clause: If + had + V3 (past perfect)
  Main clause: would have + V3 (perfect modal)
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Third Conditional PSC-ൽ ഏറ്റവും ഇടയ്ക്ക് ചോദിക്കുന്ന conditional type.
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. If I had studied, I would have been able to pass.
  2. If she had come, she would have been able to meet him.
  3. If they had tried, they would have been able to win.
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

(A) will be able
• 'Will be able' = First Conditional (future) — 'had gone' = Third Conditional. Type mismatch.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Will be able' common future expression — 'had gone' = Third Conditional only.

(B) would be able
• 'Would be able' = Second Conditional (present imaginary) — 'had gone' = Third (past).
• 🪤 PSC Trap: ഇതാണ് most common trap — 'would be able' vs 'would HAVE been able'. 'Had' if-clause = 'would HAVE' main clause!

(C) will have been able
• 'Will have been able' = Future Perfect — Conditional-ൽ 'will' ഉപയോഗിക്കില്ല, 'would' ആണ്.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Will have been' complex — Conditional = 'would' (not 'will').
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: HAD in IF → WOULD HAVE in result! 'Had gone' → 'would have been able'!
• 'Be able to' V3 = 'been able to' — ഓർക്കൂ!
• Exam strategy: If-clause-ൽ 'had' = Third Conditional = main clause = 'would have + V3'. 'Would be able' (B) = missing 'have' = wrong!

questionText:
He was __trying to convince her, but it was pointless__.
In the above sentence, the underlined words can be replaced by an idiomatic-expression. Choose the appropriate idiom from the list given below:
optionD: hitting the nail on the head

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (C) beating a dead horse

• Underlined phrase: 'trying to convince her, but it was pointless' — ഒരു effort ആണ്, പക്ഷേ ആ effort futile/pointless ആണ്, result ഒന്നും ഉണ്ടാകില്ല.
• 'Beating a dead horse' = dead horse-നെ beat ചെയ്ത് ഓടിക്കാൻ try ചെയ്യുന്നത് — dead ആണ്, ഒരിക്കലും result ഇല്ല. ഇത് pointless effort-ന്റെ perfect idiom.
• 'He was beating a dead horse' = 'ഒരു ഫലവും ഇല്ലാത്ത ശ്രമം ചെയ്തുകൊണ്ടിരുന്നു' — underlined phrase exactly replace ആകുന്നത് കാണൂ!
• Context: convince try ചെയ്തു (effort) + pointless (no result) = futile effort = beating a dead horse. ഈ idiom-ൽ ഈ exact meaning ഉണ്ട്.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Pointless/futile effort = BEATING A DEAD HORSE
• Idiom meaning: Dead horse-നെ beat ചെയ്ത് ഓടിക്കാൻ try ചെയ്യുക — dead ആയ horse ഒരിക്കലും ഓടില്ല. അതുപോലെ ഒരു result ഒരിക്കലും വരില്ലാത്ത effort ചെയ്യുന്നത്.
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Idiom questions PSC-ൽ regular topic — 'beating' idioms confuse ചെയ്യാൻ ഇടയ്ക്ക് ഉപയോഗിക്കുന്നു.
• Related idioms group (PSC-ൽ ഇടയ്ക്ക് ചോദിക്കുന്ന action idioms):
  beating a dead horse = pointless effort (ഫലഹീനമായ ശ്രമം)
  beating around the bush = avoiding the main point (indirectly സംസാരിക്കുക)
  hitting the nail on the head = exactly correct (കൃത്യമായി ശരി)
  cutting the long story short = summarizing (ചുരുക്കി പറയുക)
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. Arguing with him is beating a dead horse — he never changes his mind.
  2. Stop beating around the bush and tell me directly!
  3. She hit the nail on the head with her analysis.
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

(A) beating around the bush
• 'Beating around the bush' = main point avoid ചെയ്ത് indirectly സംസാരിക്കുക — directly പറയാതെ ചുറ്റിവളഞ്ഞ് പോകുന്നത്.
• Context-ൽ he WAS trying to convince (direct attempt) — avoiding/indirect behavior ഇല്ല. Pointless effort ആണ് ഉദ്ദേശം, not indirectness.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Beating' = correct answer-ലും ഉള്ള same word — students 'beating' കണ്ട് confuse ആകും. Bush = indirect, horse = pointless — completely different meanings!

(B) cutting the long story short
• 'Cutting the long story short' = ചുരുക്കി പറഞ്ഞാൽ, summarizing a long story briefly.
• 'Pointless effort' meaning ഇതിൽ ഇല്ല — ഇത് brevity/summary idiom ആണ്.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Students ഈ familiar phrase pick ചെയ്യും — 'effort end ചെയ്യുക' ആണ് എന്ന് confuse ആകും. Meaning: brevity, not futility.

(D) hitting the nail on the head
• 'Hitting the nail on the head' = exactly correct thing say/do ചെയ്യുക — accurate, perfectly correct.
• Positive achievement idiom ആണ് — 'pointless' idea-യുടെ exact opposite!
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Hitting' = action word, students action idiom ആണ് ശരി എന്ന് think ചെയ്യും. Positive meaning ആണ്, futile effort meaning അല്ല.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: DEAD HORSE = already dead → no matter how much you beat = NO RESULT = POINTLESS! Dead horse ഒരിക്കലും ഓടില്ല, ആ effort ഒരിക്കലും ഫലിക്കില്ല!
• 'Beating' idioms — ഈ രണ്ടും PSC-ൽ ഒരുമിച്ച് ചോദിക്കും, confuse ആകരുത്:
  beat a DEAD horse = POINTLESS effort (dead = no result)
  beat AROUND the bush = INDIRECT talk (avoid center)
• PSC exam strategy: 'Underlined phrase-ന്റെ meaning = idiom' question-ൽ — phrase-ൽ key word നോക്കൂ: 'pointless' = beating a dead horse! 'Avoiding direct answer' = beating around the bush!

questionText:
Change the following sentence into reported speech:
"Do you expect a large crowd for the meeting?" the manager asked me.
optionA: The manager asked me if I had expected a large crowd for the meeting
optionB: The manager asked me if I have expected a large crowd for the meeting
optionC: The manager asked me if I expected a large crowd for the meeting
optionD: The manager had asked me if I expected a large crowd for the meeting

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (C) The manager asked me if I expected a large crowd for the meeting

• Direct speech: '"Do you expect...?" the manager asked me' — Yes/No question ആണ്.
• Yes/No question indirect speech: asked + if/whether + subject + verb (backshift).
• 'Do you expect' = Simple Present → backshift → Simple Past = 'expected'.
• Pronoun: 'you' → 'I'. Reporting verb: 'asked' (Simple Past).
• 'The manager asked me if I expected a large crowd for the meeting' — correct indirect speech!
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Yes/No Question Indirect Speech
  Direct: 'Do/Does + you + V?' → Indirect: asked + if/whether + subject + V2 (backshift)
  Backshift: Simple Present (expect) → Simple Past (expected)
• Reporting verb: 'asked' = Simple Past (stays as is)
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Indirect speech — Yes/No questions PSC-ൽ regular question type.
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. 'Do you like tea?' she asked. → She asked if I liked tea.
  2. 'Are you coming?' he asked. → He asked if I was coming.
  3. 'Did you finish?' she asked. → She asked if I had finished.
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

(A) The manager asked me if I had expected a large crowd for the meeting
• 'Had expected' = Past Perfect — 'Do you expect' = Simple Present → backshift → Simple Past only.
• Simple Present backshift = Simple Past. Past Perfect = Simple Past-ന്റെ backshift.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: ഇതാണ് most common trap — 'had expected' vs 'expected'. Simple Present → Simple Past only!

(B) The manager asked me if I have expected a large crowd for the meeting
• 'Have expected' = Present Perfect — indirect speech-ൽ backshift ആണ് ആവശ്യം.
• Past reporting verb 'asked' → tense backshift mandatory. 'Have expected' = no backshift.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Have expected' present feel — 'asked' past = backshift mandatory.

(D) The manager had asked me if I expected a large crowd for the meeting
• 'Had asked' = Past Perfect reporting verb — direct speech-ൽ 'asked' (Simple Past) ആണ്, overcorrection.
• Reporting verb 'asked' stays Simple Past — reporting verb backshift ഇല്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Had asked' formal — reporting verb ആണ് backshift ആകുന്നത് അല്ല.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: BACKSHIFT = one step BACK in time!
  Simple Present (expect) → one step back → Simple Past (expected) ✓
• Yes/No indirect speech:
  'Do/Does' question → asked + IF + subject + V2 (past)
• Exam strategy: 'Do you expect' → backshift → 'expected' (simple past). 'Had expected' = double backshift = wrong!

questionText:
Fill up suitably:
The place is infested ____________ rats.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (B) with

• 'Infested' = ഒരു place-ൽ harmful creatures/pests കൂട്ടമായി ഉള്ളത്. 'Infested WITH' = fixed preposition collocation ആണ്.
• 'The place is infested with rats' = 'ആ സ്ഥലം എലികളുടെ ശല്യം നിറഞ്ഞതാണ്' — perfect sentence.
• 'Infested with' = fixed phrase — by/in/on ഒരിക്കലും ഉപയോഗിക്കില്ല.
• Other examples: infested with mosquitoes, infested with cockroaches, infested with ants — 'with' constant.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: INFESTED + WITH = always! (never by/in/on)
• PSC fixed adjective + preposition collocations:
  infested WITH (rats, mosquitoes, pests)
  filled WITH (water, people)
  crowded WITH (people, cars)
  satisfied WITH (result)
  pleased WITH (achievement)
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Preposition collocations PSC-ൽ popular topic.
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. The kitchen is infested with cockroaches.
  2. The area is infested with mosquitoes during monsoon.
  3. The old house was infested with rats and mice.
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

(A) by
• 'Infested by' = grammatically wrong — 'by' = agent (passive voice), infestation-ൽ use ആകില്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Passive voice feel — 'infested' fixed collocation = 'with' ആണ്!

(C) in
• 'Infested in' = not a valid collocation — 'in' location preposition ആണ്.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'In' = location feel — adjective collocation ആണ്, location preposition അല്ല.

(D) on
• 'Infested on' = not valid — 'on' surface/day preposition ആണ്, ഇവിടെ fit ആകില്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Easy eliminate — 'on' clearly doesn't fit 'infested' context.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: INFESTED WITH = like FILLED WITH! Both = 'with'. Place FILLED WITH rats = infested WITH rats!
• Fixed preposition list:
  infested WITH | filled WITH | crowded WITH | satisfied WITH | pleased WITH
• Exam strategy: 'Infested' കണ്ടാൽ → automatic 'with'! Fixed collocation — never changes. PSC-ൽ ഇത് ഒരിക്കലും miss ആകരുത്!

questionText:
Spot the error in the section in italics and choose the correct option.
__Can you give me some informations on the uses of lasers?__

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (A) information

• Error word: 'informations' — 'information' ഒരു uncountable noun ആണ്. Uncountable nouns-ന് plural form ഉണ്ടാകില്ല — 's' ചേർക്കാൻ കഴിയില്ല.
• 'Can you give me some information on the uses of lasers?' — 'some' uncountable nouns-ൊടൊപ്പം ഉപയോഗിക്കാം (some water, some information, some advice).
• 'Informations' = grammatically wrong — 'information' = always singular form, plural ആകില്ല ഒരിക്കലും.
• Correction: 'informations' → 'information' — ഇത്ര മാത്രം change ചെയ്താൽ sentence correct ആകും.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Uncountable noun + 's' = ALWAYS WRONG!
• PSC-ൽ ഏറ്റവും ഇടയ്ക്ക് test ചെയ്യുന്ന uncountable nouns (ഇവയ്ക്ക് plural ഇല്ല):
  information, advice, furniture, news, luggage, baggage,
  equipment, knowledge, traffic, weather, homework, research
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: 'informations', 'advices', 'furnitures', 'newses' — ഇവ error questions-ൽ ഇടയ്ക്ക് കാണാം.
• Quantity express ചെയ്യണമെങ്കിൽ: a piece of information / some information / a lot of information
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. She gave me useful advice. (advices ❌)
  2. The furniture in this room is expensive. (furnitures ❌)
  3. We need more information about the topic. (informations ❌)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

(B) informing
• 'Informing' = present participle / gerund form of 'inform' — "give me some informing" = meaning ഇല്ലാത്ത phrase.
• Sentence-ൽ ആവശ്യം ഒരു noun ആണ് ('information' = noun). 'Informing' = verb form, noun replace ചെയ്യില്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: '-ing' form familiar ആണ് — noun form ആണ് ആവശ്യം, verb form അല്ല.

(C) informal
• 'Informal' = adjective — ഔദ്യോഗികമല്ലാത്ത, casual. "give me some informal" = grammatically meaningless.
• Sentence-ൽ noun position-ൽ adjective വയ്ക്കാൻ കഴിയില്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'informal' = 'information'-ൽ നിന്ന് similar spelling — prefix 'in-' confuse ചെയ്യും. Adjective vs noun meaning check ചെയ്യൂ.

(D) informed
• 'Informed' = past tense / past participle of 'inform', or adjective (well-informed). "give me some informed" = meaningless.
• Noun position-ൽ past participle/adjective fit ആകില്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'informed' = 'information' similar root — past participle feel ഉണ്ടെങ്കിലും noun ആകില്ല.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: INFORMATION = IN + FORM + ATION — ഇത് ഒരു mass, form-ൽ ഉള്ള knowledge! Mass-ന് plural ഇല്ല — water-ന് 'waters' ഇല്ലാത്തതുപോലെ!
• Uncountable noun list — PSC guaranteed: information, advice, news, furniture, equipment, luggage, knowledge, research, traffic, homework
• PSC exam strategy: 'informations / advices / furnitures' — ഇവ കണ്ടാൽ = automatic error! Uncountable nouns never take 's'.

questionText:
Choose the most correct option.
I enjoyed the ————— part of the movie more than the —————

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (D) latter, former

• 'Latter' = two things-ൽ second ആയ ഒന്ന്; 'former' = two things-ൽ first ആയ ഒന്ന്.
• 'I enjoyed the latter part of the movie more than the former' = 'movie-യുടെ second/closing part, first part-നേക്കാൾ ആസ്വദിച്ചു.'
• 'The latter' = movie-യുടെ second (concluding) portion; 'the former' = movie-യുടെ first portion — ഈ pairing-ൽ second blank-ൽ 'part' repeat ചെയ്യാതെ 'the former' alone നിൽക്കും.
• Latter/former = specifically two items compare ചെയ്യുമ്പോഴുള്ള classic English pair — ഈ context-ൽ perfectly fit ആകുന്നു.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula:
  FORMER = first of two (ആദ്യത്തേത്)
  LATTER = second of two (രണ്ടാമത്തേത്)
  EARLIER = comparative of 'early' (time-based, time comparison)
  LATER = comparative of 'late' (time-based, time comparison)
• Former/Latter = two specific things refer ചെയ്യുമ്പോൾ; Earlier/Later = time comparison-ൽ
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: latter vs later, former vs earlier — PSC-ൽ confused word pairs ആയി ഇടയ്ക്ക് ചോദിക്കുന്നു.
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. Between tea and coffee, I prefer the latter. (coffee = second mentioned = latter)
  2. Of the two brothers, the former is a doctor. (first brother = former)
  3. She arrived later than expected. (time-based = later, not latter)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

(A) latest, earliest
• 'Latest' = superlative (most recent among many); 'earliest' = superlative (earliest among many) — superlatives ഇവിടെ fit ആകില്ല.
• Movie-യുടെ parts compare ചെയ്യുന്നത് superlative level-ൽ അല്ല — two parts മാത്രമേ ഉള്ളൂ.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Latest' familiar word — superlative vs comparative vs former/latter distinction check ചെയ്യൂ.

(B) late, early
• 'Late' and 'early' = base/positive degree adjectives — comparison ഇവിടെ ആവശ്യം, base degree അല്ല.
• "the late part" / "the early part" — comparison express ചെയ്യാൻ comparative/latter-former form വേണം.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Simple familiar words — comparison context-ൽ base form insufficient.

(C) later, earlier
• 'Later' and 'earlier' = time-based comparatives — chronological time compare ചെയ്യുന്നു, not position in a sequence of two.
• 'The earlier' (second blank, standalone) = slightly awkward — 'the former' is more natural as a pronoun reference.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Later/earlier' grammatically possible — but 'latter/former' = the precise pair for two items, making (D) more correct.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: LATTER = LAST (both start with L, both mean 'second/last of two')! FORMER = FIRST (both start with F)! F = First = Former, L = Last = Latter!
• When to use which:
  Two things → latter (2nd) / former (1st)
  Time comparison → later / earlier
  Most recent → latest / earliest (superlative)
• PSC exam strategy: 'two things compare' + second blank standalone = latter/former! 'later' ആണ് time-based, 'latter' ആണ് sequence-based — ഈ difference ഓർക്കൂ!

questionText:
Choose the correct Question Tag to complete the sentence.
I hope he calls soon, __________

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (B) won't he?

• 'I hope he calls soon' — Main verb: 'hope' (I hope). Subordinate clause: 'he calls soon' (positive, Simple Present).
• 'Hope/think/believe/expect' — ഈ mental state verbs ഉള്ള sentences-ൽ question tag subordinate clause-ന്റെ subject + auxiliary ഉപയോഗിച്ചാണ് ഉണ്ടാക്കുന്നത്.
• Subordinate clause: 'he calls' = positive, Simple Present → Negative tag → 'won't he?' (will = future expectation for Simple Present context).
• 'I hope he calls soon, won't he?' = 'അദ്ദേഹം വൈകാതെ call ചെയ്യും, അല്ലേ?' — ഈ expectation/hope express ചെയ്യുന്ന tag ആണ് 'won't he?'.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: I hope/think/believe/expect + clause → tag = clause-ന്റെ subject + auxiliary
  'I hope he calls' → tag = won't he? (not 'don't I?')
  'I think she is right' → tag = isn't she?
  'I believe they will come' → tag = won't they?
• Main rule: Positive clause → Negative tag; Negative clause → Positive tag
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: 'I hope/think/believe' sentences-ൽ tag — ഈ pattern PSC-ൽ ഒരു tricky favourite ആണ്.
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. I think she knows the answer, doesn't she?
  2. I believe he will come, won't he?
  3. I hope they arrive on time, won't they?
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

(A) can he?
• 'Can' = ability auxiliary — 'calls' (Simple Present) -ൽ 'can' ഒരു ബന്ധവുമില്ല.
• Tag auxiliary must match the clause auxiliary — Simple Present 'calls' → 'will/won't', not 'can'.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Can he?' = question feel ഉണ്ട് — wrong auxiliary. 'Calls' = Simple Present, no 'can' involved.

(C) did he?
• 'Did' = Simple Past auxiliary — 'calls' = Simple Present, past tense ആകില്ല.
• Tense mismatch: present clause → present-based tag. 'Did he?' = past reference.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Did' = common question auxiliary — tense match check ചെയ്യൂ. Present clause = won't/doesn't, not 'did'.

(D) could he?
• 'Could' = past modal / polite possibility — 'calls' Simple Present-ൽ 'could' ഒരു ബന്ധവുമില്ല.
• Tag must reflect the actual auxiliary of the clause, not a random modal.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Could he?' = polite question feel — wrong modal. Tag = future expectation of 'calls' = won't he?
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: I HOPE = I expect it to happen in future → tag uses WILL/WON'T! Hope = future expectation = won't he?
• 'I hope/think/believe' tag rule:
  Step 1: Look at the SUBORDINATE clause (ignore 'I hope/think')
  Step 2: Find subject + make tag from subordinate clause
  Step 3: Positive clause → negative tag (won't, doesn't, isn't)
• PSC exam strategy: 'I hope he...' → forget 'I hope', look at 'he calls' → positive → negative tag = 'won't he?' Guaranteed mark!

questionText: Which of the following sentences correctly uses the correlative conjunction "either... or"?
optionA: Either you can come with us nor stay at home.
optionB: You can either take the bus or walk.
optionC: You neither eat this nor that.
optionD: She is either tired nor hungry.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (B) You can either take the bus or walk.

• 'Either...or' = two alternatives suggest ചെയ്യുന്ന correlative pair. 'Either A or B' = A അല്ലെങ്കിൽ B.
• 'You can either take the bus or walk' = 'ബസ് എടുക്കാം, അല്ലെങ്കിൽ നടക്കാം' — two alternatives perfectly presented.
• 'Either' + 'or' ഒരുമിച്ച് — pair correct ആണ്. Parallel structure: 'take the bus' / 'walk' = both verb phrases.
• Either...or = ഒരു choice ഉണ്ട് — ഇവിടെ exactly ഒരു choice present ചെയ്യുന്നു. ✓
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula — Correlative Pairs (NEVER mix!):
  EITHER...OR (choice between two positives)
  NEITHER...NOR (negating both)
  NOT ONLY...BUT ALSO (adding extra point)
  BOTH...AND (including both)
  NO SOONER...THAN (immediate sequence)
  LEST...SHOULD (negative purpose)
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Correlatives — 'either...nor' / 'neither...or' mixing = classic PSC error question!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. Either you study now or you fail. (either...or ✓)
  2. Neither he nor she came. (neither...nor ✓)
  3. Not only did she sing but also danced. (not only...but also ✓)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

(A) Either you can come with us nor stay at home.
• 'Either...nor' = WRONG pairing! 'Either' -ൻ്റെ pair = 'or', never 'nor'.
• 'Nor' = 'neither' -ൻ്റെ pair ആണ്. Mixed pair = grammatically wrong.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: ഇതാണ് classic either/neither confusion trap! Either = or, Neither = nor — ഒരിക്കലും mix ആകില്ല.

(C) You neither eat this nor that.
• 'Neither...nor' correctly paired — but question asks for 'either...or' usage. ഇത് neither...nor ആണ്, either...or അല്ല.
• Grammatically correct sentence — പക്ഷേ wrong correlative pair use ചെയ്തു.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Grammatically correct ആണ്, പക്ഷേ question 'either...or' ആണ് ചോദിക്കുന്നത് — read carefully!

(D) She is either tired nor hungry.
• 'Either...nor' = WRONG pairing again! 'Either' -ൻ്റെ pair = 'or', not 'nor'.
• 'She is either tired or hungry' ആണ് correct form.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: (A) -ഉം (D) -ഉം same mistake — either...nor mixing. PSC ഈ trap രണ്ടു options-ൽ repeat ചെയ്യും. 'Either = or' ഓർക്കൂ!
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: EITHER → OR (E...O = vowels together!), NEITHER → NOR (N...N = same letter starts both!). Vowel pair = either/or, Same-letter pair = neither/nor!
• Never-mix rule: Either = OR only. Neither = NOR only. Mix ആകില്ല ഒരിക്കലും!
• PSC exam strategy: Options-ൽ 'either...nor' അല്ലെങ്കിൽ 'neither...or' കണ്ടാൽ = automatic wrong! Correct pair find ചെയ്ത് mark ചെയ്യൂ.

questionText: Identify the correct sentence with the proper use of a preposition.
optionA: They are depending in each other.
optionB: The book is full with interesting stories.
optionD: He congratulated her to her success.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (C) She insisted on helping him.

• 'Insist on' = fixed verb + preposition collocation. 'On' = 'insist' -ൻ്റെ correct preposition.
• 'She insisted on helping him' = 'അദ്ദേഹത്തെ സഹായിക്കണമെന്ന് അവൾ നിർബന്ധിച്ചു' — grammatically perfect.
• 'Insist on + gerund (V-ing)' = fixed pattern. 'Helping' = gerund ആണ് — ശരിയായ structure.
• ഈ sentence-ൽ preposition 'on' ശരിയായ collocation ആണ്, meaning clear ആണ്.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകรണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Fixed verb + preposition collocations (ഇവ memorize ചെയ്യണം!):
  insist ON | depend ON | rely ON | congratulate ON
  full OF | proud OF | afraid OF | capable OF | fond OF
  good AT | aim AT | laugh AT
  interested IN | succeed IN | result IN
  responsible FOR | wait FOR | look FOR
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Fixed preposition collocations PSC-ൽ ഏറ്റവും popular topic — 'full of', 'depend on', 'congratulate on' ഇടയ്ക്ക് ചോദിക്കുന്നു.
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. We depend on each other. (depend on ✓)
  2. The jar is full of water. (full of ✓)
  3. He congratulated her on her promotion. (congratulate on ✓)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

(A) They are depending in each other.
• 'Depending in' = WRONG. 'Depend' -ൻ്റെ correct preposition = 'ON' — depend ON each other.
• 'In' = location preposition, 'depend' collocation ആകില്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'in' = common preposition — 'depend on' fixed collocation ഓർക്കൂ!

(B) The book is full with interesting stories.
• 'Full with' = WRONG. 'Full' -ൻ്റെ correct preposition = 'OF' — full OF stories.
• 'With' = accompaniment (with friends), 'full' = 'of' ആണ് ചേരുക.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Full with' natural feel ഉണ്ട് — 'filled with' ആണ് 'with' ഉള്ളത്. 'Full' = 'of' ആണ്! Full of / filled with — distinguish ചെയ്യൂ.

(D) He congratulated her to her success.
• 'Congratulate to' = WRONG. 'Congratulate' -ൻ്റെ correct preposition = 'ON' — congratulate ON her success.
• 'To' = direction/indirect object, 'congratulate' collocation ആകില്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'to' = indirect object feel (gave to, said to) — 'congratulate' = 'on' ആണ്, 'to' അല്ല!
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: INSIST + ON = press ON something = insist! ON-top of pressure = ON. Full OF content = OF. Congratulate ON achievement = ON (achievement-ന് top-ൽ)!
• Three pairs to memorize together:
  full OF | proud OF | capable OF (all use OF)
  depend ON | insist ON | congratulate ON (all use ON)
  interested IN | succeed IN (all use IN)
• PSC exam strategy: Fixed collocation question-ൽ verb/adjective കണ്ടാൽ — pair preposition recall ചെയ്യൂ. 'Full' = OF, 'depend' = ON, 'congratulate' = ON. Practice list memorize ചെയ്യൂ!

questionText: 'Mortgage' is related to

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (D) financial transactions

• 'Mortgage' = property (house/land) security-ആയി വച്ച് bank-ൽ നിന്ന് loan എടുക്കുന്ന ഒരു financial arrangement.
• ഇത് 100% financial transaction ആണ് — property ownership temporarily transfer ചെയ്ത് loan repay ചെയ്ത back ആകുന്ന process.
• Real life example: വീട് വാങ്ങാൻ bank loan എടുക്കുമ്പോൾ ആ വീട് mortgage ആക്കി വയ്ക്കുന്നു — ഇത് financial domain.
• Criminal procedure / movies / chemistry — ഒരു ബന്ധവുമില്ല.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: MORTGAGE = property as security + loan = FINANCIAL TRANSACTION
• Etymology hint: French 'mort' = dead + 'gage' = pledge — literally 'dead pledge' (loan repaid ആകുമ്പോൾ pledge ends, hence 'dead')!
• Related financial terms PSC-ൽ ഇടയ്ക്ക് ചോദിക്കുന്നവ:
  mortgage = property loan / collateral loan
  collateral = loan-നുള്ള security/guarantee
  equity = property-ൽ owner-ൻ്റെ share
  interest = loan-ന് pay ചെയ്യുന്ന extra amount
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Word-to-domain matching questions PSC-ൽ ഇടയ്ക്ക് — finance, legal, medical terms.
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. They took out a mortgage to buy their first home.
  2. The bank holds the mortgage on our house.
  3. She paid off the mortgage in 20 years.
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

(A) criminal procedures
• Criminal procedure = courts, crimes, police, legal investigation — mortgage ഒരിക്കലും criminal domain-ൽ ഉൾപ്പെടില്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Mort' = death (French) — students criminal/legal feel ആകും. Death connection ഇല്ല, financial pledge ആണ്!

(B) movies and music
• Movies and music = entertainment industry — mortgage = financial domain, entertainment ആകില്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Totally unrelated option — ഈ option students quickly eliminate ചെയ്യും.

(C) chemical reactions
• Chemical reactions = chemistry/science domain — mortgage = financial domain, chemistry ആകില്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Mort' = Latin root (death) → chemistry students 'mortification' (tissue death) connect ചെയ്തേക്കും. Financial meaning ഓർക്കൂ!
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: MORTGAGE = MORE GAGE (gauge/measure) on your property for MORE money from bank! Bank measures your property = gives you loan = FINANCIAL!
• Etymology anchor: French 'mort gage' = dead pledge — loan repay ആകുമ്പോൾ pledge 'dies' (ends). ഈ story ഓർത്തിരുന്നാൽ meaning forget ആകില്ല!
• PSC exam strategy: Financial terms — mortgage, equity, collateral, dividend, depreciation — ഇവ domain-ൽ ഉൾപ്പെടുത്തൂ. 'Financial transactions' option കണ്ടാൽ financial word-ന് automatic match!

questionText:
Choose the correct meaning of the phrasal verb in italics.
I __ran into__ Ajith and his family at the mall yesterday.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (C) met unexpectedly

• 'Ran into' = accidentally/unexpectedly meet someone = പ്രതീക്ഷിക്കാതെ കണ്ടുമുട്ടുക.
• 'I ran into Ajith and his family at the mall' = 'Mall-ൽ Ajith-നെയും family-യേയും accidentally കണ്ടുമുട്ടി.'
• Context clue: 'at the mall yesterday' = accidental meeting, not planned. 'Ran into' = unexpected encounter.
• Literal meaning (physically running into/colliding) ഇവിടെ apply ആകില്ല — idiomatic meaning ആണ്: met unexpectedly.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: RUN INTO = encounter unexpectedly / meet by chance
• RUN phrasal verbs — PSC-ൽ ഇടയ്ക്ക് ചോദിക്കുന്നവ:
  run into = meet unexpectedly / encounter a problem
  run out of = exhaust supply (run out of time/money)
  run over = vehicle-കൊണ്ട് hit ചെയ്യുക / quickly review
  run away = ഓടി രക്ഷപ്പെടുക, flee
  run across = meet or find unexpectedly (similar to run into)
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Phrasal verb meaning questions PSC-ൽ regular topic — 'run into' ഒരു popular choice.
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. I ran into my old teacher at the supermarket.
  2. We ran out of time during the exam.
  3. Be careful — don't run over the cat!
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

(A) collided with
• 'Collided with' = physically hit/crash — 'I collided with Ajith at the mall' = physically ഇടിച്ചു! ഇത് sentence-ൻ്റെ context-ൽ absurd.
• 'Ran into' = literal meaning ആണ് ഇത്, idiomatic meaning ആണ് ഇവിടെ ആവശ്യം.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: ഇതാണ് ഏറ്റവും classic phrasal verb trap — literal meaning = wrong, idiomatic meaning = right!

(B) planned a meeting
• 'Planned a meeting' = intentional — 'ran into' = exactly the opposite: unplanned/accidental.
• Context: 'at the mall yesterday' = chance encounter, not arranged meeting.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Students 'ran into' = actively went to meet? എന്ന് confuse ആകും. 'Into' = accidental encounter, not planning.

(D) did not see
• 'Did not see' = ഇത് complete opposite — 'ran into' = saw AND met, not failed to see.
• 'I ran into Ajith' = definitely saw and met him!
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Ran into' = negative/miss ആണ് എന്ന് confuse ആകും — actually = successfully (accidentally) met.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: You're walking, someone suddenly appears in your path — you almost 'RUN INTO' them! That surprise meeting = RAN INTO = met unexpectedly!
• RUN phrasal verb family (learn all at once):
  run INTO = meet accidentally | run OUT OF = exhaust | run AWAY = flee | run OVER = drive over
• PSC exam strategy: Phrasal verb-ൽ literal meaning (collide) option = automatic trap! Idiomatic meaning (met unexpectedly) = correct. Always choose idiomatic meaning over literal!

questionText:
Choose the correct synonym of the word in italics.
My grandfather used to give me very __sagacious__ advice when I was growing up.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (B) wise

• 'Sagacious' = having keen mental discernment and good judgment = ബുദ്ധിശാലി, wise.
• 'Very sagacious advice' = 'വളരെ ജ്ഞാനമുള്ള ഉപദേശം' — grandfather-ൻ്റെ wise counsel describe ചെയ്യുന്നു.
• Synonym = ഒരേ അർത്ഥം ഉള്ള word. 'Sagacious' = 'wise' — ഇവ perfect synonyms ആണ്.
• Context clue: 'growing up' + 'grandfather's advice' = life wisdom, guidance = sagacious/wise — perfectly fits.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: SAGACIOUS = SAGE + -acious = WISE (sage = wise person!)
• Etymology hint: Latin 'sagax' = keen perception, sharp mind → sagacious = having sharp wisdom.
• Word family: sagacious (adjective) → sagaciously (adverb) → sagacity (noun = the quality of being wise)
• Synonyms of 'wise/sagacious' (PSC vocabulary family):
  sagacious, wise, prudent, shrewd, astute, judicious, discerning, perceptive
• Antonyms: foolish, stupid, unwise, imprudent
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Advanced vocabulary synonyms PSC-ൽ ഇടയ്ക്ക് — sagacious, prudent, astute, judicious ഒക്കെ ഒരേ family.
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. The sagacious leader made wise decisions. (= wise leader)
  2. Her sagacious advice saved the company.
  3. He was known for his sagacity in business.
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

(A) foolish
• 'Foolish' = ബുദ്ധിഹീനൻ, unwise — 'sagacious' -ൻ്റെ exact ANTONYM ആണ്!
• 'Very foolish advice from grandfather' = completely wrong context — grandfather wisdom describe ചെയ്യുകയാണ്.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: ഇതാണ് classic synonym trap — antonym option include ചെയ്യും! 'Foolish' = sagacious-ൻ്റെ opposite.

(C) continuous
• 'Continuous' = ഇടവിടാതെ നടക്കുന്ന, without interruption — 'sagacious' ആയ ബന്ധം ഇല്ല.
• Time/duration concept ആണ് 'continuous', wisdom concept ആകില്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Continuous advice' = sounds possible — but 'sagacious' ≠ 'continuous'. Meaning match ചെയ്യൂ!

(D) serious
• 'Serious' = ഗൗരവമുള്ള, not joking — 'sagacious' = wise, not the same as serious.
• Serious advice = solemn; sagacious advice = wise. Different qualities.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Very serious advice' ≈ possible phrase — but synonym ≠ same context, synonym = same meaning. 'Serious' ≠ 'wise'.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: SAGACIOUS = SAGE (wise person) + -acious (full of). A SAGE is wise → SAGACIOUS = full of wisdom! Sage = wise, sage tea = herb, sage advice = wise advice — all same root!
• Vocabulary family to learn together: sagacious = prudent = astute = judicious = wise (all mean having good judgment)
• PSC exam strategy: Unknown word + 'synonym' question → root word look ചെയ്യൂ. 'Sag-' = sage root = wisdom. Antonym option (foolish) = trap, eliminate first!

questionText:
Choose the most suitable one-word substitute for the phrase in italics.
The passage given above is __a piece of writing about the writer's journeys to different places__.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (D) travelogue

• Phrase: 'a piece of writing about the writer's journeys to different places' — travel + writing = travelogue.
• 'Travelogue' = a piece of writing, film, or talk about someone's travels and journeys = travel experiences describe ചെയ്യുന്ന document.
• 'Journeys to different places' + 'writer's own experience' = personal travel account = travelogue.
• Perfect one-word substitute: travelogue = travel writing/account. ആ entire phrase ഒരൊറ്റ word-ൽ capture ആകുന്നു.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: TRAVEL + -LOGUE = account/talk about TRAVEL = TRAVELOGUE
• Etymology hint: 'Travel' + Greek 'logos' = word/account → travelogue = an account of travels!
• '-logue' family (PSC ഇടയ്ക്ക് ചോദിക്കുന്നു):
  travelogue = account of travels
  monologue = speech by one person alone
  dialogue = conversation between two people
  prologue = introduction to a literary work
  epilogue = concluding section of a literary work
  catalogue = list of items
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: One word substitutes — writing forms PSC-ൽ popular: travelogue, autobiography, biography, monologue.
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. She wrote a travelogue about her trip to Europe.
  2. The travelogue described his adventures in the Amazon.
  3. His travelogue became a bestseller.
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

(A) monologue
• 'Monologue' = ഒരു person മാത്രം സംസാരിക്കുന്ന speech/writing — journeys about ഒന്നുമില്ല.
• 'Mono' = one, 'logue' = speech/account. Travel account ആകില്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: '-logue' ending കണ്ട് confuse ആകും — monologue = solo speech, not travel writing.

(B) diary
• 'Diary' = daily personal record of events/thoughts — travels specific ആയി describe ചെയ്യുന്നത് ആകണമെന്നില്ല.
• Travel-specific writing = travelogue (specific term), diary = general daily record.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Diary of travels' = possible — but specific travel writing = travelogue. More specific term = better answer.

(C) journal
• 'Journal' = regular written record (like diary) / academic publication — travel-specific term ആകില്ല.
• Journal = general record keeping; travelogue = specifically about travel experiences.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Travel journal' = common phrase — but one-word substitute for 'journeys to different places' = travelogue (most specific).
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: TRAVEL + LOG = TRAVELOGUE! A ship's LOG = record of journey. TRAVEL + LOG = record of travels = TRAVELOGUE!
• '-logue' family memory:
  MONO (one) + logue = monologue (one person speaks)
  TRAVEL + logue = travelogue (travel record)
  DI (two) + alogue = dialogue (two people)
  PRO (before) + logue = prologue (before story)
• PSC exam strategy: 'Writing about journeys/places' = travelogue, always. Diary/journal = general records. Monologue = solo speech. Travel writing = travelogue, guaranteed!

questionText:
Choose the correct idiom to complete the sentence.
The new water treatment plant is a ————— between the inhabitants of the town and the authorities.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (B) bone of contention

• 'Bone of contention' = two parties തമ്മിൽ argument/dispute-ഉണ്ടാക്കുന്ന issue = a subject of disagreement.
• 'A bone of contention BETWEEN the inhabitants and the authorities' — 'between X and Y' + disagreement = bone of contention.
• Sentence meaning: water treatment plant-ന്റെ issue-ൽ town residents-ഉം authorities-ഉം തമ്മിൽ dispute ഉണ്ട്.
• 'Between' keyword: bone of contention = always between two parties. ഇവിടെ: inhabitants vs authorities = two parties.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: BONE OF CONTENTION = subject of dispute between two parties (always 'between X and Y')
• Idiom meaning: Dogs fighting over a bone = ഒരു bone-ന് വേണ്ടി two dogs fight ചെയ്യുന്നു — ആ bone = 'bone of contention'!
• PSC-ൽ ഇടയ്ക്ക് ചോദിക്കുന്ന conflict/surprise idioms:
  bone of contention = subject of disagreement between two parties
  bolt from the blue = sudden unexpected surprise (like lightning from clear sky)
  storm in a teacup = big fuss about a small/trivial matter
  boon from heaven = an unexpected blessing/benefit
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Idiom completion questions PSC-ൽ regular — 'bone of contention' frequent favourite.
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. The border dispute is a bone of contention between the two countries.
  2. His promotion was a bolt from the blue.
  3. The argument was just a storm in a teacup.
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

(A) boon from heaven
• 'Boon from heaven' = an unexpected blessing — sentence-ൽ 'between' two parties ഉണ്ട്, blessing idea ഇല്ല.
• Water plant = good thing for everyone ആണ് — but sentence structure 'between inhabitants and authorities' = dispute context.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Water treatment = good thing ആണ് — students 'boon' pick ചെയ്യും. 'Between' = dispute keyword ആണ്!

(C) storm in a teacup
• 'Storm in a teacup' = minor matter-ന്е большой fuss ഉണ്ടാക്കുക — trivial issue over-dramatize ചെയ്യുന്നത്.
• Water treatment plant = major public issue, not a trivial matter. 'Between two parties' = serious dispute.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Storm' = dispute feel — but 'storm in teacup' = trivially small issue, not serious two-party dispute.

(D) bolt from the blue
• 'Bolt from the blue' = completely unexpected surprise, like lightning from clear sky — sudden shock.
• Sentence needs 'dispute between two parties' — surprise/shock idea ഇല്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Bolt' = strong impact feel — but 'bolt from blue' = surprise, not a dispute between parties.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: BONE OF CONTENTION = imagine two dogs growling over ONE bone — that bone = what they're fighting about = the bone of contention! 'Between' two = always bone of contention!
• Four idioms to know together:
  BONE of contention = dispute between two 🐕🦴🐕
  BOLT from the blue = sudden surprise ⚡ from clear sky
  STORM in a teacup = small issue, big fuss 🌪️☕
  BOON from heaven = unexpected blessing 🎁
• PSC exam strategy: Sentence-ൽ 'between X and Y' + blank = bone of contention, almost always! 'Between' keyword = dispute idiom!

questionText: A person who helps others especially through charitable work or donations of money:

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (B) Philanthropist

• 'A person who helps others especially through charitable work or donations of money' — ഇത് philanthropy ചെയ്യുന്ന ആൾ, society-ക്ക് വേണ്ടി time, money, effort donate ചെയ്യുന്ന ആൾ.
• 'Philanthropist' = philo (love) + anthropos (human being) — literally 'one who loves humanity'. Mankind- നെ love ചെയ്ത് help ചെയ്യുന്ന ആൾ = philanthropist.
• Real-world example: Ratan Tata, Bill Gates — വലിയ donations ചെയ്ത് society help ചെയ്യുന്ന famous philanthropists ആണ്.
• One-word substitute: ആ entire definition-നെ 'philanthropist' ഒരൊറ്റ word-ൽ capture ചെയ്യുന്നു.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: PHILO (love) + ANTHROPOS (human) = PHILANTHROPIST = one who loves and helps humanity
• Etymology: Greek roots — 'philos' = loving + 'anthropos' = human being
• Word family: philanthropy (noun — the act) → philanthropist (person) → philanthropic (adjective)
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: One word substitutes for 'types of people' PSC-ൽ popular — '-ist' ending words ഇടയ്ക്ക് ചോദിക്കുന്നു.
• Related '-philo' words (PSC vocabulary family):
  philanthropist = lover of humanity (charity giver)
  philologist = lover of language/words
  bibliophile = lover of books
  philosopher = lover of wisdom (philo + sophia)
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. The philanthropist donated millions to build schools.
  2. She was known as a philanthropist for her charity work.
  3. Many philanthropists fund hospitals and orphanages.
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

(A) Optimist
• 'Optimist' = എല്ലാ കാര്യങ്ങളിലും positive ആയി ചിന്തിക്കുന്ന ആൾ, always hopeful person.
• Positive thinking ≠ charitable giving. Definition-ൽ 'helping/donating' ആണ് — positive attitude അല്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Optimist' = good person feel — charity/helping = philanthropist, not optimist.

(C) Materialist
• 'Materialist' = material things/wealth-ന് ഏറ്റവും importance കൊടുക്കുന്ന ആൾ — exact opposite of a philanthropist!
• Materialist = keeps money for self; Philanthropist = gives money to others.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Material' = money/things connect ചെയ്ത് confuse ആകും — meaning exactly opposite ആണ്!

(D) Pedant
• 'Pedant' = rules and knowledge-ന്  excessive importance കൊടുക്കുന്ന ആൾ, overly academic/fussy about details.
• Charitable giving ആയി ഒരു ബന്ധവുമില്ല — pedant = academic fussiness, not generosity.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Uncommon word — 'pedant' = knowledge fussiness, philanthropy = human generosity.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: PHILAN-THROPIST = PHILAN (like 'friend') + THROP (throw) = throws friendship/money to people! A philanthropist THROWS their wealth at humanity!
• Greek root family to remember: PHILO = love → philosopher (loves wisdom), philanthropist (loves humans), bibliophile (loves books)
• PSC exam strategy: One word substitute questions — look for '-ist' person words. 'Helps others + charity + donations' = philanthropist. Never confuse with optimist (positive thinker) or materialist (money lover = opposite)!

questionText: From which language does the word __democracy__ come?

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (D) Greek

• 'Democracy' = Greek origin. 'Demos' (δῆμος) = people + 'kratos' (κράτος) = power/rule → 'rule by the people'.
• ജനാധിപത്യം — ജനങ്ങൾ ഭരിക്കുന്ന system. Greek city-states (Athens) ആണ് democracy ആദ്യം practice ചെയ്തത്.
• 'Demos' + 'kratos' = democracy — ഈ Greek roots ഓർത്തിരുന്നാൽ language of origin never forget ആകില്ല.
• English-ൽ ഏറ്റവും കൂടുതൽ political/philosophical words Greek-ൽ നിന്ന് വന്നിട്ടുണ്ട്.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: DEMO (people) + CRACY (rule) = DEMOCRACY = rule by the people (Greek origin)
• Etymology: Greek 'demos' = people + 'kratos' = strength/rule/power
• '-cracy' family (all Greek, all mean 'rule by'):
  democracy = rule by the people (demos)
  autocracy = rule by one person (auto = self)
  aristocracy = rule by nobles (aristos = best)
  bureaucracy = rule by officials (bureau = office)
  theocracy = rule by God/religion (theos = god)
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Word origin questions PSC-ൽ ഇടയ്ക്ക് — Greek vs Latin vs French ആണ് most common options.
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. Democracy originated in ancient Athens, Greece.
  2. The word 'democracy' has Greek roots meaning 'people's rule'.
  3. India is the world's largest democracy.
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

(A) Latin
• Latin = Roman language. Democracy-ക്ക് Latin origin ഇല്ല — Greek origin ആണ്.
• Latin-ൽ നിന്ന് വന്ന political words: legislature (legis = law), republic (res publica = public matter).
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Latin = classical language feel — political words-ന് Latin ആണ് origin എന്ന് confuse ആകും. 'Democracy' = Greek!

(B) German
• German = Germanic language family — modern language, ancient political philosophy words ഇതിൽ നിന്ന് English-ൽ കുറവ്.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Germany = known for philosophy/politics — language of the word's origin ≠ country's political history.

(C) French
• French-ൽ നിന്ന് English-ൽ വന്ന words: liberty, justice, parliament — political words ഉണ്ട്, പക്ഷേ 'democracy' അല്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: French = language of philosophy/revolution feel — 'democracy' specific word = Greek roots 'demos' + 'kratos'.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: DEMO-CRACY — 'demo' കേൾക്കുമ്പോൾ ഓർക്കൂ: DEMONSTRATION = people showing power in public! DEMO = people (Greek). Democracy = people's power!
• '-cracy' words — all Greek, all mean 'rule': democracy, autocracy, aristocracy, bureaucracy, theocracy
• PSC exam strategy: Word origin questions-ൽ political philosophy terms (democracy, philosophy, politics, monarchy) = mostly Greek. Legal terms (legislature, judicial) = mostly Latin. Fashion/cuisine words = mostly French. ഈ pattern ഓർക്കൂ!

questionText:
Add the correct question tag:
__Please lend your book__; ____________?

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (C) will you

• 'Please lend your book' = imperative sentence (command/request). Imperative sentences-ൽ question tag = 'will you?'
• 'Please' ഉള്ള polite request-ൽ standard question tag = 'will you?' — ഇത് fixed rule ആണ്.
• 'Please lend your book, will you?' = 'ദയവായി നിങ്ങളുടെ book തരാമോ?' — polite request feel perfectly convey ചെയ്യുന്നു.
• Imperative + will you = standard English pattern, PSC-ൽ ഇടയ്ക്ക് test ചെയ്യുന്ന rule.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula:
  Positive imperative (Do this!) → will you? / won't you?
  Please + imperative (polite) → will you? ✓ (most standard)
  Negative imperative (Don't do this!) → will you?
  Let's... → shall we?
• 'Will you?' = standard imperative tag for direct requests
• 'Won't you?' = also possible but softer/more persuasive (inviting rather than requesting)
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Imperative question tags PSC-ൽ favourite — especially 'Let's → shall we?' and 'Please → will you?'
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. Please sit down, will you?
  2. Open the window, will you?
  3. Let's go for a walk, shall we?
  4. Don't be late, will you?
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

(A) don't you
• 'Don't you' = tag for positive statements with 'you' as subject in Simple Present (e.g., "You like tea, don't you?").
• Imperative sentence-ൽ 'don't you' use ആകില്ല — statement tag ആണ്, request tag അല്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'You' subject ഉള്ള sentence — 'don't you' statement-ൻ്റെ tag ആണ്, imperative-ൻ്റെ tag 'will you?' ആണ്.

(B) won't you
• 'Won't you?' = also grammatically possible for imperatives — but 'will you?' is the more direct/standard form, especially after 'please'.
• 'Won't you?' = more of a persuasive invitation; 'will you?' = standard polite request tag.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Most common confusion — both possible, but 'will you?' = the standard/correct answer PSC expects for 'please' requests.

(D) shall you
• 'Shall you' = does not exist as a standard question tag in English — grammatically incorrect.
• 'Shall we?' = tag for 'Let's...' sentences only. 'Shall you' = wrong form entirely.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Students confuse 'shall we?' (Let's tag) with 'shall you' — 'shall you' is never used!
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: PLEASE = WILL YOU PLEASE? → tag = WILL YOU! Please ഉണ്ടെങ്കിൽ 'will you' — ഈ pair ഓർക്കൂ!
• Imperative tag rules — one table:
  Please / Do this → will you?
  Let's → shall we?
  Don't → will you?
• PSC exam strategy: Sentence-ൽ 'Please' കണ്ടാൽ → tag = 'will you?' automatic! 'Let's' കണ്ടാൽ → 'shall we?' automatic! ഈ two rules ഓർത്തിരുന്നാൽ imperative tag questions guaranteed mark!

questionText: Had I not seen this with my own eyes, I _____________ it.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തరം: (D) would not have believed

• 'Had I not seen this with my own eyes' = Third Conditional inverted form. Normal form: 'If I had not seen...'
• Inverted Third Conditional: 'Had + subject + V3' = 'If + subject + had + V3' — same meaning, formal style.
• Third Conditional formula: If + had + V3 → would have + V3. Negative: would NOT have + V3.
• 'Had I not seen..., I would not have believed it' = 'ഞാൻ സ്വന്തം കണ്ണുകൊണ്ട് കണ്ടില്ലായിരുന്നെങ്കിൽ, ഞാൻ വിശ്വസിച്ചേനേ ഇല്ല' — past unreal situation, correct Third Conditional result.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula:
  Normal: If + had + V3 → would have + V3 (Third Conditional)
  Inverted: Had + subject + V3 → would have + V3 (same meaning, formal)
• Inverted Conditionals — PSC favourite pattern:
  Had I known... = If I had known...
  Were she here... = If she were here...
  Should you need... = If you should need...
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Inverted Third Conditional PSC-ൽ ഒരു popular pattern — 'Had I...' form കണ്ടാൽ Third Conditional ആണ്.
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. Had she studied harder, she would have passed. (= If she had studied harder...)
  2. Had they left earlier, they would have caught the train.
  3. Had I known the answer, I would have told you.
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

(A) would believe
• 'Would believe' = Second Conditional result (present imaginary). 'Had I not seen' = past (Third Conditional) — tense mismatch.
• Second Conditional: If + Past Simple → would + V1. Third Conditional: If + Past Perfect → would HAVE + V3.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Would' ഉണ്ട് — 'would have' vs 'would' distinction miss ആകും. 'Had' = past perfect = Third Conditional = 'would have'!

(B) will believe
• 'Will believe' = First Conditional / future — 'Had I not seen' = past perfect, future tense completely wrong.
• 'Had' = past perfect indicator → result must be past-based ('would have'), not future ('will').
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Positive form confuse ആകും — 'had' = past, 'will' = future, cannot mix!

(C) will not have believed
• 'Will not have believed' = Future Perfect — conditional sentences-ൽ 'will' use ആകില്ല, 'would' ആണ് conditional modal.
• 'Will' = factual future; 'would' = conditional/hypothetical. Third Conditional = 'would have', never 'will have'.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Not have believed' part correct — but 'will' is wrong modal. Must be 'WOULD not have believed'!
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: HAD at the start (inverted) = Third Conditional = WOULD HAVE in result! See 'Had I...' → write 'would have' automatically!
• Inverted conditional signal: Sentence starts with 'Had', 'Were', 'Should' = it's an inverted conditional (no 'if' needed)!
• PSC exam strategy:
  'Had + subject + V3' → result = 'would have + V3' (positive) or 'would not have + V3' (negative)
  Here: 'Had I NOT seen' = negative condition → result also negative = 'would NOT have believed' ✓

questionText: __Hazardous__ means

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (A) dangerous

• 'Hazardous' = involving risk of harm, dangerous, unsafe — 'അപകടകരമായ' എന്നർഥം.
• 'Hazardous waste / hazardous chemicals / hazardous conditions' — ഇവ real-life contexts-ൽ 'dangerous' meaning-ൽ ഉപയോഗിക്കുന്നു.
• Etymology: Arabic 'az-zahr' = dice/chance → Old French 'hasard' = game of chance → English 'hazard' = risk/danger.
• 'Hazardous' = adjective form of 'hazard' — danger/risk ഉള്ള = hazardous = dangerous. Perfect synonym.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: HAZARDOUS = HAZARD + -ous = full of hazard = DANGEROUS
• Word family: hazard (noun/verb) → hazardous (adjective) → hazardously (adverb)
• Synonyms of 'hazardous/dangerous' (PSC vocabulary family):
  hazardous, dangerous, perilous, risky, treacherous, precarious, unsafe
• Antonyms: safe, harmless, secure, protected
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: '-ous' adjective synonyms PSC-ൽ ഇടയ്ക്ക് — hazardous, courageous, treacherous, conspicuous.
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. Mining is a hazardous occupation.
  2. The chemicals were too hazardous to handle without protection.
  3. She navigated the hazardous mountain road carefully.
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

(B) hypothetical
• 'Hypothetical' = based on assumption/supposition, not real — 'hazardous' = real danger, not assumption.
• 'Hypothetical situation' = imaginary; 'hazardous situation' = real and dangerous.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Both are adjectives, both are academic-sounding — meaning completely different. Hazardous = real danger!

(C) mysterious
• 'Mysterious' = full of mystery, unknown, strange — 'hazardous' = dangerous, not mysterious.
• Something can be mysterious without being dangerous, and vice versa.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Mysterious' ≈ exciting/scary feel — but hazardous = specifically dangerous/risky, not mysterious.

(D) flexible
• 'Flexible' = capable of bending, adaptable — complete opposite context from 'hazardous'.
• No connection between flexibility and danger.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Easy eliminate — 'flexible' = completely unrelated meaning. Students should eliminate this first.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: HAZARD = think of HAZARD WARNING signs ⚠️ on roads and chemical containers! HAZARDOUS = having those warning signs = DANGEROUS!
• '-ous' adjective formula: noun + '-ous' = full of that quality
  hazard + ous = hazardous (full of hazard = dangerous)
  danger + ous = dangerous
  courage + ous = courageous (full of courage)
