• PSC exam strategy: 'Hazardous' = dangerous/risky — first option is always the direct synonym. Eliminate unrelated options (flexible, mysterious) first, then verify between remaining ones.

questionText: The correct word for 'Tending to change'

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (B) Changeable

• 'Changeable' = correct spelling. 'Change' + '-able' suffix → 'changeable'.
• Spelling rule: 'change' ends in '-ge'. '-ge' or '-ce' ending words-ൽ '-able' add ചെയ്യുമ്പോൾ 'e' retain ചെയ്യണം — soft 'g'/'c' sound preserve ചെയ്യാൻ.
• 'Chang-e-able' = 'e' keep ചെയ്തതുകൊണ്ട് 'g' soft sound (j sound) remain ആകുന്നു. 'e' remove ചെയ്താൽ 'changable' = 'g' hard sound ആകും (wrong pronunciation).
• 'Changeable weather / changeable mood' — ഇടയ്ക്കിടെ മാറുന്ന = changeable.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Word ends in '-ce' or '-ge' + '-able' → KEEP the 'e'!
  change → changeable ✓ (keep 'e' — soft 'g')
  notice → noticeable ✓ (keep 'e' — soft 'c')
  manage → manageable ✓ (keep 'e' — soft 'g')
  BUT: love → lovable ✗ loveable (no '-ce'/'-ge', drop 'e')
• Normal '-able' rule: drop 'e' before '-able' (like, lovable; use, usable)
• Exception: '-ce'/'-ge' words = keep 'e' to preserve soft sound
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Spelling — 'changeable, noticeable, manageable' PSC-ൽ favourite spelling trap.
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. The weather here is very changeable.
  2. Her mood is changeable — happy one minute, sad the next.
  3. The terms of the contract are changeable.
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

(A) Changeble
• 'Changeble' = missing the 'a' in '-able' suffix. Correct suffix = '-able', not '-ble'.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Quick reading-ൽ miss ആകും — '-able' full suffix ആണ്, '-ble' അല്ല.

(C) Changable
• 'Changable' = 'e' drop ചെയ്ത് '-able' add ചെയ്തു — 'change' ends in '-ge', so 'e' must be retained!
• ഇതാണ് most common mistake — normal rule (drop 'e') apply ചെയ്ത് wrong ആകുന്നു.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: ഇതാണ് classic spelling trap! 'Change + able' → students 'e' drop ചെയ്ത് 'changable' write ചെയ്യും. '-ge' ending = 'e' keep ചെയ്യണം!

(D) Changaeble
• 'Changaeble' = 'ae' wrong insertion — completely wrong spelling, no such form exists.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Easy eliminate — 'ae' combination ഇവിടെ ഒരു rule-ഉം ഇല്ല.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: '-GE' and '-CE' words need their 'E' for a reason — without 'e', the 'g' becomes hard (like in 'gap')! So CHANGE + E + ABLE = CHANGEABLE. The 'e' is a GUARD protecting the soft 'g' sound!
• '-ge'/'-ce' + able family (always keep 'e'):
  changeable | noticeable | manageable | peaceable | traceable | serviceable
• PSC exam strategy: '-able' spelling question → first check if word ends in '-ge' or '-ce'. If yes → keep 'e' → changeable ✓. If normal word → drop 'e' → lovable, usable.

questionText: Neither food nor water ____________ to be found there.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (A) was

• 'Neither food nor water ___' — 'neither...nor' correlative conjunction ഉള്ള sentence-ൽ verb subject-ന്റ nearest (second) subject-നോട് agree ചെയ്യും.
• Nearer subject: 'water' = uncountable noun = singular → singular verb = 'was'.
• Past tense context: 'to be found there' — past situation describe ചെയ്യുന്നു → 'was' (singular past) correct.
• 'Neither food nor water was to be found there' = 'അവിടെ ഭക്ഷണവും വെള്ളവും ഒന്നും ഉണ്ടായിരുന്നില്ല' — perfect meaning.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Neither...nor / Either...or → verb agrees with the NEARER (second) subject
  Neither [food] nor [water] → agree with 'water' (singular) → was ✓
  Neither [dogs] nor [cat] → agree with 'cat' (singular) → was ✓
  Neither [cat] nor [dogs] → agree with 'dogs' (plural) → were ✓
• 'Food' and 'water' = both uncountable (always singular) → 'was'
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Neither...nor subject-verb agreement = PSC-ൽ ഒരു favourite question type!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. Neither the teacher nor the students were present.
  2. Neither she nor he was responsible.
  3. Neither the boys nor the girl was late.
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

(B) have
• 'Have' = plural present auxiliary — 'water' = singular, past context. Double error: number + tense wrong.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Neither...nor' = two subjects feel → plural 'have' pick ചെയ്യും. Rule: nearer subject = water (singular) = was!

(C) are
• 'Are' = plural present — 'water' = singular (nearer subject), past context. Again double error.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Food AND water' ആണെങ്കിൽ 'are' correct — 'NOR' = nearer subject rule. 'AND' ≠ 'NOR'!

(D) were
• 'Were' = plural past — 'water' = singular (nearer subject) → singular past 'was' ആണ് correct.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: ഇതാണ് most common trap — 'were' past tense feel ഉണ്ട്, plural form ആണ്. 'Water' singular → 'was' singular!
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: Neither...NOR — look at the word NEAREST to the blank (after 'nor')! That word's number = verb's number! NOR → NEAREST subject wins!
• Neither/Either rule in one line:
  Neither A nor B → verb agrees with B (nearer)
  Either A or B → verb agrees with B (nearer)
• PSC exam strategy: 'Neither...nor' question-ൽ → 'nor'-ൻ്റെ right side subject നോക്കൂ → singular ആണോ plural ആണോ → accordingly verb. ഇവിടെ 'water' = singular = 'was'. Guaranteed mark!

topic: Uses of Primary and Modal Auxiliary Verbs
questionText: Select the correct sentence:
optionA: You need not to do it
optionD: You need not it to do

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (C) You need not do it

• 'Need' as a modal auxiliary — 'need not' = no obligation. Modal ആയി ഉപയോഗിക്കുമ്പോൾ 'to' ആവശ്യമില്ല.
• Modal 'need' formula: need not + bare infinitive (V1 without 'to').
• 'You need not do it' = 'നിങ്ങൾ അത് ചെയ്യേണ്ടതില്ല' — obligation ഇല്ല എന്ന് express ചെയ്യുന്നു.
• 'Need not' (modal) = 'do not need to' (main verb) — same meaning, different structure. Modal form-ൽ 'to' ഉണ്ടാകില്ല.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula:
  Modal 'need': need not + V1 (no 'to') → "You need not do it" ✓
  Main verb 'need': do not need + to + V1 → "You do not need to do it" ✓
  WRONG: need not + to + V1 → "You need not to do it" ✗
• 'Need' = two uses in English:
  1. Modal auxiliary (like can/must): need not do, need you do?
  2. Main verb (like want): don't need to do, do you need to do?
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Modal 'need not' vs 'need not to' — PSC-ൽ ഒരു classic correct sentence question.
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. You need not worry about it. (modal — no 'to')
  2. You do not need to worry. (main verb — with 'to')
  3. Need I explain further? (modal question form)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

(A) You need not to do it
• 'Need not to do' = wrong! Modal 'need not' + 'to' = incorrect combination. Modal-ൻ്റെ ശേഷം 'to' ഉണ്ടാകില്ല.
• ഇതാണ് ഏറ്റവും common mistake — students 'need not to' natural ആണ് എന്ന് think ചെയ്യും.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: ഇതാണ് number one trap! 'Need not TO' = mixing modal form with main verb form = WRONG. Modal = no 'to'!

(B) You not to do it
• 'You not to do it' = incomplete, no auxiliary verb — grammatically meaningless sentence.
• Auxiliary ('need', 'do', 'must' etc.) ഇല്ലാതെ negative sentence ഉണ്ടാക്കാൻ കഴിയില്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Easy eliminate — no auxiliary = always wrong.

(D) You need not it to do
• 'Need not it to do' = completely wrong word order — English sentence structure: Subject + Verb + Object.
• 'It' = object, object verb-ൻ്റെ ശേഷം വരണം, മുൻപ് അല്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Easy eliminate — word order completely scrambled.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: NEED NOT = CANNOT with 'to'! All modals (can, must, should, need not) are followed by bare infinitive — NO 'TO'! Remember: MODAL = NO 'TO'!
• Modal auxiliaries — all take bare infinitive (no 'to'):
  can do | must do | should do | need not do | may do | will do
• PSC exam strategy: 'Need not' question-ൽ → look for option WITHOUT 'to' after 'need not'. 'Need not do' ✓ vs 'need not TO do' ✗. The 'to' = trap!

questionText: Which is the word that means __based on what seems to be so without further or deeper investigation__?

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (C) Prima facie

• 'Prima facie' = Latin. 'Prima' = first + 'facie' = face/appearance → 'at first face/glance' = based on initial appearance, without deeper investigation.
• 'Based on what seems to be so without further or deeper investigation' = prima facie evidence, prima facie case — ആദ്യ നോട്ടത്തിൽ correct ആണ് എന്ന് തോന്നുന്നത്.
• Legal context-ൽ ഇടയ്ക്ക് ഉപയോഗിക്കുന്നു: 'prima facie case' = evidence that appears sufficient at first sight.
• 'Prima facie, the accused seems guilty' = 'ആദ്യ തെളിവുകൾ നോക്കുമ്പോൾ, accused guilty ആണ് എന്ന് തോന്നുന്നു.'
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: PRIMA FACIE = at FIRST FACE = based on FIRST APPEARANCE (without deeper investigation)
• Etymology: Latin — 'prima' = first + 'facie' = face (ablative of 'facies')
• PSC-ൽ ഇടയ്ക്ക് ചോദിക്കുന്ന Latin legal phrases:
  prima facie = at first appearance / based on initial evidence
  ad hoc = for this specific purpose only (temporary arrangement)
  ex gratia = as a favour, not legally required (ex gratia payment)
  mutatis mutandis = with necessary changes having been made
  bona fide = in good faith, genuine
  sine die = without a fixed future date (adjournment sine die)
  status quo = the existing state of affairs
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Latin legal phrases PSC-ൽ favourite — prima facie, ad hoc, bona fide, sine die ഇടയ്ക്ക് ചോദിക്കുന്നു.
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. The police have a prima facie case against the suspect.
  2. Prima facie, the documents appear genuine.
  3. The court found prima facie evidence of fraud.
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

(A) ad hoc
• 'Ad hoc' = Latin 'for this purpose' — temporary, arranged for a specific purpose only. 'Ad hoc committee' = specific purpose-ന് only ഉണ്ടാക്കിയ committee.
• 'Without deeper investigation' idea ഇതിൽ ഇല്ല — 'ad hoc' = temporary/specific purpose.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Both Latin — different meanings. 'Ad hoc' = specific purpose, 'prima facie' = first appearance.

(B) ex gratia
• 'Ex gratia' = Latin 'from favour' — payment/action done as a gift/favour, not because legally obligated. 'Ex gratia payment' = legal obligation ഇല്ലാതെ goodwill-ൽ pay ചെയ്യുന്ന amount.
• 'Without investigation' meaning ഇല്ല — 'ex gratia' = voluntary favour.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Legal feel ഉണ്ട് — but 'ex gratia' = favour/gift meaning, 'prima facie' = first appearance meaning.

(D) mutatis mutandis
• 'Mutatis mutandis' = Latin 'with necessary changes having been made' — applying something to a new situation with appropriate modifications.
• 'First appearance/investigation' idea ഇല്ല — 'mutatis mutandis' = adapting with changes.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Impressive-sounding Latin phrase — students unfamiliar pick ചെയ്യും. Meaning: 'with modifications', not 'first appearance'.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: PRIMA FACIE = PRIMA (primary/first) + FACE = FIRST FACE impression! Like judging someone by their FACE alone without knowing them deeper = prima facie!
• Latin phrases group — memorize meanings together:
  prima facie = first face = at first appearance
  ad hoc = for this = specific purpose only
  ex gratia = from grace = as a favour
  bona fide = good faith = genuine
  sine die = without day = no fixed date
  mutatis mutandis = changed things changed = with modifications
• PSC exam strategy: 'Without further investigation / at first glance / initial appearance' = always prima facie!

topic: Types of Sentences and Interchange of Sentences
questionText:
Transform the given sentence without any change of meaning:
__O that I were young again!__
optionA: I wish I was young again
optionB: I wish that I were young again
optionC: I was young again and that was my wish
optionD: My wish is that I would be young again

explanation.correct:
✅ ശরিയുത്തരം: (B) I wish that I were young again

• 'O that I were young again!' = exclamatory wish sentence (subjunctive mood). 'O that...' = strong desire/wish express ചെയ്യുന്ന exclamatory form.
• Transformation: 'O that + subject + were...' → 'I wish that + subject + were...'
• 'Were' (not 'was') = subjunctive mood — wish/unreal situation-ൽ all persons-ൽ 'were' ഉപയോഗിക്കുന്നു (I were, he were, she were — not 'was').
• 'I wish that I were young again' = 'ഞാൻ വീണ്ടും യൗവ്വനക്കാരനാകണമെന്ന് ആഗ്രഹിക്കുന്നു' — same meaning, different structure.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula:
  'O that + subject + were...' → 'I wish that + subject + were...'
  Wish + unreal present → were (subjunctive, NOT was — for ALL persons)
• Subjunctive 'were' rule: 'wish' express ചെയ്യുമ്പോൾ 'was' ഉപയോഗിക്കില്ല — always 'were':
  I wish I were taller. ✓ (NOT: I wish I was taller ✗ — informal only)
  I wish she were here. ✓
  O that he were king! → I wish that he were king. ✓
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: 'O that...' exclamatory wish transformation PSC-ൽ ഇടയ്ക്ക് — subjunctive 'were' = key rule.
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. O that I were a bird! → I wish that I were a bird.
  2. O that she were here! → I wish that she were here.
  3. O that we had more time! → I wish that we had more time.
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

(A) I wish I was young again
• 'Was' instead of 'were' = subjunctive mood ignore ചെയ്തു. Formal/written English-ൽ wish + unreal = 'were', 'was' അല്ല.
• PSC exams formal English test ചെയ്യുന്നു — 'were' ആണ് correct subjunctive form.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: ഇതാണ് most common trap! 'I wish I was' = spoken English-ൽ common — but formal/exam English = 'I wish I WERE'. 'That' missing ആണ്, 'were' ആണ് correct!

(C) I was young again and that was my wish
• Complete meaning change — 'I was young again' = ഞാൻ young ആയിരുന്നു (fact), but original = wish/desire (not a fact).
• Structure totally wrong — compound sentence ആണ്, wish sentence അല്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'young again' + 'wish' words ഉണ്ട് — meaning completely different, fact statement ആക്കി മാറ്റി.

(D) My wish is that I would be young again
• 'Would be' = conditional mood — 'wish' sentences-ൽ 'would' ഉപയോഗിക്കില്ല. 'Were' = subjunctive ആണ് ആവശ്യം.
• 'I wish + would' = grammatically incorrect — 'I wish' + past subjunctive (were/had) ആണ് correct form.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Would' formal feel ഉണ്ട് — 'wish + would' = wrong combination. Wish = were (subjunctive)!
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'O that I WERE' = 'I WISH I WERE' — WERE follows both! 'O that' exclamatory → 'I wish that' statement, and WERE stays the same in both! Just swap 'O that' for 'I wish that'!
• Transformation formula:
  'O that + subject + were + ...' → 'I wish that + subject + were + ...'
  No other changes needed except replacing 'O that' with 'I wish that'!
• PSC exam strategy: 'O that I were...' → answer always has 'I wish that I WERE' (with 'were', not 'was', not 'would be'). Eliminate 'was' (A) and 'would be' (D) first — both wrong!

questionText: Either you or I _____ to choose the post.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (B) am

• "Either you or I" — ഈ structure-ൽ verb, nearest subject ആയ "I"-നോട് agree ചെയ്യണം.
• "I am" — "I" subject ആകുമ്പോൾ always "am" ആണ് ഉപയോഗിക്കുക.
• Either...or → verb agrees with the CLOSEST subject to the verb.
• ഇവിടെ "I" nearest ആണ്, so "am" ഉത്തരം — guaranteed mark!
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Either A or B → verb agrees with B (nearest subject)
• Either you or I am → I nearest → am ✓
• Either he or they are → they nearest → are ✓
• Either they or he is → he nearest → is ✓
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Either...or / Neither...nor subject-verb agreement PSC top favourite!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. Either you or she is responsible.
  2. Neither he nor I am guilty.
  3. Either the boys or the girl is late.
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) are — "Are" plural subject-ന് ഉപയോഗിക്കുന്നു. "I" subject ആകുമ്പോൾ "am" ആണ്, "are" അല്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "you" കണ്ടാൽ "are" എഴുതും — but nearest subject "I" ആണ്, "you" അല്ല!

❌ (C) was — "Was" past tense ആണ്. Sentence present/future context-ൽ ആണ് ("to choose"), so past wrong.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Tense confusion — sentence-ൽ past meaning feel ആകും but tense check ചെയ്ക്കണം.

❌ (D) were — "Were" plural past tense ആണ്. Subject plural അല്ല, tense past അല്ല — doubly wrong!
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Either...or → plural verb വേണം എന്ന് തോന്നും — but nearest subject rule apply ചെയ്ക്കണം!
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: "Either...or — look at the DOOR (last/nearest word)" — nearest subject = verb partner!
• Word family: Either...or | Neither...nor | Not only...but also — all follow nearest subject rule.
• PSC exam strategy: Either/Neither question കണ്ടാൽ → or/nor-ന് ശേഷമുള്ള subject നോക്കൂ → അതിനോട് verb match ചെയ്യും!

questionText:
Choose the passive form of the sentence.
You need not submit the document.
optionB: The document need to be submitted
optionC: The document need not be submitted

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (C) The document need not be submitted

• Active: "You need not submit the document"
• Passive-ൽ object (document) → subject ആകും, subject (you) → by you ആകും.
• "need not submit" (Active) → "need not be submitted" (Passive).
• Modal passive formula: Modal + be + V3 (past participle).
• "Submit" → past participle "submitted" — ഇതാണ് correct passive form.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Modal + V1 (Active) → Modal + be + V3 (Passive)
• need not submit → need not be submitted ✓
• can do → can be done | must finish → must be finished | should pay → should be paid
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Modal passive voice PSC-ൽ frequently ചോദിക്കും!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. You must complete the work. → The work must be completed.
  2. She can solve the problem. → The problem can be solved by her.
  3. They need not pay the fee. → The fee need not be paid.
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) The document need not submit — Passive-ൽ main verb V3 (past participle) ആകണം. "Submit" V1 ആണ് — active form ഇവിടെ wrong.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "need not" ശേഷം bare infinitive (active pattern) passive-ൽ confuse ചെയ്യും.

❌ (B) The document need to be submitted — "need not" → "need to be" ആക്കിയാൽ meaning മാറുന്നു. "Need not" = no obligation; "need to" = obligation — opposite meaning!
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "need to be" professionally correct feel — but negation "not" miss ചെയ്യരുത്!

❌ (D) None of the above — Option C correct ആണ്, so "None of the above" wrong.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "None of the above" uncertain ആകുമ്പോൾ choose ചെയ്യും — ഇവിടെ C correct!
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: "Modal Passive = Modal + BE + V3" — "MBV3" formula ഓർക്കൂ!
• Word family: can→can be done | must→must be done | need not→need not be done | should→should be done
• PSC exam strategy: Modal passive questions-ൽ → "be" ഉണ്ടോ? V3 ആണോ V1 ആണോ? — രണ്ടും check ചെയ്ക്കൂ!

questionText: They were not ashamed _____ hug each other.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (A) to

• "Ashamed to + infinitive" — ഇത് ഒരു fixed collocation ആണ്.
• "They were not ashamed to hug each other" = "hug ചെയ്യാൻ അവർക്ക് നാണമില്ലായിരുന്നു."
• "Hug" ഇവിടെ bare infinitive (V1) ആണ് — action express ചെയ്യുന്നു.
• Action express ചെയ്യുമ്പോൾ: ashamed + TO + infinitive.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: ashamed + TO + infinitive (action) | ashamed + OF + noun/gerund (thing/person)
• ashamed to do = ഒരു action ചെയ്യാൻ നാണം | ashamed of = ഒരു thing/person-നെ കുറിച്ച് നാണം
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Adjective + preposition collocations PSC top favourite!
• Similar adjective + preposition pairs:
  proud TO win (action) | proud OF results (thing)
  afraid TO speak (action) | afraid OF dogs (thing)
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. She was ashamed to admit her mistake.
  2. He was ashamed of his behaviour.
  3. They were not ashamed to ask for help.
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (B) about — "Ashamed about" standard collocation അല്ല. "Worried about", "curious about" ശരിയാണ്, but "ashamed about" — wrong preposition.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "about" common preposition ആണ്, casual ആയി feel ആകും — but "ashamed" + about = wrong!

❌ (C) of — "Ashamed of + noun/gerund" ശരിയാണ്, but ഇവിടെ "hug" bare infinitive (V1) ആണ്, gerund (hugging) അല്ല. Bare infinitive ശേഷം "of" ഉപയോഗിക്കില്ല — "to" വേണം.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "ashamed of" real pattern ആണ്, confusing — context (infinitive vs gerund) check ചെയ്ക്കൂ.

❌ (D) off — "Ashamed off" collocation ഇല്ല. "Off" ഇവിടെ fully wrong.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "of" vs "off" — spelling trap, ഒരേ sound feel ആകും!
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: "TO DO action → ashamed TO | OF a THING → ashamed OF" — action vs thing!
• Word family: proud of/to | afraid of/to | ashamed of/to | happy to | sorry for — adjective + preposition family!
• PSC exam strategy: Blank ശേഷം verb (infinitive) ആണോ? → "to" | noun/gerund ആണോ? → "of"!

topic: Types of Sentences and Interchange of Sentences
questionText: 'Get ready for the descent' is an example of:

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (A) Imperative sentence

• "Get ready for the descent" — ഇത് ഒരു command/instruction ആണ്.
• Imperative sentence = command, request, advice, instruction കൊടുക്കുന്ന sentence.
• Subject "you" hidden ആണ്, verb "Get" ൽ sentence ആരംഭിക്കുന്നു — ഇതാണ് imperative-ന്റെ hallmark.
• "Descent" = ഇറക്കം/landing — "landing-ന് ready ആകൂ" എന്ന order ആണ്.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Base verb (V1) ൽ start + subject "you" hidden = Imperative sentence!
• 4 Types of sentences:
  Assertive/Declarative = fact (.) | Interrogative = question (?) | Imperative = command (.) | Exclamatory = emotion (!)
• Imperative examples: Close the door. | Please help me. | Don't run. | Be quiet.
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Sentence types — especially Imperative vs Exclamatory — PSC repeated question!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. Sit down. (command)
  2. Please be quiet. (request)
  3. Be careful on the road. (advice)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (B) Exclamatory sentence — Exclamatory sentences strong emotion express ചെയ്യുന്നു (joy, surprise, sorrow). "What a day!" / "How clever!" — exclamation mark ഉണ്ടാകും. "Get ready" emotion അല്ല, command ആണ്.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "Descent" = risky/dramatic → emotion feel ആകും — but verb form check ചെയ്ക്കൂ!

❌ (C) Interrogative sentence — Interrogative sentences questions ചോദിക്കുന്നു: "Are you ready?" — question mark ഉണ്ടാകും. "Get ready" ഒരു question അല്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Command ചോദ്യ രൂപത്തിൽ confuse ആകും — but no question mark here!

❌ (D) Declarative sentence — Declarative sentences facts state ചെയ്യുന്നു: "She is ready." — subject + verb ആദ്യം ഉണ്ടാകും. "Get ready" — subject hidden, base verb ആദ്യം — Declarative pattern അല്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "Get ready" = "You get ready" = statement ആണ് എന്ന് confuse ആകും.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: "Subject hidden + Base verb first = Imperative!" — you invisible = imperative!
• Word family: 4 types — A(ssertive)=fact | I(nterrogative)=? | I(mperative)=command | E(xclamatory)=! — remember AIIE!
• PSC exam strategy: First word base verb (V1) ആണോ? Subject hidden ആണോ? → Imperative, guaranteed!

questionText: She will _____ the position of a manager tomorrow.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (D) take up

• "Take up a position" = ഒരു job/role ഏറ്റെടുക്കുക, ആരംഭിക്കുക.
• "She will take up the position of a manager tomorrow" = "അവൾ നാളെ manager ആയ position ഏറ്റെടുക്കും."
• "Take up" = to begin/assume a new role, job, or responsibility — career context-ൽ perfect fit.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Take UP = begin/assume a role or hobby | Take AFTER = resemble | Take IN = deceive/absorb | Take OUT = remove
• "Take up" meanings:
  1. Start a new job/role: She took up the post of principal.
  2. Start a hobby: He took up painting after retirement.
  3. Occupy space/time: This project takes up too much time.
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: "Take" phrasal verbs PSC-ൽ repeatedly ചോദിക്കും — all variants പഠിക്കൂ!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. She will take up the position of Director next month.
  2. He took up cycling as a hobby.
  3. The new project will take up all our resources.
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) take in — "Take in" = deceive someone OR understand/absorb information. "I was taken in by his lies." Position ഏറ്റെടുക്കുക = ഈ meaning ഇല്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "in a position" = logical feel — but take IN ≠ take up a position!

❌ (B) take out — "Take out" = remove ചെയ്ക്കൽ OR restaurant food takeaway. "She took out the books." Position assume ചെയ്ക്കൽ = ഈ meaning ഇല്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "take out" = begin work, get started ആണ് എന്ന് confuse ആകും — but wrong meaning!

❌ (C) take after — "Take after" = resemble a parent/relative. "She takes after her mother." Position ഏറ്റെടുക്കൽ = ഈ meaning ഇല്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "After" = come after/succeed ആണ് feeling — but take after = resemble, not succeed!
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: "Take UP = Step UP into a new role!" — going UP in career = take up!
• Word family: take up | take in | take out | take after | take off | take over — "take" family PSC favourite!
• PSC exam strategy: Job/role/hobby ആരംഭിക്കൽ context = always "take up" — career + blank = check take up first!

questionText: Choose the correctly spelt word.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (C) Manoeuvre

• "Manoeuvre" — British English correct spelling. French "manoeuvrer" ൽ നിന്ന് വന്നത്.
• Meaning: a skilled movement or strategic action (കൗശലപൂർണ്ണമായ ചലനം/strategy).
• Kerala PSC British English follow ചെയ്യുന്നു — "Manoeuvre" ആണ് correct.
• Letter order: m-a-n-o-e-u-v-r-e — "oe" BEFORE "u", "re" at end.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: man + oeuvre = Manoeuvre (British) | American: Maneuver (simplified)
• Word origin: French "manoeuvrer" = to work with hands (manu = hand, oeuvre = work)
• Kerala PSC = British English — always prefer British spelling!
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: French-origin tricky spelling words PSC favourite trap!
• Related words: manoeuvre → manoeuvred | manoeuvring | manoeuvres
• Letter order tip: m-a-n-O-E-U-v-r-e → OE before U!
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) Manoevure — Letters transposed: "oeuvre" ആണ് correct, ഇതിൽ "evure" ആണ് — order wrong.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Sound-based spelling — "oeuvr" sound confuse ചെയ്യും, transposition easy to miss!

❌ (B) Manoeuver — American English pattern-ൽ നിന്ന് influenced. But "Manoeuver" neither correct British nor American — end-ൽ "re" ആണ് വേണ്ടത്, "er" അല്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: American "Maneuver" അറിയാം, so "Manoeuver" = hybrid guess — wrong!

❌ (D) Maneouvre — "oe" and "eo" transposed: "man-eo-uvre" instead of "man-oe-uvre" — wrong order.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: All options confusingly similar — letter by letter slow ആയി read ചെയ്ക്കൂ!
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: "MAN did OEUVRE (French artwork) = MAN-OEUVRE!" — man + French word oeuvre!
• Word family: Manoeuvre | Connoisseur | Entrepreneur | Chauffeur — French-origin spellings PSC love!
• PSC exam strategy: Similar options-ൽ letter ORDER check ചെയ്ക്കൂ: m-a-n-O-E-u-v-r-e — OE BEFORE U, RE at end!

questionText: If I knew her address, I _____ a letter to her.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (C) could write

• "If I knew her address, I _____ a letter to her" — Second Conditional ആണ്.
• If-clause: "If I knew" = past simple → present unreal situation (address ഇല്ല = reality).
• Main clause formula: would/could + bare infinitive (V1).
• "I could write" = "ഞാൻ ഒരു കത്ത് എഴുതുമായിരുന്നു" — ഇപ്പോൾ address ഇല്ലാത്തതിനാൽ write ചെയ്യുന്നില്ല.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: 2nd Conditional: If + Past Simple → would/could + V1
• Present unreal / imaginary situation → Second Conditional
• "knew" (past simple, not past perfect) → 2nd Conditional, not 3rd.
• 3rd Conditional: If + had + V3 → would/could have + V3 (past unreal).
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: 2nd vs 3rd Conditional PSC top question — always check if-clause tense!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. If I had money, I would buy a car.
  2. If she studied hard, she could pass.
  3. If it rained, we would cancel the trip.
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) wrote — "Wrote" past simple ആണ്. Main clause-ൽ bare past tense ഉപയോഗിക്കില്ല conditional sentence-ൽ — modal (would/could) + V1 ആണ് വേണ്ടത്.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: If-clause past ആയതിനാൽ main clause-ഉം past = "wrote" ആണ് എന്ന് confuse ആകും.

❌ (B) could written — "Could" modal ശേഷം bare infinitive (V1) ആണ് വേണ്ടത്, V3 അല്ല. "Could written" grammatically impossible.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "could have written" (3rd Conditional) pattern-ൽ V3 വരും — but "could" alone + V3 wrong!

❌ (D) could have written — 3rd Conditional main clause pattern ആണ്: If + had known → could have written. But "If I knew" = past simple (2nd Conditional), past perfect (had known) അല്ല — so "could have" wrong here.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "could have + V3" sophisticated feel, students choose it — but if-clause tense check ചെയ്ക്കണം!
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: "KNEW = 2nd, HAD KNOWN = 3rd" — if-clause past simple → 2nd; past perfect → 3rd!
• Word family: 2nd: If + past → would/could + V1 | 3rd: If + had + V3 → would/could have + V3
• PSC exam strategy: If-clause tense ആദ്യം check ചെയ്ക്കൂ → past simple = 2nd Conditional → main clause = modal + V1!

questionText:
Synonyms of 'succumb':
i) yield  ii) relent  iii) revolt  iv) conquer

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (C) Both i and ii

• "Succumb" = yield/surrender to something stronger — "പ്രതിരോധിക്കാൻ കഴിയാതെ ഇടിഞ്ഞുവീഴുക."
• i) Yield = surrender, give way → succumb-ന്റെ synonym ✓
• ii) Relent = become less strict/firm, to yield → succumb-ന്റെ synonym ✓
• iii) Revolt = rebel, rise against → succumb-ന്റെ ANTONYM ✗
• iv) Conquer = defeat, overcome → succumb-ന്റെ ANTONYM ✗
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Succumb = yield + relent (give in) | Revolt + Conquer = antonyms (fight back)!
• Root: Latin "succumbere" = to fall under (sub = under + cumbere = to lie)
• Synonyms of succumb: yield | relent | surrender | capitulate | submit
• Antonyms of succumb: revolt | resist | conquer | overcome | fight
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Synonym combo questions (choose which are synonyms from a list) — PSC pattern!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. She finally succumbed to the pressure and resigned.
  2. He succumbed to the temptation of sweets.
  3. The patient succumbed to the illness. (= died from it)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) i, ii and iii — "Revolt" (iii) = rebel/rise against = succumb-ന്റെ OPPOSITE. iii ചേർക്കുന്നത് wrong.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "relent" and "revolt" — both 'r' ൽ start, sound similar — confuse ആകും!

❌ (B) Both iii and iv — Revolt = rebel, Conquer = overcome — both ANTONYMS of succumb. ഒരു synonym-ഉം ഇതിൽ ഇല്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "Conquer" = power/victory feel — wrong direction, succumb = loser/surrender side!

❌ (D) All the above — Revolt and Conquer antonyms ആണ്. Antonyms ഉൾക്കൊള്ളുന്ന "All" option ഒരിക്കലും correct ആകില്ല ഇവിടെ.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "All of the above" = confident-ആയി feel ആകും, but antonym mix-in ഉണ്ടോ check ചെയ്ക്കൂ!
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: "Succumb = Succumbed UNDER pressure" = give in, surrender — opposite of victory!
• Word family: Synonyms = yield, relent, surrender, capitulate | Antonyms = resist, conquer, revolt, overcome
• PSC exam strategy: Synonym combo questions-ൽ — antonym ഒന്ന് കണ്ടാൽ അത് ഉൾക്കൊള്ളുന്ന option eliminate ചെയ്ക്കൂ!

questionText:
Change to reported speech:
I asked him, "Can you join the team today?"
optionA: I asked him if he can join the team today.
optionB: I asked him that can he join the team that day.
optionC: I asked him if he joined the team on that day.
optionD: I asked him if he could join the team on that day.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (D) I asked him if he could join the team on that day.

• Direct: I asked him, "Can you join the team today?"
• Yes/No question ആണ് → reported speech-ൽ "if/whether" ഉപയോഗിക്കണം.
• 3 changes: (1) Can → could (modal backshift) (2) you → he (pronoun change) (3) today → that day (time change).
• Word order: statement order (he could join), question order (could he join) അല്ല.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Yes/No question → if/whether + statement order + backshift + time change
• Modal backshift: can → could | will → would | shall → should | may → might
• Time changes: today → that day | now → then | tomorrow → the next day | yesterday → the previous day
• Pronoun changes: you → he/she/they (context-based)
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Reported speech of Yes/No questions = PSC repeated top question!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. "Can you help?" → She asked if I could help.
  2. "Will you come?" → He asked if I would come.
  3. "Are you ready?" → She asked if I was ready.
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) I asked him if he can join the team today — "Can" backshift ആക്കിയില്ല (could ആകണം). "Today" → "that day" ആക്കിയില്ല. Double error!
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Students backshift AND time change — രണ്ടും forget ചെയ്യുന്ന common double mistake!

❌ (B) I asked him that can he join the team that day — Yes/No question reporting-ൽ "that" ഉപയോഗിക്കില്ല — "if/whether" ആണ് വേണ്ടത്. Plus "can he" = question word order, "he could" (statement order) ആകണം.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "that" = universal linker feel — but Yes/No questions = if/whether only!

❌ (C) I asked him if he joined the team on that day — "Can" → simple past "joined" = wrong backshift. "Can" → "could" (modal form) ആണ് വേണ്ടത്, simple past "joined" അല്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Backshift = simple past verb ആണ് meaning — but modals have own backshift (can→could)!
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: "3C rule for Reported Yes/No: Could + that day + if = 3 Changes!" — Can→Could, today→that day, ?→if.
• Word family: can→could | will→would | today→that day | tomorrow→next day | here→there
• PSC exam strategy: Reported speech-ൽ → 3 checks: (1) if/whether for Yes/No (2) modal backshift (3) time change — guaranteed mark!

questionText: Choose the idiom that means 'to stop avoiding the main issue and speak directly'.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (C) Beat around the bush

• "Beat around the bush" = main point-ൽ directly ആകാതെ avoid ചെയ്ക്കൽ, ചുറ്റിക്കറങ്ങി പറയൽ.
• "Stop avoiding the main issue and speak directly" = ഇതാണ് exact meaning.
• Origin: Hunters ആദ്യം bush beat ചെയ്ത് birds fly up ചെയ്യിക്കും — direct ആയി hunt ചെയ്യാതെ roundabout approach!
• PSC-ൽ ഏറ്റവും popular idiom ഇത് ആണ്.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Beat around the BUSH = go around in circles = avoid the main point!
• Opposite meaning: "Get straight to the point" / "Cut to the chase"
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Idiom meaning questions — PSC top topic, always in paper!
• PSC favourite idioms to know:
  Beat around the bush = avoid the point
  Break a leg = good luck
  Bite the bullet = endure bravely
  Beat a dead horse = waste time on hopeless cause
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. Stop beating around the bush and tell me what happened.
  2. The manager always beats around the bush instead of giving clear feedback.
  3. Don't beat around the bush — just say what you want.
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) Break a leg — "Break a leg" = Good luck! Theatre/performance world-ൽ നിന്ന് വന്ന idiom. "ഭാഗ്യം ആശംസിക്കൽ" ആണ് meaning — main issue avoid ചെയ്ക്കലല്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "Break" = negative action → avoiding feel — but actually "good luck" idiom!

❌ (B) Bite the bullet — "Bite the bullet" = face difficulty bravely, endure hardship. "Just bite the bullet and finish the task." — direct action ആണ്, avoid ചെയ്ക്കലല്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "Bite" = avoid/indirect feel — but actually OPPOSITE! Bite the bullet = face it bravely!

❌ (D) Beat a dead horse — "Beat a dead horse" = useless ആയ topic repeatedly discuss ചെയ്ക്കൽ, time waste ചെയ്ക്കൽ. Main issue avoid ചെയ്ക്കൽ അല്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "Beat" = same verb as "Beat around the bush" — same word, very different meaning!
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: "Beating AROUND = going in circles = avoiding the point!" — around = roundabout = indirect!
• Word family: Beat around the bush (avoid) | Break a leg (good luck) | Bite the bullet (endure) | Beat a dead horse (waste time) — PSC favourite 4 "B" idioms!
• PSC exam strategy: All 4 options start with "B" — PSC favourite "B-pattern" trap! Learn all 4 idioms separately, guaranteed mark!

questionText:
He apologized for arriving __late__.
The word 'late' in the above sentence is:

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (A) Adverb

• "He apologized for arriving __late__" — ഇവിടെ "late" എന്ന word "arriving" (gerund/verb form)-നെ modify ചെയ്യുന്നു.
• Verb അല്ലെങ്കിൽ verbal-നെ modify ചെയ്യുന്ന word = Adverb.
• "Late" ഒരു adjective ആകും "late train" (late + noun) ൽ — but verb modify ചെയ്യുമ്പോൾ adverb ആകും.
• "Arriving HOW?" → "arriving late" → HOW question-ന് answer = Adverb!
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Verb/Verbal modify ചെയ്യുന്നു → Adverb | Noun modify ചെയ്യുന്നു → Adjective
• "Late" dual role word:
  Late train (late + noun) → Adjective
  Arrive late (late + verb) → Adverb
  He came late. / She works late. / Don't stay late. → all Adverbs!
• Adverb answers: HOW? WHEN? WHERE? HOW MUCH? — verb-നെ കുറിച്ച് കൂടുതൽ പറയുന്നു.
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Parts of speech — same word different roles PSC favourite trap!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. He arrived late. (Adverb — modifies "arrived")
  2. She caught the late bus. (Adjective — modifies "bus")
  3. They worked late into the night. (Adverb — modifies "worked")
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (B) Adjective — Adjective noun-നെ modify ചെയ്യുന്നു. "Late" ഇവിടെ "arriving" (verb/gerund)-നെ modify ചെയ്യുന്നു, noun-നെ അല്ല. "Late train" ൽ "late" = adjective, but "arriving late" ൽ = adverb.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "Late" = adjective ആണ് usually — but ഇവിടെ verb modify ചെയ്യുന്നതിനാൽ adverb!

❌ (C) Conjunction — Conjunction sentences/words join ചെയ്യുന്നു (and, but, or, because). "Late" ഇവിടെ join ചെയ്യുന്നില്ല — completely wrong category.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Conjunction, Preposition — eliminate ചെയ്ക്കൂ, clearly wrong.

❌ (D) Preposition — Preposition noun-ന് മുൻപ് വന്ന് relationship show ചെയ്യുന്നു (in, on, at, for). "Late" ഇവിടെ preposition ആയി function ചെയ്യുന്നില്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "for arriving late" — "for" ആണ് preposition ഇവിടെ, "late" അല്ല!
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: "Verb + Late = Adverb | Noun + Late = Adjective" — what comes AFTER "late" decides!
• Word family: Late/lately/later/latest — all different parts of speech in different contexts.
• PSC exam strategy: Word identify ചെയ്യൽ questions-ൽ — what does the word modify? Noun → Adj; Verb → Adverb!

questionText: Twenty percent of the students _____ completed the assignment on time.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (D) have

• "Twenty percent of the students" — ഇവിടെ "of"-ന് ശേഷം "students" (plural noun) ആണ്.
• Rule: Percent/Part/Majority/Half + of + noun → verb agrees with the noun AFTER "of".
• "Students" = plural → verb = "have" (plural).
• "Twenty percent of the students have completed" = "20% വിദ്യാർഥികൾ assignment complete ചെയ്തു.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: X% of + plural noun → plural verb | X% of + singular noun → singular verb
• "of"-ന് ശേഷമുള്ള noun = verb decide ചെയ്യും!
  20% of the students HAVE completed. (students = plural → have)
  20% of the water HAS evaporated. (water = singular → has)
  Half of the cake IS gone. (cake = singular → is)
  Half of the apples ARE rotten. (apples = plural → are)
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Percent/fraction + of SVA questions PSC repeated pattern!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. Fifty percent of the votes have been counted.
  2. Ten percent of the budget has been spent.
  3. Most of the children have left.
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) has — "Has" singular subject-ന് ഉപയോഗിക്കുന്നു. ഇവിടെ "students" (plural) ആണ് deciding noun — "have" ആകണം.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "Twenty percent" singular feel — but "of students" plural ആണ്, noun after "of" check!

❌ (B) was — "Was" past tense, singular ആണ്. Sentence present perfect tense ("completed") ആണ് — was wrong tense.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Tense confusion — present perfect sentence-ൽ past tense was/were confuse ആകും.

❌ (C) is — "Is" present singular ആണ്. "Students" plural ആണ്, plus "have completed" present perfect-ൽ "is" fit ആകില്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "Twenty percent" → "is" = singular feel, but noun after "of" decide ചെയ്യും!
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: "OF decides the verb!" — percent/half/majority + OF → look RIGHT (after of), not LEFT!
• Word family: percent of | part of | half of | majority of | minority of | a number of — all follow same rule.
• PSC exam strategy: Percent/fraction question കണ്ടാൽ → "of"-ന് ശേഷമുള്ള noun plural ആണോ singular ആണോ check ചെയ്ക്കൂ!

questionText: We need _____ sugar to bake the cake.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (D) no article

• "Sugar" = uncountable noun (mass noun) — count ചെയ്യാൻ കഴിയാത്ത substance.
• General reference-ൽ uncountable nouns-ന് മുൻപ് article ഉപയോഗിക്കില്ല.
• "We need sugar" = "നമുക്ക് പഞ്ചസാര വേണം" — specific sugar അല്ല, sugar in general.
• "The sugar" ആകും specific sugar ഉദ്ദേശ്യമുണ്ടെങ്കിൽ (e.g., "Pass the sugar" — table-ലെ specific sugar).
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Uncountable noun + general sense = NO article | specific reference = "the"
• Common uncountable nouns (no article in general use):
  water, sugar, milk, rice, salt, butter, flour, oil, bread
  information, advice, knowledge, news, furniture, luggage
• "We need sugar" (general) = no article ✓
• "Pass the sugar" (specific — on the table) = the ✓
• "A sugar" = WRONG (uncountable cannot use "a/an")
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Articles with uncountable nouns PSC favourite!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. I need water to survive. (no article — general)
  2. The water in this bottle is clean. (the — specific)
  3. She gave me advice. (no article — uncountable)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) a — "A/An" countable singular nouns-ന് ഉപയോഗിക്കുന്നു. "Sugar" uncountable ആണ്, "a sugar" impossible.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Blank fill → "a" ആദ്യം choose ചെയ്യും — but uncountable = no a/an!

❌ (B) an — "An" vowel sound-ൽ ആരംഭിക്കുന്ന countable noun-ന് ഉപയോഗിക്കുന്നു. "Sugar" uncountable + consonant sound — doubly wrong.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "Sugar" starts with 's' not vowel, so "an" wrong even if it were countable!

❌ (C) the — "The" specific reference-ൽ ഉപയോഗിക്കുന്നു. ഇവിടെ general sugar ആണ് ഉദ്ദേശ്യം — "the sugar" = specific sugar, general sense-ന് not correct here.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "The" safe feel — but baking context-ൽ specific sugar reference ഇല്ല, general ആണ്!
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: "Can I count ONE sugar, TWO sugars? NO → no article!" — uncountable = no a/an!
• Word family: Uncountable nouns — water, rice, sugar, milk, advice, information, furniture, luggage, news.
• PSC exam strategy: Uncountable noun + general sense = always "no article" — article questions-ൽ noun type check ചെയ്ക്കൂ!

questionText: By the time the guests arrive, we _____ the hall.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (B) will have decorated

• "By the time the guests arrive, we _____ the hall."
• "By the time + present simple" structure → main clause = Future Perfect.
• "By the time" = guests arrive ആകുമ്പോഴേക്കും → decorating already complete ആയിരിക്കും.
• Future Perfect formula: will have + V3 (past participle).
• "will have decorated" = "അതിഥികൾ വരുന്ന നേരത്തേക്കും hall decorate ചെയ്തിരിക്കും.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: By the time + Present Simple → will have + V3 (Future Perfect)
• "By the time" = deadline/completion signal → Future Perfect in main clause!
• Future Perfect = action will be COMPLETE before a future point in time.
• Formula: will have + past participle (V3)
  decorate → will have decorated
  finish → will have finished
  leave → will have left
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: "By the time" trigger word + Future Perfect — PSC classic question!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. By the time he arrives, we will have finished dinner.
  2. By 2030, scientists will have discovered new planets.
  3. By the time you read this, I will have left.
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) decorate — Simple present tense ആണ്. Future action express ചെയ്യുന്നില്ല. "By the time" future completion indicate ചെയ്യുന്നു — present tense wrong.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "arrive" present tense ആണ്, so main clause-ഉം present ആകും — but "by the time" future perfect trigger!

❌ (C) will be decorating — Future Continuous = action in progress at a future time. "By the time" = completion, not ongoing action — Future Perfect ആണ് correct.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "will be decorating" = future action sound ചെയ്യും, but it means action in progress, not completed!

❌ (D) have decorated — Present Perfect ആണ്. Present Perfect past-to-now connection show ചെയ്യുന്നു. ഇവിടെ future time ("by the time guests arrive") ആണ് — present perfect wrong.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "have decorated" = completed feel, but present perfect future time-ന് ഉപയോഗിക്കില്ല!
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: "By the time = WILL HAVE done!" — deadline word "by" → future perfect!
• Word family: by + time expression → Future Perfect: by tomorrow, by then, by the time, by 2030.
• PSC exam strategy: "By the time" കണ്ടാൽ → automatically "will have + V3" — guaranteed mark!

questionText: He has been living in Delhi _____ 2015.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (C) since

• "He has been living in Delhi _____ 2015."
• "2015" = specific point in time (year) → "since" ഉപയോഗിക്കണം.
• Since = from a specific moment/point in time until now.
• For = duration (a period of time — for 5 years, for a long time).
• "Since 2015" = "2015 മുതൽ ഇപ്പോൾ വരെ" — present perfect/perfect continuous-ൽ since perfect fit!
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Point in time (year, date, event) → SINCE | Duration (period) → FOR
• Since: 2015, Monday, morning, childhood, last year, I was young
• For: 5 years, a long time, two hours, many days, ages
• Since + Present Perfect / Present Perfect Continuous = always correct pair!
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Since vs For — PSC most repeated preposition question!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. I have known her since 2010. (point in time)
  2. I have known her for 10 years. (duration)
  3. She has been working here since Monday. (point in time)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) from — "From" duration or starting point show ചെയ്യുന്നു but present perfect-ൽ "from" ഉപയോഗിക്കില്ല. "From 2015 to 2020" — two endpoints ഉള്ളപ്പോൾ "from...to" ഉപയോഗിക്കാം. Present perfect + single point = "since."
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "from 2015" = natural Malayalam feel ("2015 മുതൽ") — but English grammar-ൽ present perfect + point = "since"!

❌ (B) for — "For" duration-ന് ഉപയോഗിക്കുന്നു (for 5 years). "2015" duration അല്ല, specific year (point in time) ആണ് — "for 2015" wrong.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "For" and "since" confuse — for = duration, since = specific point. "2015" point ആണ്!

❌ (D) by — "By" deadline/completion show ചെയ്യുന്നു (by Monday, by 5 PM). Present perfect continuous living context-ൽ "by" fit ആകില്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "By 2015" ചിലപ്പോൾ ഉപയോഗിക്കും (by 2015 he had...) — but present perfect + living = since!
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: "SINCE = a SINGLE moment | FOR = a FOUR-letter duration (period)!" — point vs period!
• Word family: since Monday/2015/childhood | for five years/a long time/ages — memorise both lists!
• PSC exam strategy: Year/date/day name കണ്ടാൽ → since | number + years/hours/days കണ്ടാൽ → for — instant decision!

topic: Singular & Plural, Change of Gender, Collective Nouns
questionText: Which of the following is the correct plural of 'appendix' when referring to text?

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (A) Appendices

• "Appendix" — Latin origin word ആണ്. Text/document-ൽ refer ചെയ്യുമ്പോൾ plural = "Appendices."
• Latin plurals: -ix/-ex endings → -ices (appendix → appendices, index → indices)
• "Appendices" = a book/document-ലെ additional sections (text context).
• "Appendixes" = ശരീരത്തിലെ appendix organs (medical/anatomical context) — but question specifically says "referring to text.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Text/Document appendix → Appendices (Latin) | Body organ appendix → Appendixes (English)
• Latin plural patterns:
  appendix → appendices (text additions)
  index → indices/indexes
  matrix → matrices
  vertex → vertices
  criterion → criteria
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Latin/irregular plurals PSC favourite spelling/grammar question!
• Other irregular Latin plurals PSC loves:
  datum → data | medium → media | phenomenon → phenomena
  syllabus → syllabi | nucleus → nuclei | radius → radii
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. The book has three appendices at the back.
  2. Please refer to the appendices for further details.
  3. The indices of the textbooks were helpful.
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (B) Appendixes — Medical/anatomical context-ൽ body organ (appendix) plural-ന് "appendixes" ഉപയോഗിക്കും. But question specifically "referring to text" — so "appendices" ആണ് correct.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "Appendixes" real English word ആണ് — but context (text vs body) check ചെയ്ക്കൂ!

❌ (C) Appendixs — "Appendixs" wrong spelling. -ix ending words-ൽ simply "s" add ചെയ്യില്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Simple "-s" add ചെയ്ക്കൽ temptation — but Latin words irregular plural ആണ്!

❌ (D) Appendexes — "Appendexes" non-existent word. "Appendix" → "Appendex" = wrong root. Pure fabrication.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Confusingly similar to "appendixes" — spelling carefully check ചെയ്ക്കൂ!
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: "Text Appendix → AppendICES — ICE in books!" | "Body Appendix → AppendIXES — IX in bodies!"
• Word family: appendix→appendices | index→indices | matrix→matrices | vertex→vertices — Latin -ix→-ices!
• PSC exam strategy: Latin plural questions-ൽ context check ചെയ്ക്കൂ — text/document = appendices, body = appendixes!

questionText: Identify the correctly spelled compound word.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (C) Snowfall

• "Snowfall" = closed compound word — space ഇല്ലാതെ, hyphen ഇല്ലാതെ ഒരു word ആയി എഴുതുന്നു.
• Compound word types: closed (snowfall), hyphenated (snow-white), open (snow day).
• "Snowfall" = ഒരു well-established closed compound — dictionary-ൽ one word ആയി listed.
• PSC-ൽ compound word spelling questions-ൽ closed form correct ആകും (unless specific hyphenated form ചോദിക്കുന്നു).
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Common established compound words = closed (one word, no space/hyphen)
• Common closed compound words (PSC favourites):
  snowfall | rainfall | waterfall | football | blackboard | bookcase
  sunlight | moonlight | fireplace | doorbell | notebook | bedroom
• Hyphenated compounds: mother-in-law | well-known | up-to-date | self-confidence
• Open compounds: post office | ice cream | high school | living room
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Compound word spelling — closed vs hyphenated PSC repeated!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. The snowfall last night was beautiful.
  2. Heavy rainfall disrupted the traffic.
  3. The waterfall was a breathtaking sight.
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) Snow fall — Two separate words ആണ്. "Snowfall" ഒരു compound noun ആണ്, two separate words ആയി എഴുതില്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "Snow" + "fall" = two words feel ആകും — but established compound = one word!

❌ (B) Snow-fall — Hyphenated form wrong. "Snowfall" well-established closed compound ആണ്, hyphen ആവശ്യമില്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Compound words = hyphen ഇടണം എന്ന് തോന്നും — most common compounds closed ആണ്!

❌ (D) Snowfal — Spelling mistake — double 'l' missing ("ll" ആണ് correct: snowfall).
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Single 'l' vs double 'll' — fall = f-a-l-l, so snowfall = snow+fall = two l's!
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: "SNOW + FALL = SNOWFALL — fall always has double-L!" — fall = f-a-l-l, never f-a-l!
• Word family: snowfall | rainfall | waterfall | downfall | pitfall | windfall — all closed, all double-L!
• PSC exam strategy: Compound word questions-ൽ → closed (one word) usually correct for common nature/everyday words!

questionText: The word closest in meaning to __Abridge__ is:

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (B) Condense

• "Abridge" = to shorten a book/text/speech by removing less important parts — "ചുരുക്കുക."
• "Condense" = to make shorter/more concentrated — same meaning, exact synonym.
• "An abridged dictionary" = "ചുരുക്കിയ dictionary" — shortened version.
• Condense = compress meaning into fewer words — perfect synonym of abridge.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Abridge = Condense = Shorten | Expand = Antonym (opposite)
• Root: Latin "abbreviare" related — ab (away) + bridge (cutting) = cut away = shorten
• Word family of Abridge:
  abridged (adj): an abridged version | abridge (v): to abridge a novel
• Synonyms of Abridge: condense | shorten | abbreviate | summarise | reduce | compress
• Antonyms: expand | lengthen | elaborate | extend
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Vocabulary synonyms — especially literary/academic words PSC favourite!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. The editor abridged the 500-page novel to 200 pages.
  2. Please condense your report to one page.
  3. They published an abridged version of the dictionary.
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) Expand — "Expand" = വലുതാക്കുക, വിസ്തരിക്കുക — "Abridge"-ന്റെ exact ANTONYM (opposite). Expand = more, Abridge = less.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "Abridge" = "a bridge" sound — bridge = connect/expand feel — but meaning = shorten!

❌ (C) Bridge — "Bridge" = പാലം (noun) / connect ചെയ്ക്കൽ (verb). "Abridge" ≠ "Bridge" — completely different word. "A" prefix change ചെയ്ക്കുന്നു meaning.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "Abridge" visually contains "bridge" — but it's a different word entirely!

❌ (D) Abrade — "Abrade" = to scrape/wear away by friction (ഉരസി മാറ്റുക). Similar spelling to "abridge" but totally different meaning.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "Abr-" same start → abrade/abridge confuse ആകും — but meanings completely different!
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: "Abridge = BRIDGE cut SHORT — a bridge that got shortened!" — abridge = cut short!
• Word family: Synonyms = condense, shorten, abbreviate | Antonyms = expand, elaborate, extend.
• PSC exam strategy: Vocabulary questions-ൽ → "Expand" opposite feel ആകും, eliminate it first — then between similar words choose!

questionText: What does __Suo moto__ mean?

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (D) On its own motion

• "Suo motu" (also written "suo moto") = Latin phrase meaning "on its own motion/initiative."
• Court suo motu action = court itself taking up a case without any petition from anyone.
• "Suo" = one's own | "motu" = motion/movement → "on one's own motion."
• PSC current affairs-ൽ also important — Supreme Court suo motu cases often in news.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Suo motu = On its own motion = court/authority self-initiates action
• Common Latin legal/formal phrases PSC loves:
  Suo motu = on its own motion
  Bona fide = in good faith (sincere)
  Prima facie = at first sight (on the face of it)
  Habeas corpus = produce the body (legal writ)
  Status quo = existing state of affairs
  Vice versa = with positions reversed
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Latin/foreign phrases — PSC top vocabulary section!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. The Supreme Court took suo motu cognizance of the matter.
  2. The commission acted suo motu to investigate the case.
  3. The court intervened suo motu after reading the news report.
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) By force — "By force" = ബലം ഉപയോഗിച്ച്. Latin "vi et armis" = by force. "Suo motu" = self-initiated, voluntary action ആണ്, force ആകില്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "Motion" = movement → forced movement feel — but suo motu = voluntary self-initiation!

❌ (B) Against the law — "Suo motu" courts/authorities take ചെയ്യുന്ന legal action ആണ് — against the law ആകില്ല, legal process-ന്റെ part ആണ്.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Unknown Latin term → negative/suspicious meaning guess ചെയ്ക്കും — but it's a positive legal term!

❌ (C) With permission — "Suo motu" = WITHOUT anyone's petition/permission — self-initiated. "With permission" = opposite meaning!
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Official term → formal/with permission feel — but suo motu = no permission needed, own initiative!
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: "SUO = one's OWN, MOTU = MOTION — OWN MOTION = nobody asked, court itself moved!"
• Word family: Suo motu | Prima facie | Bona fide | Habeas corpus | Status quo — PSC Latin phrases must-know list!
• PSC exam strategy: Latin legal phrases — learn the 6-8 most common ones. Suo motu appears regularly in PSC and current affairs!

questionText: The idiom __jump on the bandwagon__ means:
optionC: to join others in doing something popular
optionD: to jump and step into a vehicle

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (C) to join others in doing something popular

• "Jump on the bandwagon" = popular ആയ trend/activity-ൽ join ചെയ്ക്കൽ, group-ൽ ചേരൽ.
• Origin: 19th century American politics-ൽ political candidate-ന്റെ parade band wagon-ൽ ആളുകൾ jump ചെയ്ത് support show ചെയ്യുമായിരുന്നു.
• "Everyone is buying electric cars now, so he jumped on the bandwagon too."
• Meaning: follow others, join a trend — sometimes implies doing it just because it's popular!
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Jump on the bandwagon = join a popular trend/movement (often without thinking)
• Negative connotation possible: "jumping on the bandwagon" = following blindly, not genuine belief.
• Opposite: "march to the beat of your own drum" = do your own thing, don't follow trends.
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Idioms with "jump/run/beat" — PSC favourite category!
• Related PSC favourite idioms:
  Jump on the bandwagon = join popular trend
  Jump the gun = act too early
  Jump to conclusions = decide too quickly
  Jump at the chance = eagerly accept opportunity
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. Many companies jumped on the AI bandwagon last year.
  2. She jumped on the fitness bandwagon after seeing her friends' results.
  3. Don't just jump on the bandwagon — think for yourself.
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) to start a band — "Bandwagon" = historical parade wagon with a band. "Bandwagon" ≠ "band" starting. Music band-ൻ ഒന്നും ബന്ധമില്ല idiom-ൽ.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "Bandwagon" contains "band" → music band feel — but idiom meaning literal translation അല്ല!

❌ (B) to avoid a situation — "Jump on" = join/embrace, avoid ചെയ്ക്കൽ അല്ല. Meaning exactly opposite — joining, not avoiding.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "Jump away from" = avoid — but "jump ON" = get on, join!

❌ (D) to jump and step into a vehicle — ഇത് literal meaning ആണ്. Idioms literal ആയി interpret ചെയ്ക്കരുത്. "Bandwagon" = ഒരു wagon ആണ് literally, but idiom = trend joining.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "Jump into a vehicle" literal translation feel — idiom meaning always figurative, not literal!
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: "BAND = popular music → BANDWAGON = popular trend → JUMP ON = join the trend!" — everyone doing it = bandwagon!
• Word family: Jump on the bandwagon | Break a leg | Beat around the bush | Bite the bullet — PSC B+J idiom family!
• PSC exam strategy: Idiom questions-ൽ literal meaning eliminate ചെയ്ക്കൂ first (option D), then find figurative trend/action meaning!

questionText:
Fill in the blank using an appropriate article.
He waited for _____ hour and a half for lunch.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (B) An

• "Hour" എന്ന word-ൽ 'h' letter ആദ്യം വന്നാലും, ആ 'h' silent ആണ് — ഉച്ചരിക്കുമ്പോൾ vowel sound (ow-er) ആണ് കേൾക്കുന്നത്.
• Article rule ലളിതം: Spelling നോക്കരുത് — SOUND നോക്കണം. Vowel sound-ൽ തുടങ്ങുന്ന word-ന് മുമ്പ് "an" ഉപയോഗിക്കണം.
• "He waited for an hour" = "അവൻ ഒരു മണിക്കൂർ കാത്തിരുന്നു" — ഇത് perfect English.
• Silent 'h' words ഒരിക്കൽ ഓർത്താൽ PSC-ൽ ഇത്തരം questions ഒരിക്കലും miss ആകില്ല!
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: A/An തിരഞ്ഞെടുക്കാൻ LETTER നോക്കരുത് — SOUND നോക്കണം!
• Vowel SOUND-ൽ ആരംഭിക്കുന്ന words → AN | Consonant SOUND-ൽ ആരംഭിക്കുന്ന words → A
• Silent 'h' words (vowel sound ആയതിനാൽ AN ഉപയോഗിക്കും):
  an hour | an honest man | an heir | an honour | an herb (American English)
• Misleading words (vowel letter ആണ്, പക്ഷേ consonant sound ആണ്, A ഉപയോഗിക്കും):
  a university (/yoo/ sound) | a European (/yoo/ sound) | a one-eyed man (/w/ sound)
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: "An hour vs A university" — PSC-ൽ ഏറ്റവും കൂടുതൽ repeat ആകുന്ന article question ഇതാണ്!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. She waited for an hour. (silent h = vowel sound = an)
  2. He is an honest officer. (silent h = an)
  3. It is a university, not a school. (u = /yoo/ consonant sound = a)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) A
• "A" consonant sound-ൽ തുടങ്ങുന്ന words-ന് മുമ്പ് ഉപയോഗിക്കുന്നു — a cat, a book, a house.
• "Hour"-ന്റെ ഉച്ചാരണം "ow-er" ആണ് — vowel sound. അതുകൊണ്ട് "a" ഇവിടെ ചേരില്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'h' letter കണ്ടാൽ consonant sound ആണെന്ന് തോന്നും — പക്ഷേ "hour"-ൽ h silent ആണ്!

❌ (C) The
• "The" specific reference ഉള്ള nouns-ന് ഉപയോഗിക്കുന്നു — before-ൽ mention ചെയ്ത, identify ചെയ്ത objects-ന്.
• ഇവിടെ "an hour" general duration refer ചെയ്യുന്നു — specific hour അല്ല. "The" ചേരില്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Hour important ആണെന്ന feel വരും — "the hour" choose ചെയ്ക്കും. But ഇത് specific hour അല്ല!

❌ (D) None of these
• "An" correct ആണ് — standard English usage. "None of these" wrong.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Rule confuse ആകുമ്പോൾ "None of these" safe option ആണെന്ന് തോന്നും — എന്നാൽ "an hour" ഒരു standard phrase ആണ്!
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: "HONEST HOUR HEIR HONOUR — Silent H Club! എല്ലാം AN ഉപയോഗിക്കും!" — ഈ 4 words ഓർത്താൽ മതി.
• Word family: an hour | an honest | an honour | an heir | an herb — PSC-ൽ ഏറ്റവും കൂടുതൽ വരുന്ന silent-h family!
• PSC exam strategy: Article blank + 'h' letter-ൽ തുടങ്ങുന്ന word കണ്ടാൽ → ആദ്യം sound check ചെയ്യൂ. Hour/honest/honour = silent h = AN. Guaranteed mark!

questionText: He sat _____ his friend.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (A) Beside

• "Beside" = next to, at the side of — "അടുത്ത്, പക്കൽ" എന്ന് അർത്ഥം.
• "He sat beside his friend" = "അവൻ തന്റെ സുഹൃത്തിന്റെ അടുത്ത് ഇരുന്നു" — physical position describe ചെയ്യുന്നു.
• ഒരാൾ ഇരിക്കുകയോ നിൽക്കുകയോ ചെയ്യുന്നത് describe ചെയ്യുമ്പോൾ → "beside" (next to) ആണ് correct.
• "Besides" (extra 's') = "കൂടാതെ, മാത്രമല്ല" — ഇതൊരു entirely different meaning ആണ്!
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Beside = NEXT TO (physical position) | Besides = IN ADDITION TO (extra information)
• Beside ഉദാഹരണങ്ങൾ (next to):
  She sat beside me. | He stood beside the door. | The shop is beside the bank.
• Besides ഉദാഹരണങ്ങൾ (in addition to):
  Besides English, she knows French. | I don't want it — besides, it's too expensive.
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Beside vs Besides — PSC-ൽ "Words Often Confused" section-ൽ regular repeat!
• Word family: beside (preposition — position) | besides (preposition/adverb — addition)
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. She sat beside her mother during the meeting. (next to)
  2. Besides the ticket price, we need money for food. (in addition to)
  3. He placed his bag beside the chair and left. (next to)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (B) Besides
• "Besides" = "കൂടാതെ, അതിന് പുറമേ" — extra information add ചെയ്യുമ്പോൾ ഉപയോഗിക്കുന്നു.
• "He sat besides his friend" grammatically incorrect — sitting position-ന് "besides" ഉപയോഗിക്കില്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Beside, Besides — ഒരേ spelling, ഒരു 's' മാത്രം difference. Students confuse ചെയ്ക്കും. 's' ഉള്ളത് = "extra/addition"!

❌ (C) Between
• "Between" = രണ്ട് specific things-ന്റെ ഇടയിൽ — "between two friends" (two people).
• ഇവിടെ "his friend" = ഒരൊറ്റ ആൾ. ഒരൊറ്റ ആളുമായി "between" ഉപയോഗിക്കില്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "Between friends" = familiar phrase ആണ് — "between his friend" wrong (singular). Between = two things!

❌ (D) Along
• "Along" = ഒരു line-ൽ കൂടി movement — "walk along the road" (movement).
• Sitting-ന്റെ physical position describe ചെയ്യാൻ "along" ഉപയോഗിക്കില്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "Along with his friend" = ഒരു context-ൽ correct ആകും — but "sat along" = wrong!
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: "BesidE = nExt to (E → position!) | BesidES = Extra Stuff (S → something more!)"
• Word family: beside/next to | besides/in addition to/moreover — ഈ pairs ഒരുമിച്ച് ഓർക്കൂ!
• PSC exam strategy: Physical position (sitting, standing, lying) → beside. Extra information/addition → besides. 'S' = "Something extra" — ഇത് ഓർത്താൽ ഒരിക്കലും miss ആകില്ല!

questionText: My brother is _____ boy in his class.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (B) The tallest

• "In his class" — ഒരു group-ൽ (class) comparison ചെയ്യുന്നതിന് superlative degree വേണം.
• Superlative degree = "the + adjective + est" — ഒരു group-ൽ ഏറ്റവും കൂടുതൽ ആണ് ഇദ്ദേഹം എന്ന് show ചെയ്യുന്നു.
• "My brother is the tallest boy in his class" = "എന്റെ സഹോദരൻ ക്ലാസ്സിലെ ഏറ്റവും ഉയരമുള്ള കുട്ടി ആണ്."
• "In/of + group" കണ്ടാൽ — superlative! ഇത് ഒരു guaranteed PSC mark!
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: "in/of + group" → SUPERLATIVE (the + adj + est / the most + adj)
• Signal words for Superlative: in the class | of all | in the world | among all | of the three
• Degrees of comparison:
  Positive: tall (simple description)
  Comparative: taller than (comparing TWO things)
  Superlative: the tallest in/of (comparing within a GROUP)
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: "In the class / of all" signal → superlative — ഇത് PSC-ൽ ഏറ്റവും കൂടുതൽ repeat ആകുന്ന degrees question pattern!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. She is the tallest girl in the school. (group = school)
  2. He is the most intelligent student in the class. (group = class)
  3. This is the best movie of the year. (group = year)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) Tall
• "Tall" = positive degree — simple description, comparison ഇല്ല.
• "My brother is tall boy in his class" — grammatically wrong ("a tall boy" ആകണം), comparison-ഉം ഇല്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Simple adjective feel — but "in his class" = group comparison trigger = superlative!

❌ (C) Taller
• "Taller" = comparative degree — TWO items compare ചെയ്യുമ്പോൾ (taller than someone).
• "In his class" = group (many students) → superlative ആണ് വേണ്ടത്, comparative അല്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "Taller" = ഉയരക്കൂടുതൽ feel — but compare TWO → taller than; compare GROUP → the tallest!

❌ (D) As tall as
• "As tall as" = positive degree equal comparison — "ഒരേ ഉയരം ഉണ്ട്" എന്ന് meaning.
• ഒരു group-ൽ ഏറ്റവും tall ആണ് brother എന്ന് sentence-ൽ imply ആകുന്നു — equal comparison wrong.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "As tall as" familiar structure — but group context = superlative, equal comparison ഇല്ല ഇവിടെ!
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: "IN a group = IN superlative!" — "in the class / in the world / of all" കണ്ടാൽ ഉടനെ superlative!
• Word family: tall → taller → the tallest | good → better → the best | bad → worse → the worst — irregular forms ഓർക്കണം!
• PSC exam strategy: "In the class / of all" phrase കണ്ടാൽ → superlative (the + adj + est). Instant answer! ഒരിക്കലും miss ആകരുത്!

questionText: I saw a _____ of dogs.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (B) Pack

• Dogs-ന്റെ collective noun ആണ് "pack" — "a pack of dogs" = "നായ്ക്കൂട്ടം."
• Pack of dogs / wolves = hunting animals-ന്റെ group describe ചെയ്യുന്ന standard term.
• "I saw a pack of dogs on the street" = "ഞാൻ തെരുവിൽ ഒരു നായ്ക്കൂട്ടത്തെ കണ്ടു."
• Collective nouns ഓരോ animal-ന്നും unique ആണ് — ഒരുമിച്ച് ഓർക്കേണ്ടത് important!
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Dogs/Wolves → PACK | Cattle/Elephants → HERD | Birds/Sheep → FLOCK
• PSC favourite collective nouns:
  a pack of dogs / wolves / cards
  a herd of cattle / elephants / horses / deer
  a flock of birds / sheep / geese
  a school of fish | a pride of lions | a murder of crows
  a swarm of bees | a litter of kittens | a gaggle of geese
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Collective nouns — PSC vocabulary section-ൽ guaranteed question! Animal groups ഒരിക്കൽ കണ്ടഠിച്ചാൽ easy marks!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. A pack of wolves surrounded the deer.
  2. A herd of elephants crossed the river near the forest.
  3. A flock of birds flew across the evening sky.
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) Herd
• "Herd" = cattle, elephants, horses, buffaloes, deer — large grazing animals-ന്റെ group.
• Dogs wild/predatory animals ആണ് — "herd" ഉപയോഗിക്കില്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Dogs large-ish ആണ്, herd feel — but herd = grazing animals, pack = hunting animals!

❌ (C) Gang
• "Gang" = thieves, robbers, criminals, workers-ന്റെ group. Human groups-ന്.
• Dogs-ന്റെ standard collective noun "gang" അല്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "Gang of dogs" informally ഉപയോഗിക്കാറുണ്ട്, standard English-ൽ pack ആണ്!

❌ (D) Flock
• "Flock" = birds, sheep, goats — flying or grazing light animals-ന്.
• Dogs carnivores ആണ്, flock ഉപയോഗിക്കില്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Flock familiar word — but sheep/birds-ന് മാത്രം. Dogs = pack!
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: "Dogs PACK their things before a HUNT!" — pack = hunting animals group. ഈ image ഓർത്താൽ മതി!
• Word family: pack (dogs/wolves) | herd (cattle/elephants) | flock (birds/sheep) | pride (lions) | school (fish) — PSC top 5 animal groups!
• PSC exam strategy: Animal കണ്ടാൽ → animal type identify ചെയ്യൂ. Hunting/wild = pack. Grazing = herd. Flying = flock. Lions = pride. Fish = school. ഈ 5 ഓർത്താൽ PSC collective noun questions complete!

questionText:
Choose the correct passive form of the sentence.
He will help the old woman.
optionA: The old woman will have helped by him.
optionB: The old woman will being helped by him.
optionC: The old woman will be helped by him.
optionD: The old woman would have helped by him.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (C) The old woman will be helped by him.

• Active: "He will help the old woman." — Simple Future tense.
• Passive formula for Simple Future: Subject + will be + V3 (past participle) + by + agent.
• "Help" → past participle "helped" → "will be helped."
• Object (old woman) → subject | Subject (he) → "by him" | will + be + V3 = correct passive form.
• "The old woman will be helped by him" = "ആ വൃദ്ധ സ്ത്രീ അദ്ദേഹത്തിന്റെ സഹായം ലഭിക്കും.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Simple Future Passive = Subject + will be + V3 + by + agent
• Active tense → Passive formula:
  Simple Present: is/am/are + V3
  Simple Past: was/were + V3
  Simple Future: will be + V3
  Present Perfect: has/have been + V3
• Active-ൽ Object → Passive-ൽ Subject ആകും.
  Active: He will help her. → Passive: She will be helped by him.
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Future tense passive — "will be + V3" pattern PSC-ൽ regular!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. He will finish the work. → The work will be finished by him.
  2. She will write the letter. → The letter will be written by her.
  3. They will build a house. → A house will be built by them.
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) The old woman will have helped by him.
• "Will have helped" = Future Perfect Active form — passive അല്ല.
• Passive-ൽ "be + V3" ആണ് വേണ്ടത്, "have + V3" passive form അല്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "Will have" = future feel → passive context-ൽ confuse ആകും. But passive = will BE helped!

❌ (B) The old woman will being helped by him.
• "Will being" = grammatically incorrect — ഇങ്ങനൊരു structure English-ൽ exist ചെയ്യുന്നില്ല.
• Correct form: "will be" — modal verb + base form of be = "will be."
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "Being" = passive feel ഉണ്ടാക്കും — but "will being" wrong! "Will be" ആണ്!

❌ (D) The old woman would have helped by him.
• "Would have helped" = Third Conditional active form — passive-ഉം അല്ല, tense-ഉം wrong.
• Simple Future passive-ന് "will be" ആണ്, "would have" conditional ആണ്.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "Would" = polite/formal feel → future passive context-ൽ wrong. "Will be" ആണ്!
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: "Future Passive = WILL + BE + done — BE sandwich ചെയ്യൂ will-ഉം V3-ഉം ഇടയിൽ!"
• Word family: will be done | will be written | will be helped | will be finished — all same pattern!
• PSC exam strategy: Passive voice question-ൽ → ആദ്യം active sentence-ന്റെ tense identify ചെയ്യൂ → Future = will be + V3. Tense table ഓർത്തിരുന്നാൽ passive questions always easy!

questionText:
My friend __has too many irons in the fire__.
(Find out the meaning of the underlined idiom)
optionA: He is engaged with many problems
optionD: He is engaged in too many enterprises at the same time

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (D) He is engaged in too many enterprises at the same time.

• "Too many irons in the fire" = ഒരേ സമയം വളരെ അധികം activities/projects handle ചെയ്യുന്ന situation.
• Origin: Blacksmith ഒരേ സമയം too many irons fire-ൽ ഇട്ടാൽ ഒന്നും properly forge ചെയ്യാൻ കഴിയില്ല — ഇതിൽ നിന്ന് ഈ idiom ഉണ്ടായി.
• "My friend has too many irons in the fire" = "എന്റെ സുഹൃത്ത് ഒരേ സമയം വളരെ അധികം കാര്യങ്ങളിൽ engaged ആണ്."
• Connotation: slightly negative — ഒന്നിലും proper attention കൊടുക്കാൻ കഴിയുന്നില്ല എന്ന feel.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Too many irons in the fire = ഒരേ സമയം അധികം activities = spreading oneself too thin
• Idiom meaning: too many projects/tasks at once, none of which can be done properly
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Work/business-related idioms PSC-ൽ regular feature — idiom meaning questions guaranteed!
• Related idioms PSC students ഓർക്കേണ്ടത്:
  bite off more than you can chew = handle ചെയ്യാൻ കഴിയുന്നതിലും കൂടുതൽ attempt ചെയ്യൽ
  burn the candle at both ends = ഒരേ സമയം work-ഉം exhaustion-ഉം
  have a lot on one's plate = വളരെ busy ആണ്
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. He has too many irons in the fire and cannot focus on any single project.
  2. Stop taking new clients — you already have too many irons in the fire.
  3. She juggles five jobs; truly too many irons in the fire.
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) He is engaged with many problems
• "Too many irons in the fire" = enterprises/activities refer ചെയ്യുന്നു — problems/difficulties അല്ല.
• Idiom-ൽ negative sense ഉണ്ടെങ്കിലും "problems" ആണ് meaning എന്ന് directly പറഞ്ഞിട്ടില്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "Too many" = problems feel — but idiom = too many ACTIVITIES, not problems!

❌ (B) He is having many qualities
• Idiom-ൽ qualities/talents-നെ കുറിച്ച് ഒന്നും ഇല്ല — activities/enterprises ആണ് meaning.
• "Irons in the fire" = tasks being handled, qualities/talents അല്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "Fire" = passionate/talented feel — but idiom = overloaded with tasks!

❌ (C) He is having many drawbacks
• "Drawbacks" = shortcomings, defects. Idiom = enterprises/activities — drawbacks-മായി യാതൊരു ബന്ധവുമില്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "Too many" + negative connotation = drawbacks feel — but correct meaning = enterprises overload!
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: "Blacksmith too many IRONS = cannot focus on ONE = too many activities!" — ഈ blacksmith image ഓർത്താൽ ഒരിക്കലും meaning മറക്കില്ല!
• Word family: too many irons in the fire | bite off more than you can chew | have a lot on one's plate — ഈ മൂന്ന് idioms ഒരേ theme (overloaded) — ഒരുമിച്ച് ഓർക്കൂ!
• PSC exam strategy: Idiom questions-ൽ literal meaning ആദ്യം eliminate ചെയ്യൂ — "irons/fire" literal meaning options out. "Enterprises at the same time" = correct figurative meaning. Always check figurative sense!

questionText:
He seemed affected by __diffidence__.
(Identify the antonym of the underlined word)

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (A) Confidence

• "Diffidence" = shyness, lack of self-confidence — "ആത്മവിശ്വാസക്കുറവ്, നാണക്കേട്" എന്ന് Malayalam meaning.
• Antonym = opposite meaning → "Confidence" = self-assurance, boldness = "ആത്മവിശ്വാസം."
• "He seemed affected by diffidence" = "ആത്മവിശ്വാസക്കുറവ് അദ്ദേഹത്തെ ബാധിച്ചതായി തോന്നി."
• Memory clue: "DIF-fidence" — "DIF-ficulty with conFIDENCE" — confidence-ന്റെ opposite!
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Diffidence = lack of confidence → Antonym = Confidence (remove the lack!)
• Word family:
  diffident (adjective) = shy, lacking confidence
  diffidently (adverb) = in a shy manner
  diffidence (noun) = shyness, lack of confidence
• Synonyms of diffidence: shyness | timidity | bashfulness | reticence | self-doubt
• Antonyms of diffidence: confidence | boldness | assertiveness | self-assurance
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Formal/literary vocabulary antonyms — PSC-ൽ regular feature. Diffidence-ഉം confidence-ഉം PSC pair!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. Her diffidence prevented her from speaking in public. (shyness)
  2. Confidence is the exact antonym of diffidence.
  3. The diffident student rarely raised her hand in class.
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (B) Valour
• "Valour" = bravery/courage in battle — "യുദ്ധധീരത."
• Diffidence = confidence issue, bravery/cowardice issue അല്ല. Confidence ≠ Valour (close, but different dimension).
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Diffident person lacks courage feel — valour = brave = antonym feel. But diffidence specifically = confidence, not battlefield courage!

❌ (C) Cleverness
• "Cleverness" = intelligence, smartness — "ബുദ്ധി."
• Diffidence = confidence-നെ കുറിച്ചാണ്, intelligence-നെ കുറിച്ചല്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Shy person seems less clever feel — cleverness antonym feel ആകും. But diffidence = confidence dimension, not intelligence!

❌ (D) Seriousness
• "Seriousness" = being grave, formal, solemn — "ഗൗരവം."
• Diffidence-ഉം seriousness-ഉം entirely different dimensions — ഒന്നും ബന്ധമില്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Serious-ൽ shy feel ഉണ്ടെന്ന് തോന്നും — but antonym of diffidence = confidence, seriousness അല്ല!
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: "dif-FIDENCE — FIDENCE sounds like conFIDENCE! Dif = different/opposite → CONFIDENCE!" — word-ൽ തന്നെ answer ഉണ്ട്!
• Word family: diffident/diffidence (shy) ↔ confident/confidence (bold) — ഈ pair together ഓർക്കൂ!
• PSC exam strategy: "Dif-" prefix = difficulty/opposition sense → remove the negative → confidence. Prefix trick ഉപയോഗിച്ചാൽ antonym questions easy!

questionText: I _____ (attend) the class since 10:00 am.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (D) have been attending

• "Since 10:00 am" = ഒരു specific time point-ൽ തുടങ്ങി ഇപ്പോഴും continue ആകുന്ന action.
• ഇപ്പോഴും class-ൽ ഉണ്ട് — action still going on → Present Perfect Continuous tense.
• Formula: have/has + been + V-ing → "have been attending."
• "I have been attending the class since 10:00 am" = "ഞാൻ രാവിലെ 10 മുതൽ class-ൽ ഉണ്ട്" (ഇപ്പോഴും).
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Past-ൽ start ആയ, STILL CONTINUING action + since/for → Present Perfect Continuous
• Formula: have/has + been + V-ing
• Signal words:
  since + specific time (since 10:00 am, since Monday, since 2020) → Perfect Continuous
  for + duration (for two hours, for a long time, for years) → Perfect Continuous
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: "Since/for" + Present Perfect Continuous — PSC tenses-ൽ ഏറ്റവും repeat ആകുന്ന combination!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. I have been studying since morning. (since = specific time)
  2. She has been working here for five years. (for = duration)
  3. They have been waiting since 9 o'clock. (since = specific time)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) have attended
• "Have attended" = Present Perfect — completed action indicate ചെയ്യുന്നു.
• "Since 10:00 am" + still in class = action ongoing → Perfect Continuous വേണം, simple Perfect അല്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "Since" → Present Perfect feel → "have attended" choose ചെയ്ക്കും. But still ongoing = CONTINUOUS form!

❌ (B) am attending
• "Am attending" = Present Continuous — ഈ moment-ൽ നടക്കുന്നത് show ചെയ്യുന്നു, duration show ചെയ്യില്ല.
• "Since 10:00 am" = duration from a past time — Present Continuous-ന് ഇത് express ചെയ്യാൻ കഴിയില്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Still attending = present continuous feel. But "since" = duration from past = Perfect Continuous!

❌ (C) had attended
• "Had attended" = Past Perfect — മറ്റൊരു past action-ന് മുമ്പ് complete ആയ action-ന്.
• "Since 10:00 am" present context ആണ് — past perfect ഇവിടെ ചേരില്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Formal sound → "had attended" educated feel — but tense mismatch, action still going on!
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: "SINCE/FOR + STILL GOING = HAVE BEEN doing!" — action start ആയ time ഉണ്ട്, ഇപ്പോഴും continue = have been + V-ing!
• Word family: have been waiting | has been studying | have been working | has been living — ഒരേ pattern, different verbs!
• PSC exam strategy: "Since" blank-ൽ കണ്ടാൽ → action still happening-ഓ check ചെയ്യൂ. Yes = have/has been + V-ing. ഈ one rule ഓർത്താൽ since/for questions always correct!

questionText:
I went for a picnic to relieve the __tedium__ of everyday life.
(Choose the synonym of the underlined word)
